  combo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the combo meal offering. Controls whether the combo is available for sale across channels. \'test\' indicates a market-test combo not yet in full rollout.']
  combo_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the combo meal by bundle format. Drives menu engineering analysis, PMIX reporting, and promotional targeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: value_meal|family_pack|kids_meal|party_pack|snack_combo|premium_combo — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this combo meal is offered. Supports global menu management, regulatory compliance, and multi-currency pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the combo meal record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage in the Silver layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary fields on this combo meal record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global franchise markets.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Daypart(s) during which this combo meal is available for sale. Daypart segmentation is a core foodservice operational concept used in PMIX analysis, labor scheduling, and revenue management.']
  discontinue_date date [note: 'The date on which the combo meal was or is scheduled to be removed from the menu. Nullable for permanent menu fixtures. Used for menu lifecycle management and supply chain wind-down planning.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the current version of the combo meal record ceases to be operationally effective. Nullable for open-ended records. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the current version of the combo meal record (including pricing, components, and availability) is operationally effective. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.']
  food_cost_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold as a percentage of the bundle price (item_cost / bundle_price * 100). Core menu engineering KPI used to classify combo meals and drive pricing decisions. Aligns with CoGS% jargon in foodservice P&L management.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL of the primary product image for the combo meal used on digital menu boards, Olo OLO platform, and 3PD channel listings. Must be a publicly accessible HTTPS URL.']
  individual_items_price_sum decimal(10,2) [note: 'The sum of the base prices of all individual component menu items if purchased separately. Used to calculate the bundle discount amount and communicate value to guests. Sourced from Oracle MICROS POS item pricing.']
  is_3pd_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available through Third-Party Delivery (3PD) partners (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub). Controls 3PD channel menu syndication via Olo integration.']
  is_customizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests can substitute or modify component items within the combo meal (e.g., swap side item, upsize drink). Drives modifier group display logic in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo.']
  is_dine_in_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available for dine-in service. Relevant for casual and fine-dining formats where combo availability may differ by service mode.']
  is_dt_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available for sale through the Drive-Thru (DT) channel. Controls channel-specific menu display in Oracle MICROS POS and KDS routing.']
  is_national_launch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is part of a national rollout versus a regional or test-market offering. Drives marketing campaign scope, supply chain planning, and franchise communication.']
  is_olo_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available through the Olo Digital Ordering Platform (OLO) for web and mobile ordering. Controls digital menu availability.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the combo meal bundle price is subject to sales tax. Drives tax calculation logic in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA AR module. Tax applicability may vary by jurisdiction.']
  item_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the combo meal, representing the combined food cost of all component items. Used to calculate COGS% and support P&L and menu engineering decisions. Sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management and SAP S/4HANA CO module.']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date on which the combo meal became or is scheduled to become available for sale. Used for menu lifecycle management, supply chain lead time planning, and marketing campaign alignment.']
  menu_engineering_class varchar [note: 'Menu engineering quadrant classification for the combo meal based on profitability and popularity: \'star\' (high profit, high popularity), \'plow_horse\' (low profit, high popularity), \'puzzle\' (high profit, low popularity), \'dog\' (low profit, low popularity). Core menu engineering decision-support field.']
  olo_item_code varchar [note: 'Olo Digital Ordering Platform external identifier for this combo meal. Used for menu synchronization between the internal menu master and the Olo platform for web, mobile, and 3PD channel ordering.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available at franchise-operated locations, company-owned locations, or both. Supports franchise compliance tracking via FranConnect and royalty reporting.']
  pmix_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Product Mix (PMIX) percentage representing the expected share of total transactions this combo meal should represent within its category. Used for menu performance benchmarking and sales forecasting.']
  pos_item_code varchar [note: 'Oracle MICROS POS-specific item code used to ring up the combo meal at the point of sale. This is the PLU/button code used by FOH (Front of House) staff and KDS (Kitchen Display System) routing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where this combo meal is available (e.g., \'NORTHEAST\', \'WEST\', \'SOUTH\'). Supports regional menu variation, pricing strategy, and PMIX analysis across the restaurant network.']
  restaurant_format varchar [note: 'Restaurant format(s) for which this combo meal is applicable. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) combos differ structurally from casual or fine-dining bundles. Drives menu engineering and franchise compliance.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sequence order for the combo meal on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Lower values appear first. Controls guest-facing menu presentation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this combo meal record originated (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']
  tax_category_code varchar [note: 'Tax category code assigned to the combo meal for sales tax calculation in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA. Determines applicable tax rates by jurisdiction and product type.']
  total_calories varchar [note: 'Total calorie count for the combo meal as configured with default component items. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance on menus with 20+ locations. Displayed on menu boards and digital ordering platforms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the combo meal record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master record for bundled combo meal offerings — pre-defined combinations of menu items sold together at a bundle price (e.g., Value Meal, Family Pack, Kids Meal). Captures combo name, component items, bundle price, individual item price sum, bundle discount amount, applicable channels, daypart availability, and active status. Distinct from LTOs in that combos are permanent or semi-permanent menu fixtures.'
}

Table menu.item_86_event {
  item_86_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each 86 event record — when a menu item is temporarily pulled from service at a restaurant. Primary key of this table.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: When an item is 86\'d, the specific channel affected (OLO, drive-thru, dine-in) must be recorded for channel suppression actions and impact reporting. channel_affected is a denormalized plain column; r']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: item_86_event tracks estimated_lost_covers (lost revenue from stockouts). Linking to financial_period enables period-level lost sales reporting, a key foodservice operations finance metric for invento']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: 86-event root cause analysis: kitchen managers and supply chain teams must identify which specific ingredient caused an item outage for reorder triggering and root cause reporting. The existing reason']
  inspection_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.inspection_violation. Business justification: When a health inspector orders removal of a menu item due to a violation, the 86 event must be traceable to the triggering inspection_violation for compliance documentation and corrective action track']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.maintenance_work_order. Business justification: Equipment failure causing item unavailability (86 event) must be correlated with the maintenance work order raised to resolve it. QSR operations teams use this link for root cause analysis, SLA compli']
  menu_lto_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu.menu_lto. Business justification: An 86 event (item temporarily removed from service) is operationally critical when it affects a Limited Time Offer item. The item_86_event already has is_lto_item BOOLEAN flag indicating the item is a']
  primary_item_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific kitchen equipment unit that failed and caused the 86 event, when reason_code = \'equipment_failure\'. Supports Repairs and Maintenance (R&M) tracking and equipment reliability analysis.']
  primary_item_reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (Back of House (BOH) or Front of House (FOH) staff member) who first reported or logged the 86 event. Links to workforce employee master.']
  primary_menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the menu item that was 86\'d (removed from service). Links to the menu.menu_item master record.']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.recall_event. Business justification: FDA/USDA recall traceability: when is_recall_related=true on an 86 event, regulators and operations teams require a direct link to the specific recall_event for compliance reporting, affected-location']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Item 86 events occur during a specific shift. Linking to the active shift enables operational reporting on which shifts experience the most stockouts, supports labor-menu correlation analysis, and is ']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: An 86 event is fundamentally triggered by stock depletion of a specific stock_item. This FK enables inventory-driven 86 management, post-event root cause analysis, and automated reorder triggers. The ']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this 86 event record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  daypart_affected varchar [note: 'The daypart during which the 86 event was active or first occurred. Enables PMIX (Product Mix) and daypart-level impact analysis. Aligns with menu.daypart definitions.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from start_timestamp to end_timestamp. Populated upon resolution. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) impact analysis and operational KPI reporting.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the menu item was restored to service and the 86 event was closed. Null if the event is still open. Used to calculate total downtime duration.']
  escalated_to_team varchar [note: 'The internal team to which the 86 event was escalated, if applicable. Supports accountability tracking and cross-functional response coordination.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the 86 event was escalated to operations management, supply chain, or franchise support teams. Null if the event was resolved without escalation. Supports SLA and response time analysis.']
  estimated_lost_covers varchar [note: 'Estimated number of guest covers (transactions) that could not be fulfilled due to the 86 event during the affected period. Derived operationally at the restaurant level for guest experience impact reporting. Not a calculated KPI — captured as a manager estimate at time of resolution.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for the 86 event (e.g., 86-2024-07-15-A3F9K2). Used in operational communications, Zenput audit tasks, and guest experience escalations.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the 86 event. \'open\' = item currently unavailable; \'resolved\' = item restored to service; \'escalated\' = issue elevated to operations or supply chain team; \'cancelled\' = event logged in error.']
  guest_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a guest-facing notification was sent (e.g., via OLO or 3PD platform) to inform customers that the item is temporarily unavailable. True = notification dispatched. Supports Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) and Net Promoter Score (NPS) impact tracking.']
  haccp_ccp_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code to the specific HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) that was breached or triggered this 86 event, if applicable. Used for food safety audit trails and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  is_food_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a food safety concern (e.g., HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) failure, quality issue, or regulatory recall). True = food safety event requiring Zenput audit task and potential regulatory notification.']
  is_lto_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 86\'d item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO). True = LTO item. 86 events on LTO items carry heightened business impact due to marketing commitments and guest expectations, and may require immediate escalation.']
  is_recall_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a formal supplier or regulatory product recall. True = item must not be served and recall protocol must be followed per FDA/USDA guidelines.']
  kds_alert_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Kitchen Display System (KDS) alert was sent to BOH staff notifying them of the 86 event. True = KDS alert dispatched. Supports BOH operational awareness and throughput management.']
  olo_item_code varchar [note: 'The item identifier in the Olo Digital Ordering Platform at the time of the 86 event. Used to confirm whether the item was also suppressed on the OLO channel during the event.']
  olo_suppressed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item was suppressed on the Olo Digital Ordering Platform during the 86 event. True = item hidden from online ordering menu. Prevents guest orders for unavailable items on digital channels.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant where the 86 event occurred is a franchise location or a company-owned location. Drives escalation routing and compliance accountability.']
  pos_item_code varchar [note: 'The Point of Sale (POS) item code as configured in Oracle MICROS POS at the time of the 86 event. Captured at event time to preserve the POS state even if the menu item code changes later.']
  pos_suppressed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item was actively suppressed (hidden/disabled) on the Oracle MICROS POS system during the 86 event. True = item removed from POS ordering screens. Ensures operational accuracy and prevents staff from ringing up unavailable items.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the menu item was removed from service. \'out_of_stock\' = inventory depleted; \'equipment_failure\' = kitchen equipment down; \'quality_issue\' = item failed quality/HACCP check; \'recall\' = supplier or regulatory recall; \'supplier_delay\' = ingredient not delivered; \'prep_time_exceeded\' = throughput constraint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_stock|equipment_failure|quality_issue|recall|supplier_delay|prep_time_exceeded — promote to reference product]']
  reason_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context beyond the reason_code. For example, specifying which piece of equipment failed, which ingredient was out of stock, or the nature of the quality issue. Supports root cause analysis.']
  reported_by_role varchar [note: 'Job role of the employee who reported the 86 event (e.g., manager, Back of House (BOH) staff, Front of House (FOH) staff, shift lead, franchisee). Supports accountability and training gap analysis.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'The corrective action taken to resolve the 86 event and restore the item to service. \'restocked\' = inventory replenished; \'equipment_repaired\' = kitchen equipment fixed; \'item_substituted\' = alternative item offered; \'recalled_item_removed\' = item permanently pulled per recall; \'supplier_order_placed\' = emergency procurement initiated; \'no_action\' = event closed without resolution (e.g., end of daypart). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: restocked|equipment_repaired|item_substituted|recalled_item_removed|supplier_order_placed|no_action — promote to reference product]']
  restaurant_format varchar [note: 'The restaurant format (Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, or fine-dining) of the location where the 86 event occurred. Supports format-level operational benchmarking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this 86 event was originated or captured. \'micros_pos\' = Oracle MICROS POS; \'zenput\' = Zenput operational compliance; \'marketman\' = MarketMan inventory alert; \'olo\' = Olo platform suppression event; \'manual\' = manually entered by operations staff.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the menu item was first removed from service (86\'d). This is the principal real-world event timestamp. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this 86 event record was last modified in the data platform, such as when the event was resolved, escalated, or corrected. Supports data lineage and change tracking in the Silver layer.']
  zenput_task_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the Zenput food safety audit task or operational compliance task created in response to this 86 event. Enables traceability between the 86 event and the corrective action workflow in Zenput.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing an \'86\' event — when a menu item is temporarily removed from service at a specific restaurant due to stockout, quality issue, or equipment failure. Captures the item, restaurant, channel affected, start timestamp, end timestamp, reason code (out-of-stock/equipment/quality/recall), reported-by, and resolution action. Critical for operational compliance and guest experience tracking.'
}

Table menu.item_assignment {
  item_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the menu_item_assignment association']
  menu_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the published menu record that this assignment belongs to']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the menu item being assigned to the menu']
  channel_availability varchar [note: 'Channel-specific availability override for this item on this menu, expressed as a pipe-delimited list of enabled channels (e.g., \'DT|OLO\'). Overrides the item-level channel flags when a menu engineer restricts or expands availability for a specific menu context. Belongs to the assignment because channel restrictions may differ per menu.']
  display_category varchar [note: 'The category label under which this item is grouped on this specific menu (e.g., \'Burgers\', \'Sides\', \'Beverages\'). Belongs to the assignment because the same item may be categorized differently across menus (e.g., a chicken sandwich may appear under \'Sandwiches\' on one menu and \'Value Meals\' on another).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this item is retired and no longer available on this specific menu. Enables item sunsets and LTO expirations within a menu version. Belongs to the assignment because the same item may be retired from one menu while remaining active on others.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this item becomes active and available for ordering on this specific menu. Enables phased item introductions and LTO launches within a menu version without requiring a full menu republish. Belongs to the assignment because the same item may have different activation dates on different menus.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is promoted or highlighted on this specific menu (e.g., shown in a featured section, displayed with a badge, or given visual prominence on digital menu boards). Belongs to the assignment because featured status is a menu-context decision, not an intrinsic item property.']
  price_override decimal(10,4) [note: 'Menu-specific selling price for this item that overrides the item\'s base_price on menu_item. Used for channel-specific pricing (e.g., 3PD surcharge menus), franchise-specific pricing, or LTO promotional pricing. Belongs to the assignment because the same item may be priced differently across menus. Null indicates no override — base_price applies.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'The display sequence position of this menu item within its display category on this menu. Controls the order in which items are rendered on digital menu boards, OLO interfaces, and POS screens. Belongs to the assignment because the same item may appear in different positions on different menus.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment between menu and menu_item. It captures the formal menu engineering decision to include a specific menu item on a specific published menu, along with all context that governs how that item appears and behaves within that menu. Each record links one menu to one menu_item and carries attributes — sort order, display category, featured flag, effective dates, channel availability override, and price override — that exist only in the context of this specific menu-item pairing. This is the configuration record that POS systems consume to render a menu.. Existence Justification: In foodservice operations, menu engineers explicitly assign specific menu items to specific menus — a menu is a curated collection of items, and the same item (e.g., \'Classic Cheeseburger\') can appear on multiple menus (breakfast, lunch, drive-thru, OLO, franchise, LTO) while each menu contains many items. This assignment is actively managed: items are added, removed, reordered, featured, and price-overridden per menu context. The relationship is not derivable from transactional data — it is a master-data configuration decision made by menu engineers before any orders are placed.'
}

Table menu.combo_meal_component {
  combo_meal_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the combo_meal_component association']
  combo_meal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this component record to its parent combo meal definition.']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this component record to the specific menu item that serves as a component in the combo.']
  calorie_contribution varchar [note: 'The caloric value (kcal) attributed to this specific component within the combo meal, used to compute the combo\'s total and range calorie display. Belongs to the pairing because the same item may contribute different calories in different combo contexts (e.g., different portion sizes).']
  component_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this menu item within the combo meal\'s component list. Drives display order on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Belongs to the pairing, not to either entity alone.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Functional role of this menu item within the combo meal bundle (e.g., ENTREE, SIDE, BEVERAGE). The same menu item may play different roles in different combos. Belongs to the pairing.']
  cost_contribution decimal [note: 'The standard cost of goods (COGS) allocated to this component within the combo meal bundle, used for food cost and margin analysis at the combo level. Belongs to the pairing, not to the item or combo alone.']
  is_combo_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this menu item can be included as a component in a combo meal offering. Used by menu engineering teams to configure combo meal bundles and upsell prompts in POS and OLO. [Moved from menu_item: This boolean flag on menu_item indicates whether an item CAN participate in a combo, but the actual participation — which combo, in what role, in what quantity — is now captured in combo_meal_component. The eligibility flag is a derived or redundant signal once the association table exists. It may be retained on menu_item as a denormalized convenience flag for filtering, but architecturally the ground truth of combo participation lives in the association. Teams should evaluate whether to deprecate it or keep it as a computed/maintained flag.]']
  is_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is mandatory in the combo meal. If FALSE, the guest may omit this item without invalidating the combo. Belongs to the pairing.']
  is_substitutable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest may substitute this component with an eligible alternative menu item. Drives substitution logic in POS and OLO systems. Belongs to the pairing.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of this menu item included in the combo meal at the standard bundle price. For example, a Family Pack may include 4 units of a burger. Belongs to the pairing, not to the item or combo alone.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Component Role between combo_meal and menu_item. It captures the formal definition of which menu items constitute a given combo meal, in what quantity, in what sequence, and under what substitution rules. Each record links one combo_meal to one menu_item and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that specific pairing — such as whether the component is required, whether it can be substituted, its position in the bundle, and its caloric and cost contribution to the combo total. This is the operational source of truth for combo meal composition used by menu engineering, POS configuration, and digital ordering systems.. Existence Justification: In QSR and casual dining, a combo meal is explicitly defined as a bundle of specific menu items — a Value Meal #1 contains a burger, fries, and a drink, while a Family Pack contains different items. One combo meal contains multiple menu items (components), and one menu item (e.g., a medium fries) participates in multiple combo meals simultaneously. This is an actively managed, operational business concept: menu engineers define, update, and retire combo compositions as part of menu management workflows.'
}

Table guest.profile {
  profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the guest profile record.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee‑level loyalty aggregation report needs each guest linked to the franchisee that owns their primary restaurant.']
  location_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location the guest most frequently visits.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program membership associated with the guest.']
  profile_preferred_store_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific store the guest prefers.']
  profile_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific store the guest prefers.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Assigning guest to a profit_center enables revenue attribution in multi‑brand restaurant profit reporting.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.guest_guest_segment. Business justification: guest_segment_membership must reference the specific segment definition within the guest domain.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the guest\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the guest\'s street address (apartment, suite, etc.).']
  average_check_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per transaction for the guest.']
  birthday_day varchar [note: 'Day of month (1‑31) of the guest\'s birth date.']
  birthday_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) of the guest\'s birth date, used for birthday promotions.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the guest\'s mailing address.']
  consent_email boolean [note: 'Guest\'s consent to receive marketing emails.']
  consent_privacy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has consented to privacy policy and data processing.']
  consent_sms boolean [note: 'Guest\'s consent to receive marketing SMS messages.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the guest\'s residence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the guest data.']
  data_source_code varchar [note: 'Original identifier of the guest in the source system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Guest\'s birth date for age verification and personalization.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email used for electronic communication and marketing.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the guest.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the guest as stored in the master record.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑declared gender of the guest for demographic analysis.']
  guest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the guest based on relationship to the business.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the guest.']
  last_visit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent guest interaction.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level of the guest within the loyalty program.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Overall opt‑in flag for marketing communications across all channels.']
  marketing_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the guest was first acquired.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Unstructured notes entered by staff about the guest.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for SMS, voice contact, and verification.']
  picture_url varchar [note: 'Link to the guest\'s profile image stored in the digital asset system.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the guest\'s mailing address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Language the guest prefers for communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: en|es|fr|de|zh|ja|pt — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Channel the guest most frequently uses for contact.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the guest profile.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the profile record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the profile.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the guest\'s mailing address.']
  total_lifetime_visits varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all visits (in‑store, drive‑thru, online) made by the guest.']
  total_spent decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount the guest has spent across all transactions.']

  Note: 'Master record for every guest identity across all service channels (dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, 3PD). Single source of truth for WHO the business serves — captures full identity and demographic attributes including name, contact details, date of birth, language preference, age band, gender identity, household income band, education level, employment status, geographic market classification, demographic data source (self-declared vs. third-party enrichment), enrichment provider and date. Also owns digital account attributes: username, account status (active/suspended/deactivated), registration date, registration channel, last login timestamp, device type, app version, two-factor authentication status, and account tier. Sourced primarily from Salesforce CRM, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, and brand mobile app. This is the anchor entity for the entire guest domain.'
}

Table guest.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the address record.']
  address_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this address belongs.']
  address_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this address belongs.']
  owner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the owning entity (guest, restaurant, etc.).']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose.']
  building_name varchar [note: 'Name of the building, if applicable.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code.']
  county varchar [note: 'County or equivalent administrative area.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the address record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for delivering to this address (e.g., gate code, porch).']
  district varchar [note: 'Sub‑municipal district or neighborhood.']
  geocode_accuracy varchar [note: 'Quality level of the geocoding result.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the guest\'s primary address.']
  landmark varchar [note: 'Nearby landmark or point of reference to aid delivery.']
  last_verified date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful verification.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the address.']
  line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line (e.g., house number and street name).']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as apartment, suite, or unit.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the address.']
  owner_type varchar [note: 'Entity type that owns or uses the address.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region (e.g., Midwest, West Coast).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the address (e.g., OLO, POS, CRM).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the address.']
  suite_number varchar [note: 'Suite, unit, or floor number within a building.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the address location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent date and time the address record was modified.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent address validation attempt.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the address was last validated.']
  validity_flag boolean [note: 'True if the address is currently considered valid for delivery.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the address.']
  verification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0‑100) from the verification service.']

  Note: 'Stores all physical and delivery addresses associated with a guest profile, including home address, saved delivery addresses, and billing addresses. Captures address type, street, city, state/province, postal code, country, geolocation coordinates, delivery instructions, and validation status. Supports OLO delivery fulfillment and personalized marketing by geography.'
}

Table guest.preference {
  preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for preference record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit standard master or transaction roles; treated as OTHER.']

Business justification: Cross-referencing a guest\'s allergen profile against the active allergen declaration powers safe-ordering recommendations at POS/OLO and satisfies FDA menu labeling compliance. Foodservice allergen ma']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: Required for allergy compliance: linking each guest\'s allergen profile to the ingredient master enables order screening and regulatory reporting of allergen exposure.']

  preference_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this preference belongs.']

Business justification: Real-time allergen-safe item filtering at POS and OLO requires linking a guest\'s allergen profile directly to unsafe menu items. This enables \'items to avoid\' warnings during ordering — a named food s']
  preferred_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Multi-brand operators (RBI, Yum! Brands) use brand-level preference for cross-brand marketing suppression and brand-specific loyalty segmentation. A guest may prefer Brand A over Brand B; this drives ']
  preferred_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Location-based personalization engine uses a guest\'s preferred unit to deliver unit-specific offers, push notifications, and menu recommendations. Distinct from profile.preferred_store_unit_id — prefe']

  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Allergy‑management process needs to map each guest\'s allergen profile to specific stock items containing the allergen for safe‑menu recommendations.']
  communication_channel_preference varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred channel for receiving communications.']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has given consent for storing this preference.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when consent was recorded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the preference record was first created.']
  data_source_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the source system recorded the preference.']

  favorite_cuisine varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred cuisine type (e.g., Italian, Mexican).']
  has_dairy_allergy boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest has a dairy allergy.']
  has_gluten_allergy boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest has a gluten allergy.']
  has_nut_allergy boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest has a nut allergy.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the preference is currently active.']
  is_halal boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest requires halal meals.']
  is_kosher boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest requires kosher meals.']
  is_vegan boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest prefers vegan meals.']
  is_vegetarian boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest prefers vegetarian meals.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred language for communications.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest has opted in to receive marketing communications.']
  marketing_opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the guest for opting out of marketing communications.']

  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the preference record.']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Category of the preference indicating its domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dietary|cuisine|menu_item|service_channel|daypart|communication|marketing|loyalty|other — promote to reference product]']
  preference_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual value or description of the preference, such as "no peanuts" or "Italian".']
  preferred_daypart varchar [note: 'Time of day the guest most often dines.']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'Guest\'s favored payment method.']
  preferred_seating varchar [note: 'Guest\'s favored seating location within the restaurant.']
  preferred_service_channel varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred way to receive service.']
  privacy_consent_version varchar [note: 'Version of the privacy consent agreement under which the preference was collected.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that supplied the preference data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the preference record.']

  Note: 'Captures all guest-stated and inferred preferences, dietary restrictions, and food allergen declarations. Covers FDA major allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame) with severity levels (intolerance vs. allergy), dietary restriction types (vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free), declaration source (self-declared, healthcare provider), cuisine preferences, favorite menu items, preferred service channel (dine-in, DT, OLO), preferred daypart, communication channel preferences (email, SMS, push), and marketing opt-in/opt-out flags. Sourced from Salesforce CRM, Olo guest data, and guest self-declaration. Drives personalization, targeted marketing, and HACCP-aligned guest food safety compliance including FDA allergen labeling requirements.'
}

Table guest.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the consent record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA compliance requires brand-scoped consent records in multi-brand operators — consent for Brand A\'s marketing does not extend to Brand B. Regulatory audits and data subject access requests req']
  primary_consent_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this consent applies.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA compliance requires consent records to be scoped to the specific loyalty program under which consent was obtained. Program-scoped consent management enables accurate consent audit trails, pr']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the consent automatically expires, if applicable.']
  consent_language varchar [note: 'Two‑letter language code of the consent notice presented to the guest.']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the guest expressed consent (opt‑in, opt‑out, implied).']
  consent_purpose varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the business purpose for which consent was obtained.']
  consent_revoked_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason provided by the guest for withdrawing consent, if any.']
  consent_revoked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the guest withdrew the consent.']
  consent_source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the guest provided consent.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consent.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the consent was originally given.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent (e.g., marketing, SMS, email, data sharing, profiling).']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy or consent notice at the time of consent.']
  created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first created in the system.']
  data_processing_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of data processing covered by the consent.']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to internal data sharing for analytics or personalization.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to capture consent (e.g., mobile device ID).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the consent is considered active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the consent remains valid (null if open‑ended).']
  email_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to receive email communications.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the consent was captured.']
  marketing_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to receive marketing communications.']
  privacy_notice_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the privacy notice version referenced by this consent.']
  sms_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to receive SMS messages.']
  third_party_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to share data with approved third parties.']
  updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consent record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of guest consent and privacy elections per regulatory requirement (GDPR, CCPA, CAN-SPAM). Tracks consent type (marketing email, SMS, data sharing, profiling), consent status (granted/withdrawn), consent timestamp, consent source channel, consent version/policy version, and expiry date. Mandatory for compliance with FDA labeling, FTC advertising regulations, and applicable data privacy laws. Immutable audit trail of all consent changes.'
}

  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: In multi-brand operators, guest segments are brand-specific (e.g., \'Lapsed Burger Fan\' belongs to a specific brand). Segment activation, suppression logic, and brand-level audience sizing all require ']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Guest segments in restaurant CRM are defined within loyalty program scope (e.g., \'lapsed Gold members in Program X\'). A program FK on guest_segment enables program-specific segment targeting for loyal']

  Note: 'Defines guest segmentation classifications used for targeted marketing, personalization, and operational planning. Captures segment name, segment type (behavioral, demographic, value-based, lifecycle stage), definition criteria, effective date range, segment owner (marketing vs. operations), and channel applicability. Examples include high-frequency QSR visitors, lapsed guests, LTO responders, and high-ACV guests. Managed in Salesforce CRM.'
}

  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: LTV calculations in restaurant CRM are program-scoped — predicted_future_value, total_historical_spend, and ltv_tier are meaningful only within a specific loyalty program context. Multi-program operat']
  average_check_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monetary value of a single transaction for the guest.']
  average_transactions_per_month decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean count of transactions the guest completes each month.']
  consent_opt_in boolean [note: 'True if the guest has opted in to data collection and marketing communications.']

  days_since_last_visit varchar [note: 'Number of days between today and the most recent visit date.']
  first_visit_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the guest\'s inaugural purchase.']
  loyalty_member_flag boolean [note: 'True if the guest is enrolled in the restaurant\'s loyalty program.']
  ltv_calculation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the LTV metrics were most recently refreshed.']
  ltv_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the LTV record was last refreshed.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the LTV record.']

  Note: 'Stores calculated and periodically refreshed guest lifetime value (LTV) metrics at the individual profile level. Captures total historical spend, visit frequency, average check value (ACV), average transaction count (ATC), predicted future value, LTV tier classification, first visit date, most recent visit date, and days since last visit. Refreshed on a scheduled cadence from order domain transactional data. Supports retention prioritization and loyalty tier management.'
}

Linking guest_visit.digital_account_id to digital_account.digital_account_id en']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.guest_order. Business justification: Every restaurant visit is anchored to a transaction. Linking guest_visit to guest_order enables visit-level SOS reporting, loyalty point attribution, and dine-in analytics. A foodservice domain expert']
  host_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Guest Flow Management records which host/hostess greeted the guest, essential for staffing analysis and guest experience metrics.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Guest visits are the primary loyalty points-earning events. Linking guest_visit directly to loyalty.member supports visit-based accrual attribution, visit count tracking for tier qualification, and vi']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.pos_terminal. Audit and finance teams use this daily for chargeb']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: guest_visit must reference the guest profile to associate each visit with a guest.']

  Note: 'Captures each confirmed guest visit to a restaurant unit across all service modes (dine-in, DT, OLO, 3PD). Distinct from the order domain\'s order transaction — this is the guest-centric visit record that may span multiple orders or be a zero-spend visit (e.g., complaint resolution visit). Captures visit date, daypart, service mode, restaurant unit, party size (cover count), table number (dine-in), DT lane (drive-thru), visit duration, and whether the visit was incentivized by a promotion.'
}

  Note: 'Represents a guest\'s registered digital account on the brand\'s owned digital channels (mobile app, website, OLO platform). Distinct from the guest profile (identity) — this is the digital access credential and session management record sourced from Olo Digital Ordering Platform.'
}

Table order.guest_order {
  guest_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each guest order record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the guest_order data product.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_channel. Business justification: guest_order currently stores channel as a string; linking to order_channel lookup provides referential integrity and removes redundant column.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Cost Center expense allocation per order, enabling finance\'s Cost Center reporting and profitability analysis.']
  coupon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.coupon. Business justification: Needed for coupon redemption audit: linking an order to the coupon entity allows accurate discount accounting and fraud detection.']
  delivery_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery_platform. Business justification: guest_order.delivery_provider is a denormalized STRING storing the third-party delivery platform name (e.g., \'DoorDash\', \'Uber Eats\'). Normalizing to delivery_platform_id FK referencing order.delivery']
  digital_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.digital_account. Business justification: Digital orders placed via app or web are associated with a specific digital account. Linking guest_order to digital_account enables digital channel analytics, app engagement reporting, and loyalty int']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Same-store sales (SSS) reporting and period-close require orders to be assigned to fiscal periods. Restaurant finance teams aggregate daily order revenue by financial period for SSS comparisons, budge']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Required for franchisee royalty reporting and performance dashboards that aggregate orders by the owning franchisee; each order must be tied to the franchisee operating the restaurant.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Needed for loyalty points accrual and member order history report linking each order to the member who placed it.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program associated with this order, if the guest is enrolled. Used to link loyalty point accrual and redemption events to the order transaction.']
  menu_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: Required for Menu Performance Reporting: each order must be linked to the specific menu version presented to the guest to analyze sales per menu and ensure compliance with menu engineering.']
  primary_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest (customer) who placed the order. Nullable for anonymous walk-in transactions where no loyalty or digital profile is associated. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction header.']
  primary_guest_segment_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_guest_segment. Business justification: Supports segment‑based sales analysis: orders are associated with the guest segment targeted by marketing campaigns for segmentation reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Order-level profit center assignment is required for daily sales reporting by profit center without item-level aggregation. Restaurant P&L reporting by profit center (unit/brand segment) is a core man']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing promotion, coupon, or Limited Time Offer (LTO) applied to this order. Null if no promotion was applied. Used for promotional campaign effectiveness and discount attribution analysis.']
  server_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Front of House (FOH) server or cashier employee who took or managed the order. Used for labor performance tracking, tip allocation, and service quality analysis.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed or fulfilled. Links to the restaurant master record.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. Business justification: Needed for territory‑level sales and compliance reporting; orders are aggregated by the franchise territory that the restaurant belongs to.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed or fulfilled. Links to the restaurant master record.']
  cancelled_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was cancelled or voided. Null for orders that were fulfilled. Used for cancellation rate reporting and operational analysis.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guest_order record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this transaction header. Distinct from placed_at which captures the business event time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the monetary amounts in this order (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Required for multi-currency international restaurant operations. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET currency component.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Operational daypart segment during which the order was placed. Dayparts are defined by the restaurant\'s operating schedule and used for Product Mix (PMIX) analysis, labor scheduling, and sales reporting. Standard segments: breakfast, lunch, afternoon, dinner, late_night.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for delivery orders. Contains personally identifiable location information for the guest. Null for non-delivery order types.']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the delivery address. Used for delivery zone analysis, geographic sales reporting, and logistics optimization. Contains guest location data classified as PII.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of all discounts, promotions, coupons, and loyalty redemptions applied to the order. Represents the adjustment component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Used for promotional effectiveness and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) analysis.']
  fulfilled_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was handed to the guest or dispatched for delivery, marking the completion of the service transaction. Used for end-to-end Speed of Service (SOS) measurement.']
  is_lto boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order contains at least one Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu item. Used for LTO performance tracking, promotional sales attribution, and menu engineering analysis.']
  is_voided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order was voided by a manager after tender. Voided orders are retained for audit and loss prevention purposes but excluded from revenue reporting. Distinct from cancelled orders (cancelled before preparation).']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual menu items (including modifiers and add-ons) included in the order. Used for throughput analysis, kitchen capacity planning, and Average Transaction Count (ATC) benchmarking.']
  kds_routed_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was routed to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for preparation. Used to calculate kitchen queue time and Back of House (BOH) throughput metrics.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points earned by the guest for this order transaction. Used for loyalty program accrual tracking and guest engagement analytics.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points redeemed by the guest as part of this order transaction. Used for loyalty liability accounting and promotional cost analysis.']
  olo_order_ref varchar [note: 'External order reference number assigned by the Olo Digital Ordering Platform for orders placed through digital channels (web, mobile app). Null for in-store POS orders.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the guest order. Tracks the order from placement through fulfillment or cancellation. \'placed\' = order received; \'in_preparation\' = KDS routing active; \'ready\' = food ready for pickup/delivery; \'fulfilled\' = order handed to guest; \'cancelled\' = cancelled before preparation; \'voided\' = voided post-tender by manager.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Fulfillment type of the order indicating how the guest intends to receive their food. Distinct from order_channel (which captures the ordering interface). Used for kitchen routing, packaging, and service time analytics.']
  party_size varchar [note: 'Number of guests in the dining party associated with this order. Applicable for dine-in orders. Used for cover count reporting, table turn analysis, and per-cover revenue calculations.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment transaction associated with this order. Distinct from order_status which tracks fulfillment lifecycle. Used for financial reconciliation and exception management.']
  placed_at timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp when the guest order was placed and confirmed in the POS or digital ordering system. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this transaction header. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) calculations and daypart assignment.']
  pos_transaction_ref varchar [note: 'Native transaction reference or check number generated by the Oracle MICROS POS system. Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the POS system of record.']
  ready_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was marked ready for pickup or delivery by kitchen staff. Used to calculate ticket time (time from order placement to food ready) and Speed of Service (SOS) metrics.']
  sos_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from order placement (placed_at) to order fulfillment (fulfilled_at). Represents the end-to-end guest experience time. A primary operational KPI for Quick-Service Restaurants (QSR) and Drive-Thru (DT) performance benchmarking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system of record from which this order record was ingested into the lakehouse. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-channel integration auditing.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing guest-specific preparation instructions, allergy notes, or customization requests entered at the time of ordering. Used by kitchen staff for order fulfillment and food safety compliance (HACCP allergen awareness).']
  subtotal_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Gross pre-tax, pre-discount monetary value of all items in the order. Represents the base order value before adjustments. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction header.']
  table_number varchar [note: 'Table or seat identifier assigned to the dine-in order within the restaurant floor plan. Null for takeout, drive-thru, and delivery orders. Used for table turn analysis and Front of House (FOH) service management.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total sales tax amount applied to the order based on applicable local, state, and federal tax rates. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment. Used for tax remittance reporting and financial reconciliation.']
  tender_type varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument used to settle the order. Supports payment mix analysis and PCI DSS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|credit_card|debit_card|mobile_pay|gift_card|loyalty_points|voucher|comp — promote to reference product]']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally visible order ticket number assigned by the Point of Sale (POS) system (Oracle MICROS). Printed on receipts and displayed on the Kitchen Display System (KDS). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction. Format varies by restaurant configuration (e.g., sequential daily number).']
  ticket_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds from order placement (placed_at) to order ready (ready_at). Represents the kitchen preparation time, a key Speed of Service (SOS) and throughput KPI in restaurant operations. Stored as a raw operational field captured by the POS/KDS system.']
  tip_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Gratuity amount added by the guest at the time of payment. Applicable for casual and fine-dining service models. Used for labor compensation reporting and financial reconciliation.']
  total_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Final net monetary amount charged to the guest after applying discounts and adding taxes (subtotal_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount). Represents the Average Check Value (ACV) for this transaction. Net total component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guest_order record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this transaction header. Used for incremental data processing and change data capture (CDC) pipelines.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for why the order was voided. Null for non-voided orders. Used for loss prevention, operational exception reporting, and manager accountability tracking.']

  Note: 'Core transactional header record for every guest order placed across all service channels (POS/MICROS, drive-thru, OLO, 3PD, catering, kiosk). Captures order-level attributes: ticket number, order type, service channel, daypart, order status, guest count, total amount, tax total, discount total, timestamps (placed, promised, completed), and source system reference. This is the authoritative SSOT for all order transactions — every order_item, order_payment, order_status_event, and kds_ticket FKs back to this record.'
}

Table order.order_item {
  order_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each line item within a guest order. Primary key for the order_item entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  allergen_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu.allergen_declaration. Business justification: Food safety liability and allergen regulatory compliance require point-in-time traceability of which allergen declaration was active when an order_item was placed. Declarations change over time; snaps']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: order_item.service_channel is a denormalized STRING storing the channel for each line item. Normalizing to channel_id FK referencing order.channel.channel_id enforces referential integrity and enables']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.department. Business justification: In full-service and casual-dining restaurants, line items are routed to specific departments (bar, kitchen, pastry) for KDS routing, departmental P&L reporting, and labor allocation. A foodservice dom']
  discount_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional discount, coupon, or loyalty reward applied to this line item. Null when no discount is applied. Links to the marketing domain for promotional effectiveness analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each order item maps to a GL revenue account by product category (food, beverage, alcohol, merchandise). Restaurant revenue recognition and product-mix P&L reporting require item-level GL posting to d']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent guest order (TRANSACTION_HEADER) to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header/detail relationship for order fulfillment and revenue reporting.']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the menu item ordered, linking to the menu domain for Product Mix (PMIX) analysis, recipe costing, and allergen reporting.']
  menu_lto_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu.lto. Business justification: Needed for Limited‑Time Offer (LTO) effectiveness tracking: linking each order line to its LTO campaign enables calculation of lift, cost, and ROI for promotional items.']
  menu_modifier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the modifier applied to this line item (e.g., extra cheese, no onions, upsize). Nullable when no modifier is applied. Supports modifier-level PMIX and upsell analysis.']
  nutrition_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu.nutrition_profile. Business justification: US ACA menu labeling law (FDA 21 CFR Part 101) and similar regulations require point-of-sale calorie disclosure. Capturing the active nutrition_profile at order time enables regulatory compliance audi']
  prep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Labor costing & food‑safety audit need to know which employee prepared each menu item; supports per‑item labor allocation and accountability.']
  primary_order_combo_combo_meal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the combo meal or bundle to which this line item belongs. Null when is_combo_component is False. Groups combo components for bundle-level revenue and PMIX reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Assigns each sold menu item to a profit center for granular P&L analysis per restaurant segment.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu.recipe. Business justification: Food safety traceability and HACCP compliance require knowing which recipe version was used when an order_item was prepared. Enables food cost variance analysis (theoretical vs actual) and regulatory ']
  reward_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.reward. Business justification: Item-level reward attribution: when a loyalty reward (free item, BOGO) is applied to a specific line item, order_item must reference the reward for COGS attribution, reward redemption reporting, and m']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Product mix (PMIX) reporting, food cost analysis, and waste tracking are performed at the unit level against individual line items. A foodservice operator expects order_item to carry unit_id for unit-']
  allergen_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manager or authorized staff member overrode an allergen warning for this line item at the Point of Sale (POS). True triggers an audit trail for food safety compliance and liability management.']
  calorie_count varchar [note: 'Total calorie count for this line item as required by FDA menu labeling regulations. Reflects the calorie value at the ordered quantity and modifier combination. Supports regulatory compliance and guest nutrition transparency.']
  cost decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard cost of goods for this line item at the time of the transaction, sourced from the recipe/Bill of Materials (BOM). Used to calculate Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (COGS%) and gross margin at the item level.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order item record was first captured in the source system (Oracle MICROS POS or Olo). Represents the moment the line item was added to the order. Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this line item (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global restaurant markets.']
  daypart_code varchar [note: 'Operational daypart during which this line item was ordered (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night). Supports daypart-level PMIX analysis, labor scheduling alignment, and revenue management by time segment.']
  is_combo_component boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a component of a combo meal or bundle (True) or a standalone item (False). Supports combo meal PMIX analysis and bundle pricing validation.']
  is_lto boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ordered menu item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO) at the time of the transaction. Supports LTO performance tracking, PMIX contribution analysis, and promotional campaign reporting.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment lifecycle status of this line item as tracked through the Kitchen Display System (KDS) and Point of Sale (POS). Drives kitchen throughput reporting and Speed of Service (SOS) measurement.']
  kds_bump_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the kitchen staff bumped (completed) this line item on the Kitchen Display System (KDS), indicating preparation is complete. Core metric for ticket time and kitchen throughput reporting.']
  kds_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this line item was transmitted to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) station for preparation. Used to calculate ticket time and Speed of Service (SOS) from order placement to kitchen receipt.']
  line_discount_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total discount applied at the line-item level, including promotional discounts, coupon redemptions, loyalty rewards, and employee meal discounts. Supports promotional effectiveness and margin analysis.']
  line_gross_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Gross extended amount for this line item before discounts and taxes, calculated as quantity × (unit_price + modifier_price). Used as the basis for COGS% and revenue reporting.']
  line_net_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Net revenue amount for this line item after all line-level discounts, before tax. Equals line_gross_amount minus line_discount_amount. Core metric for Average Check Value (ACV) and P&L reporting.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this line item within the parent order, starting at 1. Used to preserve the order of items as entered at the Point of Sale (POS) and for receipt printing.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points earned by the guest for this line item, based on the loyalty program rules in effect at the time of the transaction. Supports loyalty program accrual tracking and CRM analytics.']
  modifier_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'Incremental price charged for the applied modifier (e.g., upsize surcharge, premium add-on). Zero or null when no modifier price applies. Contributes to line-level revenue and Average Check Value (ACV) analysis.']
  pmix_category varchar [note: 'Menu engineering classification of this item within the Product Mix (PMIX) framework (e.g., Star, Plow Horse, Puzzle, Dog per the BCG matrix). Supports menu engineering decisions and item lifecycle management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: star|plow_horse|puzzle|dog — promote to reference product]']
  preparation_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text special preparation instructions entered by the guest or cashier for this line item (e.g., \'well done\', \'no salt\', \'extra crispy\'). Transmitted to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for Back of House (BOH) execution.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric promotional or coupon code entered at the Point of Sale (POS) or Online Ordering (OLO) platform that triggered the line-level discount. Supports campaign attribution and redemption tracking.']
  quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Number of units of the menu item ordered on this line. Supports decimal precision for weight-based or fractional items (e.g., bulk catering). Used in PMIX analysis and COGS% calculation.']
  refund_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'Monetary amount refunded for this line item when refund_flag is True. May be a partial or full refund of line_net_amount. Used for financial reconciliation and guest recovery reporting.']
  refund_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item has been refunded (True). Supports refund rate tracking, guest satisfaction (CSAT) analysis, and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  source_system_item_ref varchar [note: 'Native item identifier from the originating Point of Sale (POS) or Online Ordering (OLO) system (e.g., Oracle MICROS detail line ID, Olo order item UUID). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer.']
  tax_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'Tax amount assessed on this line item based on applicable jurisdiction tax rules and menu item tax category. Supports tax remittance reporting and compliance with local tax authorities.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is exempt from sales tax (True), such as for qualifying food items under state/local tax law or institutional catering orders. Supports tax compliance and remittance reporting.']
  ticket_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds from kds_sent_timestamp to kds_bump_timestamp, representing the preparation time for this line item. Key Speed of Service (SOS) metric for kitchen throughput and operational performance benchmarking.']
  unit_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'Selling price per single unit of the menu item at the time of order, before modifiers, discounts, or taxes. Reflects the menu price in effect during the transaction for historical accuracy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this order item record, such as a quantity change, void, or status update. Supports change data capture (CDC) and audit trail requirements in the Silver Layer.']
  void_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for voiding this line item (e.g., GUEST_CHANGE, PREP_ERROR, OUT_OF_STOCK, MANAGER_COMP). Null when item is not voided. Supports waste tracking and operational exception reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GUEST_CHANGE|PREP_ERROR|OUT_OF_STOCK|MANAGER_COMP|ALLERGY|DUPLICATE — promote to reference product]']
  waste_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item was recorded as food waste (True) rather than served to a guest. Supports Food Waste Percentage (Waste%) tracking, inventory reconciliation, and sustainability reporting.']
  waste_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for food waste on this line item when waste_flag is True. Supports root-cause analysis for Waste% reduction initiatives and HACCP compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each menu item within a guest order. Captures the ordered menu item, quantity, unit price, modifiers applied, line-level discounts, PMIX contribution, preparation instructions, KDS station routing, and item-level fulfillment status. Supports PMIX analysis, COGS% calculation, and kitchen throughput reporting.'
}

Table order.order_modifier {
  order_modifier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order modifier record applied to an order line item. Primary key for the order_modifier data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  applied_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance audit requires recording which employee applied a modifier (e.g., discount, special request) to ensure authorization and traceability.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: order_modifier.order_channel is a denormalized STRING storing the service channel for each modifier application. Normalizing to channel_id FK referencing order.channel.channel_id enforces referential ']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order transaction header. Enables direct order-level aggregation and reporting without requiring a join through the order item. Sourced from Oracle MICROS POS transaction records.']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: Modifiers (add bacon, sub gluten-free bun) map to specific ingredients for allergen compliance reporting, COGS delta calculation, and inventory depletion tracking. Foodservice operators require modifi']
  modifier_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the modifier group to which this modifier belongs (e.g., \'Protein Options\', \'Sauce Selection\', \'Temperature\'). Supports modifier group-level reporting and menu engineering analysis. Links to the menu domain modifier_group catalog.']
  order_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order line item to which this modifier is applied. Establishes the header-detail relationship between the order item and its customizations. Aligns with Oracle MICROS POS order item detail records.']
  primary_order_menu_modifier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the menu modifier master record defining the base modifier configuration, pricing rules, and preparation instructions. Links to the menu domain modifier catalog.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional campaign or Limited Time Offer (LTO) that triggered or discounted this modifier. Supports marketing campaign attribution and promotional CoGS% impact analysis.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where this modifier was applied. Enables location-level modifier adoption analysis, Product Mix (PMIX) reporting, and kitchen execution fidelity tracking.']
