  order_channel varchar [note: 'The service channel through which the order was placed (pos = in-store Point of Sale, drive_thru = Drive-Thru (DT), olo = Online Ordering (OLO), third_party_delivery = Third-Party Delivery (3PD), kiosk = self-service kiosk, catering). Used for channel-level tax analysis and compliance.']
  original_tax_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'The tax amount as originally computed at the time of order placement, before any post-transaction adjustments. Populated when tax_status = \'adjusted\' to preserve the audit trail of the original calculation.']
  period_date date [note: 'The business date (yyyy-MM-dd) to which this tax line is attributed for tax remittance and financial period reporting. May differ from the transaction timestamp for end-of-day batch processing or period adjustments.']
  pos_tax_line_ref varchar [note: 'The native tax line identifier or sequence number assigned by Oracle MICROS POS for this tax entry. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the POS transaction log and the lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.0875 for 8.75%). Sourced from the tax engine or POS tax configuration at the time of the transaction. Used for tax amount validation and audit.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'The tax amount refunded to the guest when is_refunded is True. May be a partial refund (less than tax_amount) for partial order refunds. Used for financial reconciliation and tax reversal reporting.']
  remittance_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (e.g., \'2024-Q1\', \'2024-03\') in which this tax amount is scheduled for remittance to the tax authority. Used for tax filing calendar management and period-end close.']
  remittance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this tax amount has been remitted to the applicable tax authority (pending = not yet remitted, remitted = successfully paid to authority, not_applicable = exempt or zero-rate, failed = remittance attempt failed). Supports tax compliance tracking.']
  sap_tax_document_ref varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI-TX tax document number associated with this tax line for GL posting and tax remittance. Enables end-to-end traceability from POS transaction to ERP financial posting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this tax line record originated (micros_pos = Oracle MICROS POS, sap_s4hana = SAP S/4HANA FI-TX, olo = Olo Digital Ordering Platform, manual = manually entered). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (or equivalent provincial code) of the tax jurisdiction where the restaurant is located (e.g., \'IL\', \'CA\', \'TX\'). Used for state-level tax rate lookup, remittance routing, and regulatory reporting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The internal or POS-assigned tax code identifying the specific tax rule applied (e.g., \'TX_CA_SALES\', \'TX_CHI_BEV\'). Maps to the tax condition record in SAP S/4HANA and Oracle MICROS POS tax configuration.']
  tax_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the tax as displayed on the guest receipt or financial report (e.g., \'Illinois Sales Tax\', \'Chicago Beverage Tax\', \'CA Bag Fee\'). Required for receipt compliance and guest transparency.']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this tax line record. \'applied\' = tax successfully computed and applied; \'voided\' = tax line cancelled due to order void; \'adjusted\' = tax amount corrected post-transaction; \'pending\' = awaiting tax engine resolution; \'reversed\' = tax reversed for refund.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'The category of tax applied to this line (e.g., sales_tax, vat, beverage_tax, bag_fee, excise_tax, service_tax). Supports differentiated tax reporting and compliance across jurisdictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_tax|vat|beverage_tax|bag_fee|excise_tax|service_tax|surcharge|environmental_fee — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  taxable_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'The portion of the order subtotal that is subject to this specific tax line, expressed in the transaction currency. Excludes non-taxable items, exempt amounts, and tax-inclusive components. Used for tax calculation validation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental loads, and audit trail.']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line was voided, if applicable. Populated only when tax_status = \'voided\'. Used for void audit trail and financial reversal processing.']

  Note: 'Records tax line details applied to a guest order, supporting multi-jurisdiction tax compliance. Captures tax authority (federal, state, county, city), tax type (sales tax, VAT, beverage tax, bag fee), tax rate, taxable amount, tax amount, and whether the tax was included in the menu price or added at checkout. Supports financial reconciliation and regulatory tax reporting across all restaurant locations.'
}

Table order.delivery_platform {
  delivery_platform_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for delivery_platform']
  commission_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Delivery platform commissions (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats fees) are accrued to a specific commission expense GL account. Restaurant finance teams post platform commission accruals by GL account for exp']
  parent_delivery_platform_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on delivery_platform (parent_delivery_platform_id)']
  average_delivery_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical time in minutes from order acceptance to delivery completion for this platform.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contractual agreement with the platform expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended contracts).']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contractual agreement with the platform became effective.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery platform record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_platform_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the platform in internal systems.']
  delivery_platform_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the delivery platform (e.g., DoorDash, UberEats).']
  delivery_platform_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the delivery platform.']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission fee charged by the platform as a percentage of order value.']
  integration_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to exchange data with the platform.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the delivery platform record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks about the platform.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the platform was first integrated and made available for order routing.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the platform based on ownership and integration model.']
  support_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for technical or operational support contacts at the platform.']
  support_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for technical or operational support contacts at the platform.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for delivery_platform. Referenced by delivery_platform_id.'
}

SSOT for supplier identity and contact details across supply chain operations. Sourced from Coupa Procurement supplier master and SAP S/4HANA vendor master (MM).'
}

Table supply.ingredient {
  ingredient_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated surrogate key uniquely identifying each ingredient record.']

Business justification: Ingredients are mapped to GL accounts for inventory valuation and COGS posting (food cost GL vs. packaging GL). Foodservice cost accountants expect each ingredient to carry a GL account for automated ']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Required for HACCP compliance: each ingredient must be linked to its HACCP plan for safety documentation and audit reporting.']
  allergen_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of allergens present in the ingredient.']
  carbohydrate_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of carbohydrates by weight in the ingredient.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating where the ingredient was produced or sourced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the ingredient record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost_per_unit (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the ingredient becomes available for ordering.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the ingredient is no longer available (null if indefinite).']
  fat_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total fat by weight in the ingredient.']
  haccp_classification varchar [note: 'Risk level of the ingredient according to HACCP guidelines.']
  halal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient meets halal certification requirements.']
  ingredient_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the ingredient for sourcing, menu engineering, and cost analysis.']

  ingredient_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ingredient in the catalog.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent inspection.']
  kosher_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient meets kosher certification requirements.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent food safety inspection for this ingredient.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days from order placement to receipt of the ingredient.']
  non_gmo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient is verified as non‑genetically modified.']
  nutritional_calories_per_unit decimal(8,2) [note: 'Energy content per unit of the ingredient, expressed in kilocalories.']

  par_level varchar [note: 'Periodic Automatic Replenishment Level – minimum inventory quantity to trigger re‑order.']
  protein_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of protein by weight in the ingredient.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the ingredient can be stored before it is considered expired.']
  sodium_mg_per_unit decimal(8,2) [note: 'Sodium content per unit of the ingredient, expressed in milligrams.']
  standard_weight_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Typical weight of one unit of the ingredient in the specified unit_of_measure.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended storage temperature range for the ingredient, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the main category (e.g., "leafy_green", "citrus_fruit").']
  traceability_batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number used for ingredient traceability throughout the supply chain.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Default unit used to quantify the ingredient in purchasing and inventory (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the ingredient record.']
  usda_grade varchar [note: 'USDA quality grade assigned to the ingredient.']
  vitamin_c_mg_per_unit decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vitamin C content per unit of the ingredient, expressed in milligrams.']
  waste_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical percentage of the ingredient that is wasted during preparation or storage.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all food ingredients, raw materials, beverages, and packaging SKUs procured across the foodservice supply chain. Captures SKU code, ingredient name, commodity category, unit of measure, allergen flags (Big 9), USDA grade, country of origin, shelf life days, storage temperature requirements (ambient/refrigerated/frozen), and HACCP critical control classification. Serves as the supply-side item master linking to menu domain BOM for recipe costing. SSOT for ingredient identity across supply, inventory, and menu domains. Sourced from SAP MM material master and MarketMan Inventory Management.'
}

This links transactional procurement data to the ingredi']

Linking POs to the NRO project enables openi']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Internal purchase‑order entity must be associated with the restaurant site it serves for accurate inventory and financial reconciliation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: A purchase order is issued to a specific supplier. supply_purchase_order must reference supply_supplier to identify which vendor the PO was sent to. This is a foundational procurement relationship — w']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: PURCHASE_ORDER creation is done per restaurant location; linking PO to unit enables inventory budgeting and location‑specific spend reporting.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for every purchase order issued to suppliers for food ingredients, beverages, packaging, and non-food supplies. Captures PO number, supplier reference, order date, requested delivery date, ship-to distribution center or restaurant, total PO value, currency, payment terms, approval status, and sourcing event linkage. Represents the contractual commitment to buy. Sourced from Coupa Procurement PO module and SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing.'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each goods receipt transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links receipt of goods to cost center to allocate receiving costs and inventory valuation to the responsible cost center.']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or restaurant where goods were received.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Goods receipts must be assigned to a financial period for period-end cut-off and food cost accrual accuracy. Foodservice operators require GR-to-period mapping for accurate COGS reporting and inventor']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Goods receipts trigger GR/IR (goods receipt/invoice receipt) GL postings in three-way match. The GL account on the GR drives automated inventory or expense posting — fundamental to foodservice AP and ']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Goods receiving is a defined CCP in HACCP plans. Receiving managers follow the applicable HACCP plan to accept or reject deliveries based on temperature and condition criteria. Regulatory cold-chain c']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: A goods receipt is the dock-level confirmation of an inbound shipment arriving at a DC or restaurant. goods_receipt must reference inbound_shipment to link the physical receiving event to the freight ']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: A goods receipt records the inbound delivery of a specific ingredient batch. goods_receipt tracks batch_number, lot_number, expiration_date, temperature_celsius, and is_cold_chain_compliant — all ingr']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the receipt inspection.']
  primary_goods_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the receipt inspection.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: A goods receipt is the physical confirmation of a purchase order being fulfilled. goods_receipt currently stores purchase_order_number as a denormalized STRING. Adding supply_purchase_order_id FK norm']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or restaurant where goods were received.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Internal batch identifier assigned during receiving for inventory control.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Optional textual remarks entered by the inspector during receipt.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Overall condition of the received shipment (accepted, rejected, or partially accepted).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the goods receipt record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the total_cost field.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the received goods should not be used or sold.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receipt (e.g., open, partial, closed, cancelled).']
  is_cold_chain_compliant boolean [note: 'True if temperature remained within required range during transport and receipt.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier‑provided lot or batch number for traceability.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable receipt number assigned by the receiving system (e.g., MIGO document number).']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the goods were recorded as received.']
  receiving_method varchar [note: 'How the goods arrived at the receiving location (e.g., dock, delivery, third‑party carrier, internal transfer).']
  temperature_celsius decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured temperature (in Celsius) of the goods at the moment of receipt.']
  temperature_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'True if measured temperature fell outside the acceptable range.']
  total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the goods received, before any discounts or taxes.']
  total_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Sum of all item quantities received in this receipt (units may be pieces, kg, liters, etc.).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the goods receipt record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of inbound goods received at a distribution center or restaurant, including both header-level receipt information and line-level detail per SKU. Header captures receipt date/time, receiving location, PO reference, receiving condition, and inspector ID. Lines capture specific ingredient/SKU received, quantity accepted/rejected, lot number, expiration date, temperature at receipt (cold chain compliance), storage location assigned, and variance from PO quantity. Critical for three-way match (PO-receipt-invoice), HACCP traceability, and ingredient-level lot tracking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (MIGO) and MarketMan receiving module.'
}

  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. The existing exclusivity_region text field is a denormalization of']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent contract audit.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the contract against internal and regulatory standards.']
  confidentiality_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a confidentiality provision is included.']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context about the contract.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic copy of the signed contract document.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the contract indicating its business purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for contract pricing.']
  data_protection_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates inclusion of data protection requirements (e.g., GDPR, CCPA).']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterms defining responsibility for delivery and risk.']
  dispute_resolution varchar [note: 'Mechanism for resolving disputes (e.g., arbitration, litigation).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes legally binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or ends, if applicable.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has exclusive rights for the contracted items.']
  executed_date date [note: 'Date the contract was executed and entered into the system.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Specific legal framework applied to interpret the contract.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Minimum insurance coverage the supplier must maintain.']
  legal_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State, province, or country whose laws govern the contract.']
  liability_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary liability the supplier assumes under the contract.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method of payment for invoicing under the contract.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms defined in the contract.']
  rebate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rebate applied once the threshold amount is reached.']
  rebate_threshold_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cumulative spend amount that triggers a rebate.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration required to issue a renewal notice.']
  renewal_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract renews automatically, manually, or not at all.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for goods covered by the contract.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the contract was signed by all parties.']
  supplier_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to give notice before terminating the contract.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  volume_tier_1_min varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity required to qualify for the first volume discount tier.']
  volume_tier_1_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price applied when the purchase quantity meets tier 1 minimum.']
  volume_tier_2_min varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity required to qualify for the second volume discount tier.']
  volume_tier_2_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price applied when the purchase quantity meets tier 2 minimum.']

  Note: 'Master record for negotiated supply agreements and their associated price schedules. Captures contract number, effective and expiration dates, volume commitments, rebate agreements, exclusivity terms, renewal type, and compliance status. Includes contracted unit prices per ingredient/SKU with validity periods, volume tiers, and price types (fixed, indexed, market-based). Used by supply chain for price validation during goods receipt and invoice matching, and for COGS variance analysis. Sourced from Coupa contract management module.'
}

Table supply.contract_price {
  contract_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the contract price record.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Contract prices are effective within financial periods for budget vs. actual food cost variance analysis. Foodservice finance teams require period-level contract price tracking to reconcile contracted']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: A contract price record defines the negotiated unit price for a specific ingredient or SKU. contract_price must reference ingredient to identify which item the price applies to. This enables price com']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ingredient or stock keeping unit covered by this price.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: contract_price is the line-level price schedule record under a supplier_contract header. This is the canonical header-line relationship for contract pricing. contract_price must reference supplier_con']
  contract_price_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the contract price (e.g., CP-2024-001).']
  contract_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price record was initially created in the source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  default_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Baseline price per unit (SKU) when no volume tier applies. [Moved from supplier_contract: default_price on supplier_contract represents a baseline price per SKU — but in a contract covering multiple ingredients, each ingredient has its own contracted price. This attribute is more accurately represented as contracted_unit_price on the contract_price association, where it is scoped to a specific contract-ingredient combination. Keeping it on supplier_contract implies a single price for the entire contract, which misrepresents multi-SKU contracts.]']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the contracted price becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the contracted price expires (null if open‑ended).']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price record is the currently active price for the SKU.']
  price_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value of the contracted unit price.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the price was changed or created.']
  price_index_reference varchar [note: 'External index or commodity reference used for indexed pricing (e.g., USDA Corn Index).']
  price_tier_max_qty decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound quantity for this price tier (null if no upper limit).']
  price_tier_min_qty decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound quantity for this price tier (volume break).']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price calculation method.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the default price (e.g., per unit, per case). [Moved from supplier_contract: price_uom on supplier_contract is the UOM for the default_price, which is ingredient-specific. Since each ingredient in a contract may have a different UOM (e.g., beef by lb, buns by case), this attribute belongs on the contract_price association as unit_of_measure, scoped to the specific contract-ingredient price line.]']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp capturing when the record was first loaded into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp capturing the last load or refresh time for the record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the price (e.g., kilogram, pound, each).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the price record.']

  Note: 'Contracted unit price records tied to a supplier contract for specific ingredients or SKUs over a defined validity period. Captures ingredient/SKU reference, contracted unit price, currency, price validity start and end dates, price tier (volume break), and price type (fixed, indexed, market-based). Enables COGS% variance analysis against actual invoice prices and supports menu costing in the menu domain.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the distribution center record.']
  waste_management_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the center holds a certified waste‑management program.']

  Note: 'Master record for each distribution center (DC), warehouse, and logistics node in the foodservice supply network including company-owned DCs, third-party logistics (3PL) facilities, cross-dock locations, and commissary kitchens. Captures DC code, name, full address, geographic region/market, facility type (ambient, refrigerated, frozen, mixed-temp), total storage capacity (pallet positions), operating hours, supported restaurant count, primary and backup DC assignments, and facility manager. SSOT for DC identity used across inbound logistics, inventory allocation, and restaurant replenishment. Sourced from SAP EWM and network design tools.'
}

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking each inbound freight shipment from a supplier to a distribution center or restaurant. Supports inbound logistics visibility and carrier performance tracking.'
}

Foodservice operators require cost cente']

Business justification: Recall events are frequently caused by a CCP failure (e.g., temperature exceedance, contamination at a specific process step). FDA/USDA recall root-cause investigations require identifying the specifi']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_center. Business justification: A recall event affects specific distribution center locations where the recalled ingredient was stored or distributed. recall_event currently stores affected_dc_locations as a denormalized STRING. Add']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Recall costs must be recorded in the correct financial period for P&L impact, regulatory disclosure, and insurance claim timing. Foodservice finance teams require period-level recall cost tracking for']

Business justification: Recall expenses post to specific GL accounts (recall expense, extraordinary items) for financial statement disclosure and insurance recovery. Required for SEC/regulatory reporting and accurate P&L imp']

Business justification: FDA/USDA recall investigations require traceability to the HACCP plan governing the recalled ingredient at time of recall. Food safety directors must identify which HACCP plan was in effect and whethe']

Adding ingredient_id FK normalizes this to the ingredient master, e']
  recall_coordinator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FDA/USDA recall regulations (21 CFR Part 7) require a designated recall coordinator responsible for executing recalls, notifying affected restaurant locations, and filing regulatory reports. Every foo']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Recall scope identification requires knowing which inventory SKUs are affected by a recall event. recall_event already links to ingredient (supply domain) but not to the inventory stock_item. Foodserv']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: A recall event is initiated by or attributed to a specific supplier. recall_event must reference supply_supplier to identify the source vendor of the recalled product.   territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. Business justification: Territory-scoped recall containment is a named FDA/USDA regulatory process. When a recall is geographically bounded, linking recall_event to the affected franchise territory enables automated franchis']
  affected_lot_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of lot or batch numbers impacted by the recall.']

  compliance_usda boolean [note: 'True if the product complies with USDA regulations.']

  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the recalled quantity at the time of recall.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the recalled quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recall event record.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a food product or ingredient recall event initiated by a supplier, regulatory agency (FDA/USDA), or internal quality team. Captures recall reference number, recall class (Class I, II, III), affected ingredient/SKU, affected lot numbers, recall reason (contamination, allergen mislabeling, foreign material), recall initiation date, affected DC and restaurant locations, quantity recalled, disposal method, and regulatory notification status. Enables rapid traceability response using ingredient_lot linkage.'
}

Table supply.approved_vendor_item {
  approved_vendor_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the approved_vendor_item association']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the ingredient or SKU being sourced from this supplier.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the approved supplier in the supply_supplier master record.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this supplier for this specific ingredient on the Approved Vendor List. Managed by procurement and food safety teams.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard purchase cost for one unit of the ingredient in the specified currency. [Moved from ingredient: Purchase cost per unit is negotiated per supplier-ingredient combination. Storing it on the ingredient master implies a single price, which is incorrect when multiple suppliers offer the same ingredient at different prices. It belongs on the approved_vendor_item association as unit_price.]']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which this supplier-ingredient sourcing agreement becomes active and the supplier is approved to fulfill orders for this ingredient.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this supplier-ingredient sourcing agreement expires and the supplier is no longer approved for this ingredient. Null if the agreement is open-ended.']
  lead_time_days decimal [note: 'Number of days from order placement to receipt of this ingredient from this specific supplier. Supplier-specific and belongs to the sourcing relationship, not the ingredient master.']
  min_order_quantity decimal [note: 'Minimum number of units that must be ordered per purchase order for this supplier-ingredient combination. A contractual term that belongs to the sourcing relationship.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier is the designated primary (preferred) source for this ingredient. Used in multi-sourcing strategy to determine default sourcing decisions.']
  unit_price decimal [note: 'Negotiated purchase price per unit of the ingredient from this specific supplier. Varies by supplier-ingredient combination and cannot reside on either master record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the sourcing contract between a supplier and an ingredient in the foodservice supply chain. It captures the Approved Vendor List (AVL) entry that procurement teams actively manage — recording which suppliers are approved to provide which ingredients, at what price, under what terms, and for what time period. Each record links one supply_supplier to one ingredient with attributes that exist only in the context of this supplier-ingredient sourcing relationship, enabling multi-sourcing strategy, price benchmarking, and supply continuity planning.. Existence Justification: In foodservice procurement, a supplier provides many ingredients (e.g., a broadline distributor like Sysco supplies beef, lettuce, cooking oil, and packaging), and a single ingredient (e.g., ground beef) is sourced from multiple approved suppliers at different prices, lead times, and minimum order quantities. This relationship is actively managed by procurement teams as an Approved Vendor List (AVL) or Supplier Item Catalog — a recognized operational business concept with its own lifecycle (approval, pricing updates, deactivation). The relationship carries substantial data (unit_price, lead_time_days, min_order_quantity, preferred_supplier_flag, effective_from, effective_until) that belongs to neither the supplier master nor the ingredient master alone.'
}

Table inventory.stock_item {
  stock_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock item. Primary key for the stock_item product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allows allocation of inventory holding costs to specific cost centers for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for financial reporting of inventory valuation and COGS per item, enabling accurate GL posting of stock costs.']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Each stock item (ingredient) is governed by a specific HACCP plan defining its safe handling, temperature limits, and critical controls. stock_item already stores haccp_max_temp_f/haccp_min_temp_f. Li']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: REQUIRED: Ingredient Inventory Valuation Report needs each stock item linked to its ingredient master for nutrition, allergen, and compliance tracking.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.uom. Business justification: stock_item.unit_of_measure is a STRING column that denotes the storage/base unit of measure for the SKU. The uom table is the authoritative UOM reference master in this domain. Adding uom_id FK normal']
  allergen_eggs boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain eggs. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.']
  allergen_fish boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain fish. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.']
  allergen_milk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain milk or dairy derivatives. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.']
  allergen_peanuts boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain peanuts. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.']
  allergen_shellfish boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain crustacean shellfish. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.']
  allergen_soybeans boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain soybeans. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.']
  allergen_tree_nuts boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain tree nuts. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.']
  allergen_wheat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain wheat. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.']
  case_pack_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual units contained in one case. Used for ordering and receiving conversions.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the standard cost (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock item record was first created in the inventory management system.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date when the item was or will be discontinued from inventory. Used for phase-out planning and historical analysis.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'International product identifier used for supply chain tracking and barcode scanning. May be UPC, EAN, or other GS1 format.']
  haccp_max_temp_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum safe storage temperature in Fahrenheit to maintain product quality and food safety standards.']
  haccp_min_temp_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum safe storage temperature in Fahrenheit required to prevent bacterial growth and ensure food safety compliance.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stock item is currently available for ordering and use. Inactive items are retained for historical reporting but not available for new transactions.']
  is_gluten_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is certified gluten-free or naturally contains no gluten. Critical for dietary accommodation and menu labeling.']
  is_gmo_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is certified non-GMO. Used for menu transparency and consumer preference alignment.']
  is_halal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item meets Islamic dietary law requirements. Used for menu segmentation and cultural accommodation.']
  is_kosher boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item meets Jewish dietary law requirements. Used for menu segmentation and cultural accommodation.']
  is_organic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is certified organic according to USDA standards. Used for menu marketing and premium pricing.']
  is_vegan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains no animal products or by-products. Used for menu filtering and dietary preference alignment.']
  is_vegetarian boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains no meat, poultry, or seafood. Used for menu filtering and dietary preference alignment.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the stock item by food service category. Used for inventory organization, COGS analysis, and procurement planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protein|produce|dairy|dry_goods|beverage|paper_goods|cleaning|packaging — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the stock item including brand, size, packaging, and other distinguishing characteristics.']
  item_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the stock item as it appears in inventory management and ordering systems.']
  item_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing finer granularity within the item category (e.g., beef, chicken, lettuce, tomato).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock item record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  par_level decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target inventory quantity that should be maintained at all times to support operational needs without overstocking. Used for automated reorder triggers.']
  reorder_point decimal(10,2) [note: 'Inventory threshold quantity that triggers a replenishment order. Set below PAR level to account for lead time.']
  reorder_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard quantity to order when inventory falls below reorder point. Calculated to restore inventory to PAR level.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the item remains usable after receipt. Critical for waste management, FIFO rotation, and food safety compliance.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the stock item across all restaurant units and inventory systems. This is the business identifier used in MarketMan and POS systems.']
  standard_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Expected unit cost of the item used for COGS calculations and variance analysis. Updated periodically based on procurement contracts.']
  storage_class varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature zone for the item. Determines BOH storage location and HACCP compliance requirements.']
  vendor_item_code varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s unique identifier for this item. Used for purchase order matching and invoice reconciliation.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of usable product after preparation and trimming. Critical for recipe costing and waste tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for every SKU tracked in restaurant inventory — food, beverage, packaging, and non-food supplies. Captures SKU code, item name, unit of measure (UOM), item category (protein, produce, dry goods, beverage, paper goods, cleaning), storage class (ambient, refrigerated, frozen), reorder point, reorder quantity, standard cost, vendor item code, shelf life days, HACCP temperature range, allergen flags, daypart applicability, seasonal adjustment flags, and active status. Also owns PAR-level configuration per unit-location: PAR quantity, minimum quantity (reorder point), maximum quantity (shelf capacity), day-of-week overrides, seasonal adjustment flags, and effective date range. This is the SSOT for all stockable items and their replenishment parameters managed through MarketMan.'
}

Table inventory.stock_location {
  stock_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock location. Primary key.']
  brand_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand_standard. Business justification: Brand standard compliance audits for storage: each storage location must comply with a governing brand standard defining temperature zone requirements, HACCP storage protocols, and access controls. Br']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Stores overhead allocation of each storage location to cost centers for accurate expense tracking.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Facility‑level inventory mapping needed for inventory valuation and rent‑cost allocation reports per physical restaurant property.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for HACCP temperature monitoring accountability; each storage location has an assigned manager employee who signs compliance reports.']
  primary_stock_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the refrigeration or climate control equipment serving this location, used for maintenance tracking and temperature monitoring integration.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit where this stock location is physically situated.']
  access_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether electronic or physical access control (keycard, PIN, lock) is required to enter this location.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the stock location was first activated and made available for inventory storage.']
  allows_receiving boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as a receiving point for incoming inventory deliveries.']
  allows_transfers boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory can be transferred into or out of this location to other stock locations within the restaurant or across units.']
  allows_waste_tracking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether waste events (spoilage, damage, expiration) can be recorded against inventory in this location for Waste% calculation.']
  bin_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual storage bins or compartments within this location for granular SKU organization.']
  building_section varchar [note: 'Specific section or wing of the building where the location is situated (e.g., Kitchen, Bar, Prep Area, Receiving Dock).']
  capacity_cubic_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total storage capacity of the location measured in cubic feet.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock location record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled physical inventory cycle counts for this location to maintain stock accuracy.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the stock location was deactivated or decommissioned and is no longer available for inventory storage.']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Physical floor level where the storage location is situated (e.g., 1 for ground floor, -1 for basement, 2 for second floor).']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent physical inventory cycle count was completed for this location.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance or inspection was performed on the storage location or its equipment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock location record was most recently updated.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storage location within the restaurant unit (e.g., WIC01 for walk-in cooler 1, DRY02 for dry storage room 2).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the stock location (e.g., Walk-In Cooler, Dry Storage Room, Bar Storage, Prep Area Shelf).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage location based on its primary function and environmental control (refrigerated, frozen, ambient, bar, prep_area, service_station).']
  next_scheduled_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the next physical inventory cycle count is scheduled for this location.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the next scheduled maintenance or inspection is planned for the storage location or its equipment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information, special handling instructions, or operational notes about the stock location.']
  par_level_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PAR level inventory management is enabled for this location to trigger automatic replenishment orders.']
  primary_commodity_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of inventory items typically stored in this location (e.g., Proteins, Dairy, Produce, Dry Goods, Beverages, Alcohol).']
  requires_haccp_monitoring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location requires HACCP monitoring and documentation for food safety compliance.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification for the location indicating access control requirements (open: unrestricted, restricted: authorized staff only, locked: key/code required, high_value: premium items with enhanced security).']
  shelf_count varchar [note: 'Number of shelves or storage levels available in this location for organizing inventory.']
  stock_location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the stock location (active: in use, inactive: temporarily not in use, maintenance: under repair, decommissioned: permanently retired).']
  storage_area_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the location is in Back of House (BOH) or Front of House (FOH) area of the restaurant.']
  target_temperature_max_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum target temperature in Fahrenheit for this storage location to maintain food safety and quality standards.']
  target_temperature_min_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum target temperature in Fahrenheit for this storage location to maintain food safety and quality standards.']
  temperature_monitoring_frequency_hours varchar [note: 'Required frequency in hours for temperature checks and logging at this location to ensure food safety compliance.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for the location (freezer: below 0°F, cooler: 32-40°F, ambient: room temperature, controlled: climate-controlled but not refrigerated).']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical storage location within a restaurant unit where inventory is held — walk-in cooler, walk-in freezer, dry storage room, BOH prep area, FOH service station, bar storage. Captures location code, location name, location type (refrigerated, frozen, ambient, bar), temperature zone, capacity (cubic feet or shelf count), restaurant unit reference, and active status. Enables granular stock-on-hand tracking by storage zone.'
}

Table inventory.on_hand_balance {
  on_hand_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the on-hand balance record. Primary key for the inventory position snapshot.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links on‑hand quantities to cost centers to reflect inventory value in financial statements per cost center.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: On‑hand snapshots need facility context for real‑estate cost allocation, insurance valuation, and property‑level inventory audits.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: On-hand balance snapshots are taken at period end for inventory valuation on the balance sheet. Linking to financial_period enables period-over-period inventory value comparison and supports COGS calc']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: FRANCHISEE‑LEVEL INVENTORY DASHBOARD aggregates on‑hand balances per franchisee for performance and royalty calculations.']
  primary_on_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit where this inventory is held. Links to the restaurant operations master.']
  primary_on_sku_stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this balance is recorded. Links to the inventory item master.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location within the restaurant (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer, prep area). Links to storage location master.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.uom. Business justification: on_hand_balance.unit_of_measure is a STRING column denoting the UOM for the on-hand quantity snapshot. The uom table is the authoritative UOM reference master. Adding uom_id FK normalizes this referen']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'The ABC inventory classification based on value and usage velocity. A items are high-value/high-velocity requiring tight control, C items are low-value/low-velocity.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which unit_cost and extended_value are denominated.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this SKU should be physically counted as part of the cycle count program. Typically aligned with ABC classification.']
  days_until_expiration varchar [note: 'The number of days remaining until the expiration date. Used to prioritize usage and identify items at risk of waste.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date by which this inventory must be used or discarded. Critical for food safety, waste management, and FIFO (First In First Out) rotation.']
  extended_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of the on-hand inventory, calculated as quantity_on_hand multiplied by unit_cost. Used for financial reporting and COGS% analysis.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this inventory position. Determines whether the inventory is available for use, held for quality review, or flagged for disposal.']
  is_perishable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is a perishable item requiring temperature control and expiration tracking. True for fresh produce, dairy, meat; false for dry goods.']
  last_adjustment_date date [note: 'The date when the last inventory adjustment (waste, transfer, or correction) was recorded for this SKU at this location.']
  last_movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the last inventory transaction (receipt, transfer, issue, or adjustment) that affected this balance. Used to identify stagnant inventory.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'The date when the last physical inventory count was performed for this SKU at this location. Used to track audit compliance and inventory accuracy.']
  last_received_date date [note: 'The date when this SKU was last received into inventory at this location. Used to track inventory freshness and identify slow-moving items.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The supplier or manufacturer lot number for traceability and recall management. Critical for food safety compliance under HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points).']
  par_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'The target inventory level for this SKU at this location, used for automatic replenishment decisions. Represents the optimal stock level to maintain service without excess waste.']
  quantity_available decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net quantity available for use or sale, calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_reserved. Represents the true available-to-promise inventory.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total physical quantity of the SKU currently in stock at this location. Represents the gross inventory position before reservations.']
  quantity_reserved decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU that is committed to prep, production, or pending orders and not available for new allocation. Used in BOH (Back of House) prep planning.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this on-hand balance record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this on-hand balance record was last updated in the data platform. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'The inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered. When quantity_available falls below this threshold, the system generates a purchase order.']
  safety_stock decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum buffer stock maintained to protect against stockouts due to demand variability or supply delays. Part of the PAR level calculation.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'The business identifier code for the SKU. Denormalized from SKU master for reporting convenience.']
  sku_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the SKU. Denormalized from SKU master for reporting convenience.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this on-hand balance snapshot was captured. Represents the point-in-time inventory position for reporting and reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that provided this inventory balance record (MarketMan Inventory Management, SAP Materials Management, manual physical count, or POS integration).']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'The required storage temperature zone for this SKU (ambient/dry, refrigerated, or frozen). Critical for food safety compliance and storage location assignment.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost per unit of measure for this SKU at this location. Used to calculate extended inventory value and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold).']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to value this inventory (FIFO - First In First Out, LIFO - Last In First Out, weighted average, or standard cost). Determines COGS calculation.']
  variance_from_par decimal(18,4) [note: 'The difference between quantity_on_hand and par_level. Positive values indicate overstocking, negative values indicate understocking and trigger replenishment.']

  Note: 'Current stock-on-hand snapshot for each SKU at each storage location within a restaurant unit. Captures quantity on hand, quantity reserved (committed to prep), quantity available, last physical count date, last adjustment date, last received date, unit cost, extended value, and variance from PAR level. Updated by receiving, transfers, waste events, and physical counts. The authoritative real-time inventory position record used for replenishment decisions and food cost reporting.'
}

Table inventory.receiving_order {
  receiving_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the receiving order record. Primary key for inbound inventory transactions at restaurant unit level.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: 3-way match (PO + goods receipt + AP invoice) is a standard AP control in restaurant foodservice. Linking receiving_order to ap_invoice enables automated invoice matching, dispute resolution, and supp']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Receiving transactions are posted to a cost center for food cost allocation and budget variance reporting. Restaurant operators track receiving costs by cost center to manage food cost percent targets']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Receiving dock assignment per facility supports labor‑cost tracking and health‑inspection compliance at each restaurant location.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Received goods must be posted to the correct fiscal period for COGS accrual and inventory valuation. Period-end close in restaurant finance requires all receiving transactions to be period-stamped. No']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee receiving reports track supplier deliveries per franchisee to reconcile costs against royalty fees.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Goods receipt to inventory posting reconciliation is a core foodservice receiving control. The receiving_order (inventory posting) must reference the supply-side goods_receipt (physical inspection rec']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Receiving is a critical HACCP control point. Each receiving order must be processed per the applicable HACCP plan governing temperature checks, seal integrity, and quality inspection. receiving_order ']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Delivery performance tracking requires linking the inventory receiving event to the logistics shipment record. Foodservice operators compare scheduled vs actual arrival (inbound_shipment) against what']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: Three-way match (PO → receipt → invoice) is a fundamental AP control in foodservice procurement. Linking receiving_order to supply_purchase_order enables PO fulfillment reporting and quantity variance']
  receiving_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the restaurant manager or BOH staff member who inspected and accepted the delivery. Links to Workday HCM employee master.']
  receiving_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the restaurant manager or BOH staff member who inspected and accepted the delivery. Links to Workday HCM employee master.']
  receiving_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit receiving the goods. Links to restaurant operations master data.']
  receiving_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit receiving the goods. Links to restaurant operations master data.']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: A receiving_order is the physical fulfillment event for a replenishment_order. Linking receiving_order.replenishment_order_id → replenishment_order.replenishment_order_id closes the procurement loop: ']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Receiving orders occur during specific shifts; shift-level receiving reports (delivery accuracy, temperature compliance by shift) are standard foodservice operational controls. receiving_shift is a pl']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: receiving_order.receiving_location is a free-text STRING denoting where goods were received within the restaurant unit. Replacing it with a FK to stock_location.stock_location_id normalizes this refer']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the receiving order record was first created in MarketMan or source system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the received value (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations for international franchise units.']
  days_variance varchar [note: 'Calculated difference in days between expected and actual delivery date. Positive values indicate late delivery; negative values indicate early delivery. Used for supplier scorecard.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the goods were delivered to the restaurant unit. Used for inventory aging and FIFO tracking.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Packing slip or delivery note number provided by the supplier. Cross-reference document for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  delivery_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the delivery was received at the restaurant Back of House (BOH). Critical for temperature control compliance and HACCP documentation.']
  delivery_timeliness varchar [note: 'Classification of delivery punctuality relative to expected delivery date. On Time: within agreed window; Early: before window; Late: after window. Key supplier performance indicator.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the driver who delivered the goods. Captured for traceability and food safety audit trail per HACCP requirements.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Scheduled delivery date from the original purchase order. Used to calculate delivery timeliness and supplier on-time performance metrics.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the receiving order record was last updated. Tracks changes to status, variance resolution, or quality inspection updates.']
  posted_to_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that received goods have been posted to restaurant unit inventory in MarketMan and SAP S/4HANA. True when goods receipt is complete and stock levels updated.']
  posted_to_inventory_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the receiving transaction was posted to inventory system. Used for inventory valuation date and FIFO/LIFO costing.']
  quality_inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall quality assessment result for the received goods. Approved: meets standards; Rejected: fails quality criteria; Conditional: accepted with notes; Not Inspected: visual check only. Aligns with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) standards.']
  quality_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured by receiving manager regarding product quality, packaging condition, or other observations. Used for supplier feedback and continuous improvement.']
  receiving_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique receiving document number generated by MarketMan or restaurant POS system. Format: RCV-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  receiving_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receiving transaction. Pending: awaiting inspection; Partial: some items received; Complete: fully received and accepted; Rejected: delivery refused; Disputed: discrepancy under review.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation if receiving status is rejected. Captures specific food safety, quality, or compliance failure that led to refusal of delivery. Required for FDA and HACCP audit trail.']
  seal_integrity_check varchar [note: 'Verification that tamper-evident seals on delivery containers were intact upon arrival. Intact: seal unbroken; Broken: potential contamination risk; Not Applicable: no seal required. Part of food safety protocol.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that originated this receiving record. MarketMan: primary inventory system; SAP MM: ERP goods receipt; Manual Entry: paper-based receiving logged retrospectively.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the supplier or distributor. Denormalized for reporting convenience; authoritative source is supplier master.']
  temperature_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of temperature verification for refrigerated and frozen goods upon delivery. Pass: within safe range; Fail: out of range, reject or quarantine; Not Applicable: ambient goods. Critical for HACCP and FDA compliance.']
  temperature_recorded decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading in Fahrenheit recorded during delivery inspection for refrigerated or frozen items. Null for ambient goods. Used for food safety audit trail.']
  total_items_ordered varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) listed on the originating purchase order. Used for completeness verification.']
  total_items_received varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct SKUs actually received and accepted in this transaction. Variance from total_items_ordered triggers partial or disputed status.']
  total_received_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of all goods received in this transaction, in local currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation and variance analysis against PO.']
  variance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that quantity or value variance exists between PO and received goods. True triggers exception workflow for manager review and supplier follow-up.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for any discrepancy between ordered and received goods. Used for supplier performance tracking and root cause analysis.']

  Note: 'Records the receipt of goods delivered to a restaurant unit from a supplier or distribution center, capturing both delivery-level header information and line-level SKU detail in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: purchase order reference, delivery date/time, supplier reference, driver name, invoice number, total received value, receiving status (pending/partial/complete/rejected), temperature check result, seal integrity check, and receiving manager reference. Line attributes (one per SKU received): stock item reference, ordered vs received vs rejected quantities, unit of measure, unit cost, extended cost, lot number, expiration date, temperature at receipt (°F/°C), condition code (acceptable/damaged/short-dated/rejected), and variance reason code. Links to MarketMan purchase order and SAP S/4HANA goods receipt. Drives on-hand balance updates, COGS% tracking, and supplier performance measurement.'
}

Table inventory.physical_count {
  physical_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the physical inventory count event. Primary key.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fiscal period (month/quarter) for which this count is being performed, used for period-end food cost reconciliation and P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee audit of physical inventory counts is required for compliance and variance analysis.']
  gl_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger journal entry created to record the inventory variance from this physical count.']
  primary_physical_initiated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who initiated or started the physical count event.']
  primary_physical_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit where the physical count was performed.']
  recount_of_count_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original physical count ID if this count is a recount or correction of a previous count event.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: A physical count event can be scoped to a specific storage location within a restaurant unit (e.g., counting only the walk-in cooler or dry storage). Adding stock_location_id FK to physical_count link']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the physical count was completed and submitted.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the physical count was started by the counting team.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the physical count was approved by the manager or supervisor.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the physical count was cancelled or voided.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the physical count was cancelled or voided, if applicable.']
  count_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the physical inventory count was conducted.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'The method used to perform the physical count: manual (paper-based or manual entry), barcode-scan (handheld scanner), rfid (radio-frequency identification), or hybrid (combination of methods).']
  count_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the physical count event, formatted as PC-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trail.']
  count_period varchar [note: 'The daypart or shift during which the count was performed, aligned with restaurant operating periods.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the count event: scheduled (planned but not started), in-progress (counting underway), submitted (completed awaiting approval), approved (validated by manager), posted (applied to inventory system), or cancelled (voided).']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the count event: full (complete inventory), spot-check (random sample), cycle-count (scheduled rotation), pre-close (before period end), post-close (after period end), or opening (new restaurant opening).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this physical count record was first created in the system.']
  is_period_end_count boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this count is a mandatory period-end count for financial close and COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculation. True if period-end count, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this physical count record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the count event, including explanations for significant variances, operational issues during count, or special circumstances.']
  physical_inventory_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of inventory based on the physical count results, calculated by multiplying counted quantities by unit costs.']
  posted_to_gl_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the count variance was posted to the General Ledger (GL) for financial reporting, typically after approval.']
  recount_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a recount is required due to excessive variance or data quality issues. True if recount needed, False otherwise.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when the physical count was scheduled to begin.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system or application from which the physical count data originated: MarketMan (primary inventory system), SAP (ERP integration), Manual (paper-based), or Mobile-App (handheld device).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the physical count was submitted for approval after completion.']
  system_inventory_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of inventory according to the system (MarketMan) at the time of count, used as the baseline for variance calculation.']
  total_sku_counted varchar [note: 'The total number of unique SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) that were physically counted during this count event.']
  total_sku_with_variance varchar [note: 'The number of SKUs that showed a variance (difference) between system inventory and physical count.']
  total_variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value variance between system inventory and physical count results, calculated as (physical count value - system inventory value). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.']
  total_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The total variance expressed as a percentage of system inventory value, calculated as (variance amount / system inventory value) * 100. Key metric for COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) analysis and food cost control.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Primary reason code for inventory variance: theft (shrinkage), spoilage (expired/damaged), waste (prep waste), receiving-error (incorrect receipt), transfer-error (incorrect transfer), system-error (data entry mistake), or unknown. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theft|spoilage|waste|receiving-error|transfer-error|system-error|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records scheduled or ad-hoc physical inventory count events at a restaurant unit, capturing both count-level header metadata and line-level SKU counts in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: count date, count type (full/spot-check/cycle-count/pre-close/post-close), count status, count period, initiated-by/approved-by employees, total variance value and percentage. Line attributes (one per SKU-location counted): stock item reference, storage location, system quantity (book inventory), counted quantity, variance quantity and value ($), unit of measure, unit cost, count method (manual/scan), counted-by employee reference, and recount flag. Variance lines trigger investigation workflows and feed period-end food cost reconciliation and COGS% reporting.'
}

Table inventory.waste_log {
  waste_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the waste log entry. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Marketing ROI analysis needs to attribute waste spikes to specific campaigns; this FK supports that report.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.foodsafety_corrective_action. Business justification: When a food safety corrective action mandates disposal of non-compliant stock (temperature exceedance, contamination), the resulting waste event must be traceable to that corrective action for HACCP c']
  critical_control_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.critical_control_point. Business justification: waste_log.haccp_violation flag indicates a CCP breach caused the waste event. Linking to the specific critical_control_point enables HACCP deviation tracking, regulatory reporting, and CCP performance']
  equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.equipment_asset. Business justification: HACCP equipment-linked waste tracking: waste events caused by equipment failure (refrigeration breakdown, fryer malfunction) must reference the responsible equipment asset for food safety audits, insu']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Waste tracking per facility enables waste‑cost attribution to each property for sustainability and expense reporting.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Waste is reported by fiscal period for P&L variance analysis and food cost percent calculation. Restaurant operators and franchisors track waste cost against period budgets. No existing FK from waste_']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee waste reporting feeds sustainability metrics and waste‑related royalty adjustments.']
  health_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.health_inspection. Business justification: Health inspectors can mandate immediate disposal of food items during an inspection. Linking waste_log to health_inspection provides regulatory traceability — health departments and operators need to ']
  illness_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.illness_report. Business justification: When a foodborne illness is reported, suspected food items must be immediately disposed of and the disposal recorded. Linking waste_log to illness_report provides regulatory traceability for health de']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.maintenance_work_order. Business justification: Refrigeration or equipment failures cause waste events directly attributable to a maintenance work order. Linking waste_log to maintenance_work_order supports warranty/insurance claims, HACCP violatio']
  order_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_item. Business justification: Void-to-waste reconciliation: when an order item is voided, comped, or returned, the physical food becomes waste. Linking waste_log to the triggering order_item enables loss prevention reporting, food']
