  primary_waste_recorded_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who recorded the waste event in the system.']
  primary_waste_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where the waste event occurred.']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.recall_event. Business justification: FDA/USDA recall compliance requires linking disposal and waste records to the triggering recall event. Foodservice operators must demonstrate that recalled product was properly disposed of; waste_log ']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recipe or Bill of Materials (BOM) associated with the wasted prepared item, if applicable.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work shift during which the waste event occurred, used for labor and operational analysis.']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) that was wasted.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Waste is recorded per storage location; replacing the denormalized storage_location field with a FK enables location‑level waste analytics.']
  waste_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Posts waste expense to a designated GL account for waste cost reporting and profitability analysis.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the waste log entry was approved by a manager.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production or receiving batch identifier for the wasted item, used for traceability and food safety compliance.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective action implemented to prevent recurrence of this type of waste.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'The operational time segment during which the waste event occurred (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night).']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The method used to dispose of the wasted item: trash (landfill), compost, donation (food rescue), rendering (animal feed/byproduct), or other.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The use-by or best-before date of the wasted item, critical for expiration-related waste tracking.']
  haccp_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waste event was associated with a HACCP critical control point violation.']
  manager_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manager has reviewed and approved this waste log entry.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments about the waste event for operational context.']
  on_hand_quantity_before_waste decimal(12,4) [note: 'The inventory quantity on hand immediately before the waste event was recorded.']
  par_level_at_waste decimal(12,4) [note: 'The PAR level setting for this item at the time of waste, used to analyze whether overstocking contributed to waste.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when this waste log record was first created in the database.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when this waste log record was last modified.']
  responsible_station varchar [note: 'The operational area where the waste occurred: BOH (Back of House - kitchen/prep areas) or FOH (Front of House - service/dining areas).']
  temperature_at_waste decimal(5,2) [note: 'The temperature in Fahrenheit of the item or storage area at the time of waste, relevant for food safety and spoilage analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the waste quantity is measured (e.g., kilograms, pounds, each, gallons). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|lb|oz|g|ea|case|box|gal|ltr|ml — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waste_category varchar [note: 'The classification of the waste event: spoilage (deterioration), overproduction (excess prepared food), prep-loss (trimming/cooking loss), expiration (past use-by date), quality-reject (failed quality standards), theft-unknown (unexplained loss).']
  waste_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary cost of the wasted item in the restaurant\'s operating currency, calculated as quantity times unit cost.']
  waste_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the waste event occurred.']
  waste_prevention_opportunity varchar [note: 'Notes on potential process improvements or training opportunities identified from this waste event.']
  waste_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'The numeric quantity of the item wasted, measured in the unit of measure specified.']
  waste_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context and specific reason for the waste event.']
  waste_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the waste event was recorded.']

  Note: 'Records every food waste event at a restaurant unit — spoilage, overproduction, prep waste, expiration, and quality rejection. Captures waste date, waste time, stock item reference, waste quantity, unit of measure, waste cost ($), waste category (spoilage, overproduction, prep loss, expiration, quality-reject, theft/unknown), waste reason description, responsible station (BOH/FOH), recorded-by employee reference, and manager approval flag. Drives Waste% KPI calculation and supports yield management and COGS% optimization.'
}

Table inventory.stock_transfer {
  stock_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock transfer transaction. Primary key for the stock transfer record.']
  catering_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.catering_order. Business justification: Catering-driven inter-unit stock transfers: high-volume catering events at one unit may require stock transferred from a nearby unit. Linking stock_transfer to the catering_order enables event-driven ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Captures internal transfer cost allocation to cost centers for inter‑unit inventory movement accounting.']
  destination_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit or distribution center receiving the transferred inventory. For intra-unit transfers, this is the same as origin unit.']
  destination_stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer) within the destination unit where items will be received. Critical for BOH (Back of House) inventory accuracy.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Inter‑facility stock transfers require facility context to allocate transfer costs and maintain accurate on‑hand balances per property.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Inter-unit stock transfers affect inventory valuation at both origin and destination units and must be posted to the correct fiscal period. stock_transfer has fiscal_period as a plain text attr — a pr']
  origin_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit or distribution center from which inventory is being transferred. For intra-unit transfers, this is the same as destination unit.']
  origin_stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer) within the origin unit from which items are being transferred. Critical for BOH (Back of House) inventory accuracy.']
  primary_stock_requested_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically kitchen manager or shift supervisor) who initiated the stock transfer request. Used for accountability and audit trail.']
  tertiary_stock_received_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee at the destination unit who physically received and confirmed the transferred inventory. Null until transfer is received.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the stock transfer was cancelled. Null if transfer was not cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the transfer was cancelled. Used for operational analysis and process improvement.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the transportation carrier or delivery service used for inter-unit transfers. Null for intra-unit transfers or self-pickup.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stock transfer record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned or estimated date when the transferred inventory is expected to arrive at the destination. Used for receiving planning and PAR (Periodic Automatic Replenishment) level management.']
  external_transfer_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier from the source operational system (MarketMan, SAP MM). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (year and period number) to which this transfer is assigned for financial reporting. Format: YYYY-PNN (e.g., 2024-P03 for period 3 of fiscal year 2024).']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory transfer transaction was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting. May differ from physical transfer date due to period-end cutoffs.']
  haccp_monitoring_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer requires continuous temperature monitoring and documentation per HACCP critical control point protocols. True for high-risk perishable items.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from quality inspection documenting any issues, observations, or conditions. Used for quality tracking and supplier performance evaluation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stock transfer record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency classification for the transfer: routine (standard replenishment), high (needed within 24 hours), urgent (needed same day), emergency (critical stockout situation requiring immediate action).']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items in this transfer require formal quality inspection upon receipt per GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) or food safety protocols. True for high-value or high-risk items.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of quality inspection at receiving: not-required (no inspection needed), pending (awaiting inspection), passed (accepted), failed (rejected), conditional-accept (accepted with notes or partial rejection).']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transport inventory between locations: internal-delivery (company fleet), courier (contracted delivery service), third-party (3PD logistics provider), self-pickup (destination unit retrieves), direct-transfer (hand-carried for intra-unit).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that originated this stock transfer record: MARKETMAN (MarketMan Inventory Management), SAP-MM (SAP Materials Management), MANUAL (manually entered), LEGACY (migrated from prior system).']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer requires temperature-controlled transportation to maintain food safety and quality. True for perishable items requiring refrigeration or freezing per HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) standards.']
  temperature_zone_required varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone for transport: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (33-40°F), frozen (0°F or below), multi-temp (mixed temperature requirements). Critical for HACCP compliance and food safety.']
  total_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) line items included in this transfer. Used for transfer complexity assessment and receiving workload planning.']
  total_quantity_transferred decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all items transferred, summed across all line items. Unit of measure varies by item (cases, pounds, gallons, each). Used for high-level transfer volume tracking.']
  total_transfer_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of all items included in this stock transfer, calculated at standard cost. Critical for COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) tracking and inventory valuation across units.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number provided by carrier for monitoring in-transit status. Null for internal transfers without formal tracking.']
  transfer_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the stock transfer was approved by authorized manager. Null if not yet approved or if auto-approved (same as request date).']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transfer document number used for tracking and reference in operational systems and communications. Format: STR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  transfer_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for initiating the stock transfer: par-replenishment (restore PAR levels), excess-stock (reduce overstock), expiring-soon (move near-expiry items), quality-issue (return defective goods), menu-change (redistribute due to menu update), seasonal-adjustment (rebalance for seasonal demand).']
  transfer_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context for the transfer. Used for operational communication and audit documentation.']
  transfer_received_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory was physically received and confirmed at the destination location. Null until transfer is fully or partially received.']
  transfer_request_date date [note: 'Date when the stock transfer was initially requested. Used for tracking lead times and transfer cycle analytics.']
  transfer_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory physically left the origin location. Used for in-transit tracking and speed of service metrics.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer: requested (initiated but pending approval), approved (authorized for execution), rejected (denied by approver), in-transit (shipped but not yet received), received (fully received and confirmed), cancelled (voided before completion), partially-received (some items received, others pending). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|approved|rejected|in-transit|received|cancelled|partially-received — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transfer based on origin and destination: inter-unit (between restaurant units), intra-unit (between storage locations within same unit), return-to-dc (from unit back to distribution center), return-to-vendor (from unit back to supplier), emergency (urgent replenishment), rebalance (inventory optimization transfer).']
  variance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a discrepancy was identified between shipped quantities and received quantities. True if any line item shows variance requiring investigation.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause classification for any quantity variance: damage-in-transit (items damaged during shipment), short-shipment (fewer items sent than documented), overage (more items received than expected), quality-rejection (items rejected at receiving), counting-error (human error in count), none (no variance).']

  Note: 'Records the movement of inventory between restaurant units, between storage locations within a unit, or from a unit back to a distribution center — capturing both transfer header and line-level SKU detail in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: transfer date, transfer type (inter-unit/intra-unit/return-to-vendor/return-to-DC), origin and destination units/locations, transfer status, requested-by/approved-by employees, and total transfer value. Line attributes (one per SKU transferred): stock item reference, transferred quantity, unit of measure, unit cost, extended cost, lot number, expiration date, and condition code. Maintains inventory accuracy across the restaurant network and supports inter-unit food cost allocation.'
}

Table inventory.replenishment_order {
  replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the replenishment order. Primary key for the replenishment order entity.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Replenishment orders generate AP invoices upon fulfillment. Linking the replenishment order to its AP invoice enables PO-to-invoice reconciliation, accrual accounting, and supplier payment tracking — ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: LTO and promotional campaign planning directly drives replenishment orders in foodservice — supply chain teams generate campaign-specific purchase orders to stock featured items. Links procurement spe']
  catering_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.catering_order. Business justification: Pre-event inventory planning: large catering orders require advance stock purchasing. Linking replenishment_order to the catering_order that drove it enables demand-driven procurement tracking, ensure']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Associates purchase orders with cost centers to allocate procurement spend in financial reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (typically BOH manager or receiving clerk) who received and verified the replenishment order delivery at the restaurant unit.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Replenishment orders are received at a specific physical facility (receiving dock). Facility-level procurement reporting, HACCP receiving compliance, and delivery scheduling all require knowing which ']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Replenishment orders are accrued to fiscal periods for AP liability recognition and inventory cost reporting. Period-end close requires all open POs to be assigned to the correct financial period. No ']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchise agreements mandate approved supplier purchasing. Linking replenishment_order to franchisee enables franchisor audits of franchisee purchasing compliance, cost allocation by franchisee, and a']
  primary_replenishment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (manager or approver) who approved the replenishment order. Null if approval not required or still pending.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.promotion. Business justification: Specific promotions (BOGO, LTO price points) with defined start/end dates drive precise replenishment timing and quantities in QSR operations. Promotion-level procurement traceability is required for ']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: Replenishment-to-PO traceability is a standard foodservice inventory management report showing which internal reorder requests generated which supplier POs. This link closes the demand-to-supply loop.']
  quaternary_replenishment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (manager or approver) who approved the replenishment order. Null if approval not required or still pending.']
  receiving_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (typically BOH manager or receiving clerk) who received and verified the replenishment order delivery at the restaurant unit.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit placing the replenishment order. Links to the restaurant operations master data.']
  tertiary_replenishment_cancelled_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who cancelled the replenishment order. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  tertiary_replenishment_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the replenishment order. For auto-PAR orders, may reference system user account.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when the replenishment order was received at the restaurant unit. Used for supplier performance tracking and variance analysis.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the replenishment order. Orders above threshold or emergency orders may require manager or regional approval before submission.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order was approved. Used for audit trail and approval turnaround time analysis.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the replenishment order was cancelled. Captures business reason such as supplier unavailability, budget constraints, duplicate order, or inventory correction.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order was cancelled. Used for order lifecycle tracking and supplier performance analysis.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the shipping carrier or logistics provider delivering the replenishment order. May be supplier-managed or third-party carrier.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Supplier-confirmed date for delivery of the replenishment order. May differ from requested date based on supplier availability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and order lifecycle analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the replenishment order. Typically USD for US operations, but supports multi-currency for international units.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for delivery of the replenishment order. May include receiving hours, loading dock location, contact person, or handling requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order record was last updated. Tracks any changes to order details, status, or metadata.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes or comments about the replenishment order. May include special requests, context, or internal communication.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the replenishment order was created or initiated. Business event date for order placement.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique order number for tracking and reference. Format: RO-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX where YYYYMMDD is order date and XXXXXX is sequence.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'System or channel that originated the replenishment order. Identifies whether order was auto-generated by MarketMan, manually created by manager, or triggered by integrated system.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from creation through fulfillment or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|confirmed|in_transit|delivered|partially_delivered|cancelled|rejected — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the replenishment order trigger mechanism. Auto-PAR: triggered automatically when stock falls below PAR level; Manual: manager-initiated; Emergency: urgent out-of-stock order; Scheduled: routine periodic order; Spot: one-time special order.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with supplier for this replenishment order. Examples: Net 30, Net 60, COD, 2/10 Net 30. Inherited from supplier master agreement or negotiated per order.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification for the replenishment order. Urgent priority for emergency out-of-stock situations; high for items approaching stockout; normal for routine PAR replenishment.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order was physically received and logged into inventory at the restaurant unit. Triggers inventory update and AP invoice matching.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Target date by which the restaurant unit requests delivery of the replenishment order. Used for planning and scheduling.']
  shipping_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Delivery or freight charge for the replenishment order in USD. May be waived based on minimum order value or supplier agreement.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Delivery method for the replenishment order. Determines delivery speed and cost. Will-call indicates restaurant will pick up from supplier.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replenishment order was submitted to the supplier. Marks transition from draft to active order.']
  supplier_order_reference varchar [note: 'Supplier-provided order confirmation number or reference. Used for cross-referencing with supplier systems and delivery documentation.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the replenishment order in USD. Includes sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction.']
  total_amount_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final total amount payable for the replenishment order in USD, including order value, tax, and shipping fees. Used for AP invoice reconciliation.']
  total_order_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the replenishment order in USD, including all line items before tax and fees. Used for budget tracking and COGS calculation.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number provided by carrier for the replenishment order. Enables real-time delivery status monitoring.']
  variance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received replenishment order had discrepancies (quantity, quality, or item substitutions) compared to the original order. True if variance detected.']
  variance_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes describing any discrepancies found during receiving. Includes short shipments, damaged goods, wrong items, or quality issues. Used for supplier dispute resolution.']

  Note: 'Automated or manually triggered replenishment order generated when stock falls below PAR level or reorder point, capturing both order header and line-level SKU detail in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: order date, order type (auto-PAR/manual/emergency), restaurant unit reference, supplier reference, requested delivery date, order status, total order value, order source (MarketMan auto-replenishment/manager-initiated), and approval status. Line attributes (one per SKU ordered): stock item reference, ordered quantity, unit of measure, unit cost, extended cost, PAR quantity at time of order, on-hand quantity at time of order, variance-to-PAR quantity, and confirmed delivery quantity. Bridges inventory management and procurement; links to purchase orders in Coupa and SAP MM. Enables food cost forecasting and supplier fill-rate tracking.'
}

Table inventory.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory adjustment record. Primary key.']
  adjustment_approved_by_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  adjustment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or supervisor who reviewed and approved the inventory adjustment.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.corrective_action. Business justification: Inventory adjustments may result from corrective actions issued after audits; the FK records the originating action.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Adjustment entries need facility reference for GL posting and COGS allocation to the correct restaurant building.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Inventory adjustments must be posted to the correct fiscal period for accurate COGS and shrinkage reporting. Period-end close procedures require all adjustments to be period-stamped. No existing FK fr']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Adjustments affect COGS and royalty calculations; franchisee‑level tracking is required for financial reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Ensures inventory adjustments affect the correct GL account for accurate COGS and inventory valuation.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Receipt discrepancy correction is a named adjustment type in foodservice. When a quantity or quality error is discovered post-receipt, the corrective inventory_adjustment must reference the originatin']
  inspection_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.inspection_violation. Business justification: Health inspection violations (expired product, temperature abuse, improper storage) directly trigger inventory adjustments to remove non-compliant stock. Linking enables regulatory compliance traceabi']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Inventory adjustments generate GL journal entries (debit shrinkage/COGS, credit inventory). Audit trails require traceability from the operational adjustment record to the financial journal entry. phy']
  primary_inventory_adjusted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who initiated and recorded the inventory adjustment transaction.']
  primary_inventory_physical_count_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical inventory count record that triggered this adjustment, if the adjustment resulted from a count reconciliation.']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.recall_event. Business justification: Recall-driven inventory write-downs must be traceable to the recall event for FDA compliance and financial impact reporting. Foodservice operators are required to quantify recalled inventory value; li']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit where the inventory adjustment occurred.']
  reversal_adjustment_inventory_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the adjustment record that reversed this adjustment, if applicable.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Shift-level inventory shrinkage and adjustment reporting is a standard foodservice operational control. Managers review which shifts generate the most adjustments to identify training gaps or theft pa']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the stock keeping unit (SKU) being adjusted.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location (walk-in, dry storage, freezer) where the adjusted inventory is held.']
  waste_log_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.waste_log. Business justification: When a waste event is recorded in waste_log, it typically triggers a corresponding inventory_adjustment to reduce on-hand balances. Adding waste_log_id FK to inventory_adjustment links the adjustment ']
  adjusted_quantity decimal(15,4) [note: 'The quantity of inventory adjusted, expressed in the stock item\'s unit of measure. Positive values indicate increases to on-hand inventory; negative values indicate decreases.']
  adjustment_date date [note: 'The business date on which the inventory adjustment was recorded and applied to on-hand balances.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the inventory adjustment transaction. Format: ADJ-YYYYMMDD-NNNNNN.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the inventory adjustment transaction was recorded in the system.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory adjustment indicating the nature of the non-standard inventory movement (positive adjustment, negative adjustment, write-off, theft, breakage, spoilage, system correction, transfer correction, count reconciliation, damaged goods, expired product, vendor credit). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: positive adjustment|negative adjustment|write-off|theft|breakage|spoilage|system correction|transfer correction|count reconciliation|damaged goods|expired product|vendor credit — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the inventory adjustment, calculated as adjusted quantity multiplied by unit cost. Positive values increase inventory asset value; negative values decrease it.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the inventory adjustment (pending, approved, rejected, auto-approved).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the manager approved the inventory adjustment.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number of the stock item being adjusted, used for traceability and food safety compliance under Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center code associated with the restaurant unit or department responsible for the inventory adjustment, used for Profit and Loss (P&L) allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the adjustment value (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, GBP).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-by date of the stock item being adjusted, relevant for adjustments due to expired product removal.']
  impacts_cogs boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory adjustment impacts the restaurant unit\'s Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) percentage calculation.']
  is_reversed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory adjustment has been reversed by a subsequent correcting adjustment.']
  is_shrinkage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment represents inventory shrinkage (unexplained loss, theft, or discrepancy between physical count and system records).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory adjustment record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments providing context, details, or special instructions related to the inventory adjustment.']
  on_hand_quantity_after decimal(15,4) [note: 'The on-hand inventory quantity for this stock item at this location immediately after the adjustment was applied.']
  on_hand_quantity_before decimal(15,4) [note: 'The on-hand inventory quantity for this stock item at this location immediately before the adjustment was applied.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific business reason for the adjustment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: THEFT|DAMAGE|SPOIL|EXPIRE|RECOUNT|SYSCORR|XFERERR|VNDCRED|BREAK|SPILL|MISREC|OVERPOUR|PORTIONERR|TEMPFAIL|RECALL — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing detailed explanation of why the inventory adjustment was necessary.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment type or value threshold requires manager approval before being applied to inventory balances.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for supporting documentation (e.g., incident report number, physical count sheet ID, vendor credit memo number, manager approval form) that justifies the adjustment.']
  temperature_at_adjustment_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'The recorded storage temperature in Fahrenheit at the time of adjustment, relevant for temperature-related spoilage or HACCP compliance documentation.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'The cost per unit of the stock item at the time of adjustment, used to calculate the total adjustment value.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the adjusted quantity is expressed (each, case, pound, kilogram, ounce, liter, gallon, box, bag, pallet). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pound|kilogram|ounce|liter|gallon|box|bag|pallet — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waste_category varchar [note: 'Classification of waste type for adjustments related to inventory loss, used for waste percentage (Waste%) tracking and analysis (operational waste, spoilage, theft, damage, other).']

  Note: 'Records manual adjustments to on-hand inventory balances outside of standard receiving, waste, or count workflows. Captures adjustment date, adjustment type (positive adjustment, negative adjustment, write-off, theft, breakage, system correction), stock item reference, storage location, adjusted quantity, adjustment value ($), reason code, supporting document reference, adjusted-by employee, and manager approval. Provides a complete audit trail for all non-standard inventory movements and supports shrinkage analysis.'
}

Table inventory.food_cost_period {
  food_cost_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the food cost period record. Primary key for this entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.agreement. Business justification: Franchise agreements define contractual COGS% benchmarks and royalty obligations. Linking food_cost_period to the governing agreement enables franchisor compliance reporting — verifying actual food co']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Food‑cost period calculations consider campaign impact on COGS; linking provides the necessary data for cost‑impact reports.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links periodic food‑cost calculations to cost centers for departmental profitability reporting.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Facility-level food cost reporting is a standard foodservice management report used for remodel ROI analysis, occupancy cost as % of food sales, and lease vs. owned performance benchmarking. No existi']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Food cost periods must align with canonical fiscal periods for P&L reporting and COGS recognition. Restaurant finance teams reconcile food cost percent against the financial period close. No existing ']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Food‑cost period statements are aggregated per franchisee for royalty and performance scorecards.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Food cost period close generates a GL journal entry to post COGS, inventory adjustments, and waste to the general ledger. Linking food_cost_period to journal_entry enables audit traceability from oper']
  labor_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_budget. Business justification: Prime cost reporting (food cost % + labor cost %) is the foundational P&L metric in foodservice operations. Linking food_cost_period directly to the corresponding labor_budget for the same unit and pe']
  physical_count_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.physical_count. Business justification: food_cost_period.physical_count_date is a DATE field that denotes when the physical count anchoring this period\'s food cost calculation occurred. Replacing it with a FK to physical_count.physical_coun']
  primary_food_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who closed and finalized this food cost period. Typically a restaurant manager or accounting staff member.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each restaurant unit is a profit center; food cost reporting is the primary P&L metric tracked at profit center level. food_cost_period has cost_center_id but not profit_center_id — profit center is t']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit for which this food cost period is calculated. Links to the specific restaurant location.']
  sales_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.sales_report. Business justification: Food cost percentage is calculated using sales revenue from the franchisee\'s submitted sales report. Linking food_cost_period to the specific sales_report used as the revenue denominator enables audit']
  tertiary_food_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who closed and finalized this food cost period. Typically a restaurant manager or accounting staff member.']
  unit_performance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit_performance. Business justification: Period-end food cost reconciliation: food_cost_period records (actual_food_cost, cogs_percent_actual, waste_percent) are directly reconciled against the unit_performance record for the same fiscal per']
  actual_food_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the period, calculated as: Opening Inventory + Purchases + Transfers In - Transfers Out - Closing Inventory. The real food cost incurred.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any manual adjustments applied to the food cost calculation for the period, such as corrections for inventory errors, vendor credits, or accounting reclassifications. Positive or negative value.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any manual adjustment applied to the food cost calculation. Provides audit trail and justification for period adjustments.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this food cost period was reviewed and approved by management. Indicates final sign-off on the period results.']
  beverage_sales_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total beverage sales revenue for the period. Tracked separately from food sales for category-specific cost analysis.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this food cost period was officially closed and calculations were finalized. Marks the completion of the period-close process.']
  closing_inventory_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of food inventory on hand at the end of the period, determined by physical count or perpetual inventory system. Represents the ending stock value for COGS calculation.']
  cogs_percent_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual food cost as a percentage of food sales revenue for the period. Key performance metric for restaurant profitability and cost control. Calculated as (Actual Food Cost / Food Sales Revenue) * 100.']
  cogs_percent_theoretical decimal(5,2) [note: 'The theoretical food cost as a percentage of food sales revenue for the period. Represents the ideal COGS% if all operational standards were met. Calculated as (Theoretical Food Cost / Food Sales Revenue) * 100.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'The method used to determine closing inventory value: physical (manual count), perpetual (system-tracked), or hybrid (system with periodic physical verification).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this food cost period record was first created in the system. Marks the initiation of the period tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for international restaurant chains.']
  food_sales_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total food sales revenue (excluding beverage) for the period. Used as the denominator in COGS% calculations. Sourced from Point of Sale (POS) system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this food cost period record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this food cost period, such as unusual events, operational issues, inventory discrepancies, or explanations for variance. Used for management review and audit purposes.']
  opening_inventory_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of food inventory on hand at the beginning of the period. Represents the starting stock value for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the accounting period for which food costs are being calculated. Marks the close of the cost accumulation window.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'The accounting period identifier in format YYYY-PNN (e.g., 2024-P01 for period 1 of 2024). Used for financial reporting alignment.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the accounting period for which food costs are being calculated. Marks the beginning of the cost accumulation window.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the food cost period: open (still accepting transactions), closed (period ended, calculations complete), locked (finalized, no further changes allowed), or under_review (being audited or reviewed).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'The frequency type of the accounting period for food cost calculation (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, or quarterly).']
  purchases_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of all food and beverage purchases received during the period. Includes all vendor invoices posted to inventory during the period.']
  theoretical_food_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The calculated ideal food cost based on recipe costing and actual sales mix (PMIX). Represents what food cost should have been if all recipes were followed perfectly with no waste or theft.']
  total_sales_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The combined food and beverage sales revenue for the period. Represents total top-line revenue for the restaurant unit during the period.']
  transfers_in_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of inventory transferred into this restaurant unit from other locations during the period. Increases available inventory.']
  transfers_out_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of inventory transferred out of this restaurant unit to other locations during the period. Decreases available inventory.']
  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The dollar difference between actual food cost and theoretical food cost (Actual - Theoretical). Positive variance indicates higher-than-expected costs due to waste, theft, portioning errors, or recipe non-compliance.']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The variance expressed as a percentage of theoretical food cost. Calculated as (Variance Amount / Theoretical Food Cost) * 100. Used to assess the magnitude of cost control issues.']
  waste_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The waste value as a percentage of total food usage (Waste Value / (Actual Food Cost + Waste Value)) * 100. Key metric for food quality control and operational efficiency.']
  waste_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of food waste recorded during the period, including spoilage, expiration, preparation errors, and quality control discards. Tracked for Waste% calculation and food cost control.']

  Note: 'Period-level food cost summary record for each restaurant unit, capturing the calculated COGS% for a defined accounting period (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly). Captures period start date, period end date, opening inventory value ($), purchases value ($), transfers-in value ($), transfers-out value ($), waste value ($), closing inventory value ($), theoretical food cost ($), actual food cost ($), COGS% actual, COGS% theoretical, variance ($), and variance percentage. The operational food cost control record — NOT an analytics aggregate but the posted period-close record used by restaurant managers and finance.'
}

Table inventory.uom {
  uom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the unit of measure. Primary key.']
  base_uom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the base UOM for this measurement type. Used to establish conversion chains. Null if this UOM is itself the base unit.']
  abbreviation varchar [note: 'Short form or symbol for the UOM (e.g., lb, oz, gal, kg, g, ml). Used in compact displays, labels, and Kitchen Display System (KDS) screens.']
  allows_fractional_quantities boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fractional quantities are permitted for this UOM (e.g., 2.5 pounds allowed, but 2.5 cases may not be). Enforces business rules in inventory transactions and recipe scaling.']
  allows_temperature_tracking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items measured in this UOM require Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) temperature monitoring during storage and transfer. Supports food safety compliance per Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations.']
  applicable_item_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) categories or commodity types where this UOM is applicable (e.g., dry goods, produce, dairy, beverages, proteins). Supports context-aware UOM selection in ordering and recipe management.']
  conversion_factor_to_base decimal(18,6) [note: 'Multiplier to convert this UOM to the base UOM (e.g., 1 pound = 453.592 grams, so conversion_factor_to_base = 453.592). Critical for accurate Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) calculation and yield management across ordering, storage, and recipe contexts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this UOM record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage tracking per Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) requirements.']
  default_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Default shelf life in days for items measured in this UOM. Used as a fallback when Stock Keeping Unit (SKU)-specific shelf life is not defined. Supports waste tracking (Waste%) and First In First Out (FIFO) rotation.']
  display_sequence varchar [note: 'Sort order for displaying UOMs in user interfaces and dropdown lists. Lower numbers appear first. Supports user experience optimization in Point of Sale (POS) and inventory management systems.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this UOM was retired or deprecated. Null for currently active UOMs. Supports historical reporting and audit trails for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this UOM became available for use in inventory transactions. Supports temporal validity and historical analysis of UOM changes.']
  is_base_uom boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UOM is the base unit for its type (e.g., gram for weight, milliliter for volume). Base UOMs have a conversion factor of 1.0 and serve as the reference for all conversions within their type.']
  is_system_standard boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UOM is a system-defined standard (e.g., gram, liter, each) that cannot be modified or deleted by users. Protects core reference data integrity.']
  iso_code varchar [note: 'ISO 80000 standard code for the unit of measure. Supports international operations and regulatory compliance across multiple jurisdictions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this UOM record was last updated. Supports change tracking and data governance for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) accuracy and regulatory compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, usage guidelines, or special handling instructions for this UOM. Supports operational documentation and training materials.']
  ordering_uom_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UOM is used in purchase orders and vendor ordering (e.g., case, pallet). Supports procurement workflows and vendor catalog integration.']
  plural_name varchar [note: 'Plural form of the UOM name (e.g., Pounds, Ounces, Cases). Supports grammatically correct reporting and user interface text generation.']
  precision_decimal_places varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places to use when displaying or calculating quantities in this UOM. Ensures consistent rounding and precision across inventory transactions.']
  recipe_uom_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UOM is used in recipe Bill of Materials (BOM) and menu item costing (e.g., ounce, gram, tablespoon). Critical for accurate recipe yield management and menu engineering.']
  requires_lot_tracking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items measured in this UOM require lot number and expiration date tracking for traceability. Supports food safety recalls and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.']
  storage_uom_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UOM is used for Back of House (BOH) inventory storage and stock tracking (e.g., each, bag, box). Supports Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) level management and cycle counts.']
  symbol varchar [note: 'Standard international symbol for the UOM (e.g., °F, °C, %, #). Used in scientific and regulatory contexts, particularly for Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) temperature monitoring.']
  un_cefact_code varchar [note: 'Standardized UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20 code for the unit of measure. Supports Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) integration with suppliers and third-party logistics providers.']
  uom_category varchar [note: 'Measurement system category (metric, imperial, count-based, or custom). Supports multi-region operations with different measurement standards.']
  uom_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the unit of measure (e.g., EA, CS, LB, OZ, GAL, KG, G, ML, L). Used as the business identifier in inventory transactions and recipe management.']
  uom_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the unit of measure (e.g., Each, Case, Pound, Ounce, Gallon, Kilogram, Gram, Milliliter, Liter). Human-readable label for reporting and user interfaces.']
  uom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the UOM. Active UOMs are available for use in transactions; deprecated UOMs are retained for historical data but not available for new transactions.']
  uom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the unit of measure by dimension type. Determines conversion logic and applicability to different inventory contexts (receiving, storage, recipe). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weight|volume|count|length|area|temperature|time — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Reference master for all units of measure used in inventory transactions — ordering UOM, storage UOM, and recipe UOM — plus their conversion factors. Captures UOM code, UOM name, UOM type (weight/volume/count/length), base UOM flag, and applicable item categories. Conversion detail: from-UOM, to-UOM, stock-item-specific overrides (item-specific conversions override global defaults), conversion factor, effective date, and source (vendor spec/lab measurement/standard). Supports accurate translation between ordering (case of 6), storage (each), and recipe (ounce/gram) contexts — critical for inventory valuation and COGS% calculation across all restaurant units.'
}

Table inventory.vendor_item {
  vendor_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor item mapping record. Primary key.']
  expense_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maps vendor items to expense GL accounts for automated AP posting and cost tracking.']
  managed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Vendor item master management is a named procurement process: a purchasing/category manager employee owns each vendor item record, negotiating pricing and preferred vendor status. Foodservice procurem']
  order_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.uom. Business justification: vendor_item.order_uom is a STRING column denoting the unit of measure used when ordering from this vendor. The uom table is the authoritative UOM reference master. Adding order_uom_id FK (prefixed \'or']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the stock item (SKU) in the inventory master that this vendor supplies.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: REQUIRED: Procurement audit needs to know which supplier contract governs each vendor‑supplied item for pricing and compliance.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Vendor item master management requires a direct supplier reference independent of contract status. Prospective or contract-free suppliers must be linkable to their catalog items for vendor qualificati']
  activation_date date [note: 'The date when this vendor item mapping became active and available for ordering.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'The date when the contract pricing becomes effective for this vendor item.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the contract pricing expires for this vendor item.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the procurement contract governing this vendor item pricing, if applicable.']
  contract_price_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit cost is based on a negotiated contract (True) or spot pricing (False).']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|CAD|EUR|GBP|MXN|AUD|JPY|CNY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where this item is produced or sourced by the vendor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|BRA|ARG|AUS|NZL|GBR|FRA|DEU|ITA|ESP|JPN|KOR|THA|VNM|IND — 18 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this vendor item mapping record was first created in the system.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'The date when this vendor item mapping was deactivated or discontinued, if applicable.']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for deactivating this vendor item mapping (e.g., vendor discontinued, better pricing found, quality issues).']
  gtin varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s GTIN/UPC/EAN barcode for this item, used for receiving and inventory tracking.']
  last_cost_update_date date [note: 'The date when the unit cost for this vendor item was last updated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this vendor item mapping record was most recently updated.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'The most recent date when this item was ordered from this vendor.']
  last_received_date date [note: 'The most recent date when this item was received from this vendor.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between order placement and expected delivery from this vendor for this item.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the actual manufacturer of this item, which may differ from the vendor/distributor.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer\'s unique part or item number for this product.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum quantity (in order UOM) that must be ordered from this vendor for this item.']
  next_cost_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next cost review or renegotiation with this vendor for this item.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information, special handling instructions, or vendor-specific requirements for this item.']
  on_time_delivery_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of orders for this vendor item that were delivered within the promised lead time window.']
  order_increment decimal(10,2) [note: 'The quantity increment (in order UOM) in which this item must be ordered (e.g., must order in multiples of 5).']
  pack_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of individual packs or units contained in one order UOM (e.g., 12 cans per case, 4 bags per box).']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'The size or weight of each individual pack or unit within the order UOM (e.g., \'5 LB\', \'12 OZ\', \'1 GAL\').']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred supplier for this stock item (True) or an alternate source (False).']
  quality_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Quality score for this vendor\'s supply of this item, typically on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00, based on receiving inspections and quality audits.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'The current cost per order UOM from this vendor. Used for purchase order generation and COGS calculation.']
  vendor_brand_name varchar [note: 'The brand name under which the vendor supplies this item, if applicable.']
  vendor_item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the item from the vendor\'s catalog, including specifications, grade, or quality attributes.']
  vendor_item_name varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s product name or description for this item as it appears in their catalog.']
  vendor_item_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this vendor item mapping in the system lifecycle.']
  vendor_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of this vendor for this item when multiple vendors supply the same SKU (1=first choice, 2=second choice, etc.).']
  vendor_product_category varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s classification or category for this item in their catalog structure.']
  vendor_sku varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s unique item code or SKU for this product. Used in purchase orders and receiving documents.']

  Note: 'Maps each stock item to the supplier(s) that provide it, capturing the supplier-specific item details used in ordering and receiving. Captures supplier reference, stock item reference, supplier item code (vendor SKU), supplier item description, order UOM, pack size, pack quantity, current unit cost, contract price flag, lead time days, minimum order quantity, preferred vendor flag, and active status. Enables multi-vendor sourcing for critical items and supports cost comparison and replenishment order generation. Distinct from the procurement domain\'s supplier master — this is the inventory-domain view of what each vendor supplies.'
}

Table inventory.physical_count_line {
  physical_count_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the physical_count_line association']
  physical_count_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this count line to its parent physical count event header.']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this count line to the specific SKU being counted.']
  count_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used by the counting team for this SKU during this count event (e.g., each, case, lb). May differ from the stock item\'s standard UOM and is specific to this count line.']
  counted_quantity decimal [note: 'The physical quantity of this SKU observed and recorded by the counting team during the count event. Belongs to the line, not the header or the stock item master.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration or use-by date of the lot being counted. Captured at count time to support FIFO rotation compliance and waste tracking. Belongs to the count line.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The supplier or internal lot identifier for the batch of this SKU being counted. Required for lot-level traceability and HACCP compliance. Belongs to the count line, not the stock item master.']
  recount_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific SKU line requires a recount due to excessive variance or counting error. This is a per-SKU-per-count flag that belongs to the line, not the count header.']
  system_quantity decimal [note: 'The inventory system\'s expected on-hand quantity for this SKU at the time of the count event. Captured at count time and belongs to this specific count line, not the stock item master.']
  variance_quantity decimal [note: 'The difference between counted_quantity and system_quantity for this SKU in this count event. Positive indicates overage; negative indicates shortage.']
  variance_value decimal [note: 'The dollar value of the variance for this SKU line, calculated as variance_quantity multiplied by the unit cost at count time. Feeds period-end COGS% and GL journal entry.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the line-level Event between physical_count and stock_item in restaurant inventory operations. It captures the per-SKU count result for each physical inventory count event, including the counted quantity, system (book) quantity, variance, lot and expiration data, and recount disposition. Each record links one physical_count event to one stock_item and carries the operational data required for COGS% analysis, MarketMan reconciliation, variance investigation workflows, and period-end food cost reporting. Without this entity, the model cannot represent per-SKU variance, recount flags, or lot-level count data.. Existence Justification: A physical inventory count in foodservice operations produces one count line per SKU per storage location — a physical_count event covers many stock_items, and each stock_item appears across many physical_count events over time. This line-level detail (counted_quantity, system_quantity, variance_quantity, variance_value, recount_required_flag) is the operational record that drives COGS% reporting, MarketMan reconciliation, and variance investigation workflows. The relationship is universally recognized in foodservice inventory management as a \'physical_count_line\' or \'count_detail\' — a first-class operational entity that humans create, review, and act upon.'
}

Table restaurant.unit {
  unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the restaurant unit. Primary key for the unit master record.']
  area_management_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.area_management. Business justification: Every restaurant unit belongs to an area/district management hierarchy — this is a fundamental operational relationship in foodservice. The area_management product defines the geographic and operation']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Unit should reference its brand via a foreign key rather than a free‑text code; brand_id provides referential integrity.']
  brand_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand_standard. Business justification: Units must be linked to the applicable brand standard for compliance tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting reports allocate expenses per unit via a cost center; linking unit to cost_center enables accurate budgeting and variance analysis.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.department. Business justification: Linking a unit to its department enables organizational reporting and eliminates the department table silo.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Facility‑to‑Unit link required for building‑level maintenance, energy‑rating compliance, and health‑inspection tracking.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchisee entity that owns and operates this unit. Null for company-owned units.']
  landlord_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.landlord. Business justification: Landlord‑to‑Unit link supports landlord contact management, rent negotiations, and compliance audits; each unit has a responsible landlord.']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.lease. Business justification: Lease‑to‑Unit association needed for lease‑expiry alerts, rent schedule generation, and financial reporting; each unit operates under a specific lease.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Required for consolidated financial statements and tax compliance; each restaurant unit must be mapped to its legal entity for reporting.']
  primary_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_center. Business justification: DC-to-unit routing assignment: supply chain operations require knowing each unit\'s primary distribution center for inbound shipment routing, emergency replenishment planning, and freight cost allocati']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Program Assignment per Restaurant Unit: needed for reporting which loyalty program each unit runs, enabling rollout planning and performance tracking.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Site‑to‑Unit link required for property‑level rent, CAPEX, and compliance reporting; experts expect each restaurant unit to be tied to its real‑estate site.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the restaurant unit including street number and name.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite number, building name, or floor.']
  average_cover_count varchar [note: 'Average number of guests (covers) served per day. Used for labor planning and revenue forecasting in table-service restaurants.']
  average_ticket_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Average time in seconds for a customer order to be completed from entry to fulfillment, tracked via KDS (Kitchen Display System).']
  average_unit_volume_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average annual sales revenue for this unit in US dollars. Key performance metric for franchise valuation and benchmarking.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the restaurant unit is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date this restaurant unit permanently ceased operations. Null for active units.']
  concept_type varchar [note: 'The restaurant format classification. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) for fast food, casual dining for table service, fast casual for counter service with higher quality, fine dining for upscale full service, food court for mall locations, ghost kitchen for delivery-only.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the restaurant unit is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit record was first created in the system.']
  daypart_schedule varchar [note: 'Structured schedule defining daypart time windows (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late night) for menu and pricing management.']
  drive_thru_lanes varchar [note: 'Number of drive-thru lanes available at this unit. Zero indicates no drive-thru capability.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the restaurant unit used for operational communications.']
  haccp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this unit maintains current HACCP certification for food safety management.']
  has_online_ordering boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this unit supports online ordering through web or mobile app channels.']
  has_third_party_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this unit is enabled for third-party delivery services such as DoorDash, Uber Eats, or Grubhub.']
  health_inspection_score varchar [note: 'Most recent health inspection score from local health department. Scale and passing thresholds vary by jurisdiction.']
  kds_station_count varchar [note: 'Number of KDS stations installed in the BOH for order routing and kitchen workflow management.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent health and safety inspection by local regulatory authority.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit record was most recently updated.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the restaurant unit in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the restaurant unit in decimal degrees.']
  opening_date date [note: 'The date this restaurant unit first opened for business to customers.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the unit, typically formatted as day ranges with time windows (e.g., Mon-Fri 6:00-22:00, Sat-Sun 7:00-23:00).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the restaurant unit. Active indicates normal operations, temporarily closed for renovations or seasonal closure, permanently closed for shut down units, under construction for new builds, pending opening for units awaiting launch.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'The operating model for this unit: company-owned (corporate operated), franchised (independent franchisee), joint venture (shared ownership), or licensed (brand licensing agreement).']
  parking_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of dedicated parking spaces available for guest use at this location.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the restaurant unit.']
  pos_system_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the POS system software deployed at this unit.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the restaurant unit location.']
  same_store_sales_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Year-over-year comparable store sales growth percentage. Measures organic growth for units open at least 12 months.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest seats available in the FOH (Front of House) dining area.']
  speed_of_service_seconds varchar [note: 'Average time in seconds from order placement to order delivery. Critical QSR operational metric for customer satisfaction.']
  square_footage varchar [note: 'Total interior floor space of the restaurant unit in square feet, including FOH and BOH (Back of House) areas.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the restaurant unit is located.']
  table_turn_rate decimal(4,2) [note: 'Average number of times a table is occupied by different parties during a service period. Relevant for casual and fine-dining concepts.']
  throughput_capacity_orders_per_hour varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customer orders this unit can process per hour at peak efficiency, based on equipment and staffing design.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'The customer-facing name of the restaurant unit as displayed on signage and marketing materials.']
  unit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the restaurant unit. Externally-known unique code used across operational systems and franchise communications.']

  Note: 'Master record for every restaurant unit — company-owned and franchised. The authoritative identity of each physical location including unit number, brand, concept type (QSR/casual/fine-dining), ownership model (company-owned vs. franchised), legal entity name, trade name, opening date, closure date, current operational status, and geographic coordinates. This is the operational anchor for the entire restaurant domain and the primary FK target for all cross-domain joins (order, inventory, workforce, finance, franchise). One row per physical restaurant location. All other restaurant domain products reference this entity.'
}

Business justification: Each restaurant location falls within a franchise territory for royalty allocation, territory compliance, and development rights management. Franchise operations teams need to know which territory a l']

  parking_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of parking spaces available at or near the restaurant location. Influences site selection and accessibility.']

  restaurant_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the restaurant unit. Externally-known code used in operations, reporting, and franchise communications.']
