  site_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the restaurant site. Used for reporting, site portfolio management, and NRO tracking.']
  square_footage varchar [note: 'Total interior square footage of the restaurant building. Used for real estate valuation, CapEx planning, and operational benchmarking.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region code. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the restaurant location. Used for daypart scheduling, operating hours, and time-based reporting.']
  trade_area_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the trade area type where the restaurant is located. Influences site selection, menu offerings, and operational strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: urban|suburban|rural|highway|airport|mall|downtown|neighborhood — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Physical and geographic attributes of each restaurant unit including full street address, city, state, province, postal code, country, DMA (Designated Market Area), trade area classification, latitude/longitude, timezone, locale, accessibility features, parking capacity, drive-thru lane count, patio seating availability, and proximity to key landmarks. Supports site analytics, delivery radius configuration, and regional reporting.'
}

Table restaurant.format_config {
  format_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the restaurant format configuration record. Primary key.']
  brand_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand_standard. Business justification: format_config already carries brand_standard_compliance_status, which is an execution-specific compliance assessment against a brand standard. Adding brand_standard_id FK directly to format_config all']
  primary_format_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit to which this format configuration applies.']
  alcohol_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant format is licensed and configured to serve alcoholic beverages.']
  boh_cooking_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of cooking lines or production stations in the Back of House (BOH) kitchen.']
  boh_kitchen_sq_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total square footage of the Back of House (BOH) kitchen and food preparation area.']
  boh_prep_station_count varchar [note: 'Number of food preparation stations in the Back of House (BOH) kitchen.']
  brand_standard_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the format configuration against brand standards and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).']
  breakfast_daypart_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant format operates during the breakfast daypart and serves breakfast menu items.']
  config_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this format configuration, used to track changes and updates over time.']
  cover_count_capacity_per_daypart varchar [note: 'Maximum number of covers (individual meals served) the restaurant can accommodate during a single daypart (breakfast, lunch, dinner) based on seating and throughput.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this format configuration record was first created in the system.']
  curbside_pickup_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant format supports curbside pickup service for online orders.']
  dining_format_type varchar [note: 'The primary dining format classification of the restaurant unit: Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), fast-casual, casual dining, or fine-dining.']
  drive_thru_lane_config varchar [note: 'Drive-Thru (DT) lane configuration: none (no drive-thru), single lane, dual lane, or triple lane.']
  drive_thru_window_count varchar [note: 'Number of Drive-Thru (DT) service windows available for order pickup.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this format configuration became or will become effective for the restaurant unit.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this format configuration expires or is superseded by a new configuration. Null indicates current active configuration.']
  foh_bar_seating_count varchar [note: 'Number of bar seats available in the Front of House (FOH) area, if applicable.']
  foh_outdoor_seating_count varchar [note: 'Number of outdoor patio or sidewalk seats available in the Front of House (FOH) area.']
  foh_seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of guest seats available in the Front of House (FOH) dining area, including indoor and outdoor seating.']
  foh_table_count varchar [note: 'Total number of dining tables in the Front of House (FOH) area.']
  format_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for this format configuration (e.g., \'Standard QSR with DT\', \'Urban Fast-Casual\').']
  kds_screen_count varchar [note: 'Number of Kitchen Display System (KDS) screens installed in the Back of House (BOH) for order routing and production management.']
  kiosk_count varchar [note: 'Number of self-service digital ordering kiosks installed in the restaurant.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this format configuration record was last updated or modified.']
  last_remodel_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent remodel or renovation that affected the format configuration of the restaurant.']
  late_night_daypart_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant format operates during late-night hours (typically after 10 PM).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding special considerations, exceptions, or unique characteristics of this format configuration.']
  olo_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant format supports Online Ordering (OLO) through web or mobile app channels.']
  parking_space_count varchar [note: 'Number of parking spaces available for guest use at the restaurant location.']
  pos_terminal_count varchar [note: 'Number of Point of Sale (POS) terminals installed for order capture and payment processing.']
  service_model varchar [note: 'Primary service delivery model: counter service, table service, drive-thru, self-service kiosk, or hybrid combination.']
  sos_benchmark_seconds varchar [note: 'Target Speed of Service (SOS) benchmark in seconds from order placement to order delivery, based on format and service model.']
  table_turn_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target table turn time in minutes for table-service formats, representing the average time from guest seating to table availability.']
  third_party_delivery_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant format supports Third-Party Delivery (3PD) integration with external delivery platforms.']
  throughput_capacity_per_hour varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customer transactions the restaurant can process per hour under optimal conditions, based on format configuration.']
  twenty_four_hour_operation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant operates 24 hours per day, 7 days per week.']

  Note: 'Defines the operational format, physical configuration, and total capacity of a restaurant unit. Includes dining format (QSR, fast-casual, casual, fine-dining), service model (counter, table, drive-thru, kiosk), FOH layout (total indoor seating capacity, outdoor/patio seating capacity, bar seating count, private dining room capacity, ADA-compliant seating count, table count, cover count), BOH layout (kitchen footprint sq ft, cooking lines, prep stations), drive-thru configuration (lane count, stacking capacity for vehicles), kiosk count, counter service positions, and maximum cover count per daypart. This is the single source of truth for all physical capacity and layout attributes of a unit — governs brand standard compliance, throughput benchmarking, labor staffing ratios, health department permit compliance, and fire marshal occupancy limits.'
}

Table restaurant.operating_hours {
  operating_hours_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the operating hours record. Primary key.']
  primary_operating_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this operating hours schedule. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.']
  primary_operating_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit to which these operating hours apply. Links to the restaurant master record.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this operating hours schedule was approved, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.']
  close_time timestamp [note: 'The time the restaurant closes for the specified day and daypart, in 24-hour HH:mm format (e.g., 22:00). May extend past midnight for late-night operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this operating hours record was first created in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'The day of the week for which these operating hours are defined. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  daypart varchar [note: 'The daypart segment (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, all-day, overnight) for which these hours apply. Used for menu availability, labor scheduling, and Same-Store Sales (SSS) period alignment.']
  daypart_end_time timestamp [note: 'The end time of the specific daypart window (e.g., breakfast ends at 11:00), in 24-hour HH:mm format. Used for menu engineering and Product Mix (PMIX) analysis.']
  daypart_start_time timestamp [note: 'The start time of the specific daypart window (e.g., breakfast starts at 06:00), in 24-hour HH:mm format. Used for menu engineering and Product Mix (PMIX) analysis.']
  delivery_window_close_time timestamp [note: 'The time the restaurant stops accepting delivery orders (Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD)), in 24-hour HH:mm format. May differ from dine-in hours.']
  delivery_window_open_time timestamp [note: 'The time the restaurant begins accepting delivery orders (Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD)), in 24-hour HH:mm format. May differ from dine-in hours.']
  drive_thru_close_time timestamp [note: 'The time the Drive-Thru (DT) lane closes, in 24-hour HH:mm format. May differ from Front of House (FOH) close time for QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) units.']
  drive_thru_open_time timestamp [note: 'The time the Drive-Thru (DT) lane opens, in 24-hour HH:mm format. May differ from Front of House (FOH) open time for QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) units.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which these operating hours expire, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Null for open-ended schedules. Used for temporary hour changes and seasonal adjustments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which these operating hours become effective, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for scheduling future hour changes and seasonal adjustments.']
  expected_cover_count varchar [note: 'The expected number of covers (individual diners served) during this daypart for casual and fine-dining establishments. Used for labor scheduling and inventory planning.']
  expected_table_turn_count decimal(5,2) [note: 'The expected number of table turns (times a table is occupied and vacated) during this daypart for casual and fine-dining establishments. Used for capacity planning and revenue forecasting.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'The name of the holiday for which this schedule override applies (e.g., Christmas, Thanksgiving, New Year\'s Day). Null if not a holiday override.']
  holiday_schedule_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether these operating hours represent a holiday schedule override. True if this is a holiday exception, False for regular schedule.']
  is_24_hour_operation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant operates 24 hours a day for the specified day. True if open continuously, False otherwise.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant is closed for the specified day and daypart. True if closed, False if open.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this operating hours record was last modified, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_order_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'The time by which the last order must be placed before the restaurant stops accepting new orders, in 24-hour HH:mm format. Used for Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD) routing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this operating hours schedule. May include reasons for schedule changes, special instructions, or operational context.']
  open_time timestamp [note: 'The time the restaurant opens for the specified day and daypart, in 24-hour HH:mm format (e.g., 06:00).']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this operating hours record. Active schedules are in effect; pending schedules are future-dated; expired schedules are past their end date; suspended schedules are temporarily disabled.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'The type of operating hours schedule. Regular for standard weekly hours, holiday for holiday overrides, seasonal for seasonal adjustments, temporary for one-time changes, special_event for event-driven hours.']
  seasonal_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether these operating hours represent a seasonal adjustment (e.g., extended summer hours, reduced winter hours). True if seasonal, False for standard schedule.']
  seasonal_period_name varchar [note: 'The name of the seasonal period for which this schedule adjustment applies (e.g., Summer, Winter, Back-to-School). Null if not a seasonal adjustment.']
  special_event_name varchar [note: 'The name of the special event for which these operating hours apply (e.g., Super Bowl Sunday, Local Festival, Grand Opening). Null if not a special event schedule.']
  target_speed_of_service_seconds varchar [note: 'The target Speed of Service (SOS) in seconds for this daypart. Represents the benchmark time from order placement to order fulfillment. Used for operational performance tracking.']
  target_ticket_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The target ticket time in seconds for this daypart. Represents the benchmark time from order entry to Kitchen Display System (KDS) completion. Used for Back of House (BOH) performance tracking.']
  throughput_capacity_per_hour varchar [note: 'The estimated number of customer transactions the restaurant can process per hour during this daypart. Used for labor scheduling and Speed of Service (SOS) benchmarking.']

  Note: 'Scheduled operating hours for each restaurant unit by day of week and daypart (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, 24hr). Captures open time, close time, daypart start/end times, holiday schedule overrides, seasonal hour adjustments, drive-thru-specific hours, delivery window hours, and last-order cutoff times. Used for order routing, labor scheduling, and SSS (Same-Store Sales) period alignment.'
}

Table restaurant.equipment_asset {
  equipment_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the equipment asset record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: equipment_asset tracks acquisition_cost, maintenance costs, and depreciation. Equipment maintenance and depreciation expenses are allocated to cost centers for restaurant-level cost accounting. No exi']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Facility-level equipment audits (health inspection, fire safety, ADA compliance) require knowing all equipment assets within a facility. In foodservice, equipment is physically tied to a facility for ']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Asset depreciation and financial reporting require each physical equipment asset to be tied to a fixed‑asset record in finance.']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Critical equipment (fryers, coolers, holding units) is governed by specific HACCP plans and CCPs. Linking equipment assets to their governing HACCP plan enables targeted corrective actions, maintenanc']
  primary_equipment_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where this equipment asset is installed.']
  acquisition_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original purchase price paid for the equipment asset in US dollars, used for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) tracking and depreciation calculations.']
  asset_condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current physical and operational condition assessment of the equipment asset based on inspection and performance criteria.']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique asset tag or barcode identifier affixed to the equipment for tracking and inventory purposes.']
  compliance_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current compliance certification expires and recertification or inspection is required.']
  compliance_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification or approval number from regulatory bodies (FDA, NSF, UL, OSHA) confirming the equipment meets safety and sanitation standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this equipment asset record was first created in the system.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to allocate the cost of the equipment asset over its useful life for financial reporting and tax purposes.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment asset was decommissioned and removed from the restaurant, marking the end of its operational lifecycle.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the equipment was disposed of at end of life, supporting asset lifecycle tracking and environmental compliance.']
  energy_rating varchar [note: 'Energy efficiency rating or certification (e.g., Energy Star rating) indicating the equipment\'s power consumption performance.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping equipment by operational domain (cooking, refrigeration, POS systems, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cooking|refrigeration|food_prep|beverage|pos_system|kitchen_display|sanitation|hvac — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the equipment asset (e.g., Fryer Station 1, POS Terminal 3, Walk-in Cooler).']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Category of equipment asset distinguishing its primary function within BOH (Back of House) or FOH (Front of House) operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fryer|grill|oven|refrigeration_unit|freezer|ice_machine|pos_terminal|kds_display|espresso_machine|beverage_dispenser|dishwasher|ventilation_hood|warming_cabinet|prep_table — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment asset was installed and commissioned at the restaurant location.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this equipment asset record was most recently updated.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Most recent date when preventive maintenance or repair service was performed on this equipment asset.']
  last_temperature_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent date and time when temperature was verified for temperature-critical equipment, supporting food safety audit trails.']
  lease_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the lease or rental agreement for this equipment expires, requiring renewal or return of the asset.']
  location_zone varchar [note: 'Physical zone within the restaurant where the equipment is installed: BOH (Back of House), FOH (Front of House), Drive-Thru, Storage, or Outdoor.']
  maintenance_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled preventive maintenance services as defined by manufacturer guidelines or internal SOPs.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the equipment asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned model number or designation for the equipment.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next preventive maintenance service visit based on manufacturer recommendations and SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) schedules.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special handling instructions, or historical context about the equipment asset.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status indicating whether the equipment is actively in use, under maintenance, or retired from service.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment is owned outright by the restaurant, leased under a capital or operating lease, or rented short-term.']
  power_consumption_watts varchar [note: 'Rated electrical power consumption of the equipment in watts under normal operating conditions, used for energy cost forecasting.']
  replacement_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated current market cost to replace this equipment asset with a comparable new unit, used for R&M (Repairs and Maintenance) budgeting and insurance valuation.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific equipment unit for warranty and service tracking.']
  service_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the active service or maintenance contract covering this equipment asset, if applicable.']
  temperature_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment maintains temperature-sensitive food products and requires continuous monitoring for HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) compliance.']
  temperature_max_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum safe operating temperature threshold in Fahrenheit for temperature-critical equipment, above which food safety is compromised.']
  temperature_min_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum safe operating temperature threshold in Fahrenheit for temperature-critical equipment, below which food safety is compromised.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected operational lifespan of the equipment asset in years, used for depreciation calculations and replacement planning.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage ends, after which repairs and parts are at the restaurant\'s expense.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage begins for this equipment asset.']

  Note: 'Inventory of all BOH and FOH equipment assets installed at each restaurant unit including all equipment types (fryer, grill, oven, KDS stations, POS terminals, refrigeration units, ice machines, espresso machines, drive-thru timers). Captures make, model, serial number, installation date, warranty expiry, last service date, next scheduled maintenance date, asset condition rating, replacement cost, software version (for digital equipment), and equipment-specific configuration attributes. Supports R&M (Repairs and Maintenance) planning, CapEx forecasting, PCI DSS compliance for payment terminals, and food safety compliance for temperature-critical equipment.'
}

Table restaurant.brand_standard {
  brand_standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the brand standard record. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Brand standards are defined and owned by a specific brand — a brand standard for a QSR brand is distinct from one for a casual dining brand. brand_standard has applicable_format and applicable_ownersh']
  owner_department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.department. Business justification: brand_standard carries an owner_department STRING column representing the department responsible for maintaining the standard (e.g., Operations, Food Safety, Marketing). The department product exists ']
  superseded_by_standard_brand_standard_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand_standard_id of the newer standard that replaces this one. Null if this standard is current and has not been superseded.']
  applicable_format varchar [note: 'Restaurant format(s) to which this brand standard applies. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) for fast food, casual for casual dining, fine_dining for upscale establishments, or combinations/all formats. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|qsr|casual|fine_dining|qsr_casual|qsr_fine_dining|casual_fine_dining — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  applicable_ownership_model varchar [note: 'Ownership model(s) to which this standard applies: company-owned units, franchised units, or all units regardless of ownership.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Expected frequency at which compliance with this standard should be audited or inspected (e.g., daily temperature logs, monthly cleanliness audits, annual safety inspections). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|ad_hoc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  brand_standard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the brand standard. Active standards are in force. Inactive standards are no longer enforced. Draft standards are under development. Under_review standards are being revised. Superseded standards have been replaced by a newer version.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external organization or authority that issues the required certification (e.g., National Restaurant Association ServSafe, OSHA, Local Health Department). Null if no certification is required.']
  certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff must obtain a formal certification to perform tasks related to this standard (e.g., ServSafe Food Handler certification for food safety standards). True if certification is mandatory, False otherwise.']
  compliance_requirement_level varchar [note: 'Enforcement level of the standard. Mandatory standards must be met for operational compliance and are subject to audit consequences. Recommended standards are best practices encouraged but not enforced. Aspirational standards represent future-state goals.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand standard record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this brand standard becomes active and enforceable. Standards are not applicable before this date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this brand standard is no longer active or enforceable. Null indicates an open-ended standard with no planned expiration.']
  governing_body_reference varchar [note: 'External regulatory or industry body that mandates or influences this standard (e.g., FDA, USDA, OSHA, NRA ServSafe, Local Health Department, ISO 22000). Null if the standard is purely internal.']
  guest_facing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this standard directly impacts the guest experience and is visible or perceptible to customers (e.g., FOH cleanliness, service speed, food presentation). True if guest-facing, False if back-of-house or internal only.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which this brand standard was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and compliance with current regulations. Used to track review cycles and ensure standards remain current.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method by which compliance with this standard is assessed: direct observation (visual inspection), checklist (structured audit form), measurement (thermometer, timer), testing (lab analysis, swab test), documentation review (log verification), or guest feedback (CSAT, NPS).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this brand standard. Ensures periodic reassessment to maintain relevance and regulatory alignment.']
  non_compliance_consequence varchar [note: 'Consequence or remediation action triggered when a unit fails to meet this standard. Ranges from warnings and corrective action plans to financial penalties, operational suspension, or franchise termination for critical violations.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level of the standard. Critical standards directly impact guest safety or regulatory compliance. High standards affect brand reputation or operational efficiency. Medium and low standards are important but less urgent.']
  sop_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URI to the detailed SOP document that operationalizes this brand standard. May be a document management system ID, SharePoint link, or file path.']
  standard_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the brand standard into operational domains: food quality (taste, temperature, freshness), food safety (HACCP compliance, contamination prevention), cleanliness (sanitation, hygiene), service excellence (guest interaction, speed of service), brand presentation (signage, uniforms, decor), workplace safety (OSHA compliance, injury prevention), or equipment maintenance (preventive maintenance, calibration). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food_quality|food_safety|cleanliness|service_excellence|brand_presentation|workplace_safety|equipment_maintenance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  standard_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the brand standard, typically following a category-number pattern (e.g., FS-001 for Food Safety standard 001, CL-042 for Cleanliness standard 042).']
  standard_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the brand standard, including the specific requirement, rationale, and expected outcome. May reference specific procedures, thresholds, or quality criteria.']
  standard_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the brand standard (e.g., \'Proper Handwashing Protocol\', \'Drive-Thru Speed of Service Target\', \'Uniform and Appearance Guidelines\').']
  target_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target metric value (e.g., degrees Fahrenheit, seconds, percentage, count). Null if the standard does not have a quantitative metric.']
  target_metric_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Specific quantitative or qualitative target that defines compliance with this standard (e.g., \'Food temperature >= 165°F\', \'Speed of Service <= 90 seconds\', \'Cleanliness score >= 95%\', \'Zero critical violations\'). Null if the standard is qualitative without a numeric threshold.']
  training_module_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the training module or course that covers this brand standard. May be an LMS (Learning Management System) course ID or training document reference. Null if no formal training module exists.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal training is required for staff to understand and comply with this standard. True if training is mandatory, False otherwise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand standard record was last modified in the system.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the standard, following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0). Incremented when the standard is revised or updated.']

  Note: 'Defines the brand standards and SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) applicable to each restaurant unit by concept type and ownership model. Captures standard code, standard name, standard category (food quality, cleanliness, service, safety, brand presentation), applicable format (QSR/casual/fine-dining), compliance requirement level (mandatory/recommended), effective date, expiry date, governing body reference (NRA, FDA, OSHA), and linked SOP document reference. Operational anchor for audit and compliance workflows.'
}

Table restaurant.unit_performance {
  unit_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the unit performance record. Primary key for this periodic operational and financial performance snapshot.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: unit_performance tracks labor_cost_amount, cogs_amount, utility_expense_amount — all cost-center-level actuals. Linking to cost_center enables cost center variance analysis against budget targets (cog']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Unit performance records are period-close financial snapshots. Linking to financial_period enables SSS calculations, period-over-period variance reporting, and fiscal calendar alignment — a core requi']
  primary_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit for which this performance record is captured. Links to the restaurant master record.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Unit P&L KPIs (EBITDA, net income, operating income) in unit_performance roll up to profit centers for segment reporting and brand-level P&L consolidation. A foodservice finance controller expects uni']
  acv_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average Check Value (ACV) - average revenue per customer transaction during the performance period. Calculated as total revenue divided by transaction count.']
  adt_count varchar [note: 'Average Daily Transactions (ADT) - average number of customer transactions processed per day during the performance period.']
  atc_count varchar [note: 'Average Transaction Count (ATC) - total number of customer transactions during the performance period.']
  auc_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average Unit Volume (AUV) - total revenue generated by the restaurant unit during the performance period. Key metric for unit-level revenue performance.']
  cogs_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) - total cost of food and beverage inventory consumed during the performance period.']
  cogs_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Percentage - COGS as a percentage of total revenue. Key metric for food cost management.']
  comp_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Comparable Store Sales (Comp Sales) - revenue for the current period for units that were operational in both the current and prior comparison period.']
  comp_sales_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Variance in Comparable Store Sales between current period and prior period, expressed as an absolute amount.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this performance record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  ebitda_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA) - key profitability metric for unit-level P&L management.']
  gross_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross revenue before any deductions (discounts, promotions, refunds, voids) during the performance period.']
  labor_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total labor cost including wages, benefits, and payroll taxes for the performance period.']
  labor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Labor Cost Percentage - total labor cost as a percentage of total revenue. Key metric for workforce efficiency.']
  marketing_expense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Marketing and promotional expenses allocated to this unit during the performance period, including local store marketing and national campaign contributions.']
  net_income_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net income (profit after all expenses, taxes, interest, depreciation, and amortization) for the performance period.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net revenue after deducting discounts, promotions, refunds, and voids during the performance period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this performance period, including explanations for anomalies, special events, or operational issues.']
  operating_income_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Operating income (revenue minus operating expenses) for the performance period. Excludes interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization.']
  performance_status varchar [note: 'Status of the performance record indicating whether the data is draft, preliminary, final, revised, or archived.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance record was last updated in the system.']
  rent_expense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Rent or lease expense for the restaurant facility during the performance period. Includes base rent and Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges for leased locations.']
  rm_expense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Repairs and Maintenance (R&M) expense for equipment, facilities, and infrastructure during the performance period.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Primary source system from which this performance data was extracted. Oracle MICROS POS for transaction data, SAP S/4HANA for financial data, or Integrated for combined sources.']
  sss_growth_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Same-Store Sales (SSS) growth percentage comparing current period revenue to the same period in the prior year for units open at least 12 months. Excludes new restaurant openings.']
  total_operating_expenses_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total operating expenses including COGS, labor, utilities, rent, repairs and maintenance, and other operational costs.']
  utility_expense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total utility expenses (electricity, gas, water, waste disposal) during the performance period.']
  waste_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost of food waste (spoilage, preparation errors, expired inventory) during the performance period.']
  waste_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Food Waste Percentage - waste cost as a percentage of total COGS. Key metric for inventory and quality management.']

  Note: 'Periodic operational and financial performance record for each restaurant unit capturing AUV (Average Unit Volume), SSS (Same-Store Sales) growth %, comp sales (Comparable Store Sales) vs. prior period, ADT (Average Daily Transactions), ATC (Average Transaction Count), ACV (Average Check Value), COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage), Labor% (Labor Cost Percentage), Waste% (Food Waste Percentage), EBITDA, and net revenue. Sourced from Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA. Primary performance scorecard for unit-level P&L management.'
}

Table restaurant.capacity_config {
  capacity_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capacity configuration record. Primary key.']
  primary_capacity_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit to which this capacity configuration applies.']
  ada_compliant_seating_count varchar [note: 'Number of seating positions that meet ADA accessibility requirements for guests with disabilities.']
  avg_party_size decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of guests per dining party used for capacity planning and labor forecasting.']
  bar_seating_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats available at the bar or counter service area.']
  config_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this capacity configuration becomes effective for the restaurant unit.']
  config_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this capacity configuration ceases to be effective. Null indicates the configuration is currently active.']
  config_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special considerations, constraints, or context for this capacity configuration.']
  config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity configuration record.']
  counter_service_positions varchar [note: 'Number of staffed counter service positions available for order taking and guest interaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity configuration record was first created in the system.']
  curbside_pickup_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of designated parking spaces available for curbside pickup and online ordering (OLO) fulfillment.']
  delivery_staging_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of completed delivery orders that can be staged simultaneously in the designated pickup area for third-party delivery (3PD) drivers.']
  drive_thru_sos_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target time in seconds for drive-thru order fulfillment from order point to pickup window.']
  drive_thru_stacking_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles that can queue in the drive-thru lane before reaching the order point.']
  fire_code_occupancy_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of occupants (guests and staff) permitted in the facility as determined by local fire safety regulations.']
  health_permit_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum seating capacity approved by the local health department on the restaurant operating permit.']
  indoor_seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of indoor seats available for guest dining in the Front of House (FOH) area.']
  kiosk_count varchar [note: 'Number of self-service ordering kiosks available in the Front of House (FOH) for guest order placement.']
  kitchen_production_capacity_orders_per_hour varchar [note: 'Maximum number of orders the Back of House (BOH) kitchen can produce per hour at full operational capacity.']
  labor_staffing_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target ratio of Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) staff to seating capacity or covers, used for workforce scheduling and labor cost planning.']
  max_cover_count_breakfast varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest covers (individual diners) that can be served during the breakfast daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.']
  max_cover_count_dinner varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the dinner daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.']
  max_cover_count_late_night varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the late night daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.']
  max_cover_count_lunch varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the lunch daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.']
  outdoor_seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of outdoor or patio seats available for guest dining.']
  peak_hour_throughput_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest transactions or covers that can be processed during the busiest hour of operation.']
  private_dining_capacity varchar [note: 'Total seating capacity in private dining rooms or event spaces within the restaurant.']
  sos_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target time in seconds from order placement to order delivery, used as a key operational performance metric.']
  table_turn_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for a complete table turn cycle from guest seating to table reset for the next guest.']
  total_seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Aggregate seating capacity across all dining areas including indoor, outdoor, bar, and private dining.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity configuration record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Defines the seating and service capacity configuration for each restaurant unit including total indoor seating capacity, outdoor/patio seating capacity, bar seating count, private dining room capacity, drive-thru stacking capacity (number of vehicles), kiosk count, counter service positions, maximum cover count per daypart, and ADA-compliant seating count. Used for throughput planning, labor staffing ratios, and health department permit compliance.'
}

Table restaurant.area_management {
  area_management_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the area management record. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: area_management currently carries a \'brand\' STRING column which is a denormalized brand name. In a multi-brand foodservice operator, each area/district management structure is typically aligned to a s']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Area management holds financial targets (auv_target, cogs_percent_target, labor_percent_target, marketing_fund_contribution_percent). Areas map to cost centers for area-level budget roll-up and field ']
  parent_area_area_management_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent area in the management hierarchy. Null for top-level areas.']
  area_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the area or district. Used in operational reporting and area manager assignment.']
  area_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the area or district (e.g., \'Northeast Metro\', \'Southern California Region\').']
  area_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the area management record.']
  area_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the area management level within the organizational hierarchy.']
  auv_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target Average Unit Volume (AUV) for restaurants in this area, representing expected annual sales per unit.']
  cogs_percent_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) as a percentage of sales for restaurants in this area.']
  company_owned_unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of company-owned restaurant units within this area.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the primary country in which this area operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this area management record was first created in the system.']
  csat_target_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score for this area, typically measured on a scale of 0-100.']
  division varchar [note: 'Corporate division or business unit to which this area is assigned.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this area management configuration became effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this area management configuration expires or is superseded. Null for current configurations.']
  franchise_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this area is governed by a franchise area development agreement.']
  franchise_unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of franchised restaurant units within this area.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Broader geographic region to which this area belongs (e.g., \'West Coast\', \'Midwest\', \'Southeast\').']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the area management hierarchy, with 1 being the top level (e.g., national) and higher numbers representing more granular levels (e.g., region, district).']
  labor_percent_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for restaurants in this area.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this area management record was last updated.']
  marketing_fund_contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sales contributed to the marketing fund by units in this area.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding this area management record.']
  nps_target_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Net Promoter Score (NPS) for this area, measuring customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend.']
  nro_target_count varchar [note: 'Target number of New Restaurant Openings (NRO) planned for this area within the current fiscal year.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard royalty rate percentage applied to franchise units in this area.']
  sos_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target Speed of Service (SOS) in seconds for restaurants in this area, measuring average transaction time.']
  sss_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Same-Store Sales (SSS) growth percentage for this area, used to measure year-over-year comparable store performance.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Primary state or province for this area. May be null for multi-state areas.']
  territory_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic or operational boundaries defining this area (e.g., \'All units within 50 miles of downtown Chicago\').']
  unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of restaurant units (company-owned and franchised) within this area.']

  Note: 'Defines the operational management hierarchy above the restaurant unit level. Captures area/district/region boundaries, area manager assignment (name, employee ID, assignment effective dates), number of units in area, geographic region, division, brand, and area performance targets (AUV target, SSS target, CSAT target, Labor% target). Supports multi-unit management accountability, area manager performance reviews, regional performance rollups, and franchise territory alignment. One row per area/district assignment period.'
}

Table restaurant.unit_ownership {
  unit_ownership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each unit ownership record. Primary key for the unit ownership history table.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.agreement. Business justification: Unit ownership is governed by a specific franchise agreement defining royalty rates, term, and operating obligations. Franchise compliance and legal teams require the direct link between a unit\'s owne']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: unit_ownership records the operating legal entity for each restaurant (operating_entity_legal_name, tax_identification_number, financial_reporting_entity_code). Linking to legal_entity is required for']
  primary_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit for which this ownership record applies. Links to the restaurant master record.']
  primary_unit_employee_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the user who created this ownership record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  primary_unit_franchise_partner_franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the franchise partner master record when ownership_type is franchised, licensed, joint_venture, master_franchise, or area_development. Null for company-owned units.']
  tertiary_unit_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the user who last modified this ownership record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  area_development_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership is part of a multi-unit area development agreement. Used for franchise development tracking and compliance reporting.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this ownership arrangement with franchise agreement terms and brand standards. Used for franchise compliance reporting and risk management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  franchise_agreement_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the franchise agreement. Used for renewal planning and compliance tracking.']
  franchise_agreement_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the franchise agreement governing this ownership. May differ from ownership_effective_start_date if ownership transferred mid-agreement.']
  initial_franchise_fee_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The one-time initial franchise fee paid at the start of this ownership arrangement. Null for company-owned units. Used for revenue recognition and franchise economics analysis.']
  joint_venture_partner_name varchar [note: 'The name of the joint venture partner if ownership_type is joint_venture. Used for partnership management and financial reporting.']
  last_compliance_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent compliance audit for this ownership arrangement. Used for audit scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  marketing_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of gross sales contributed to the marketing fund under this ownership arrangement. Used for marketing fund accounting and campaign budget allocation.']
  master_franchise_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership operates under a master franchise arrangement with sub-franchising rights. Used for franchise hierarchy management and royalty flow tracking.']
  next_compliance_audit_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next compliance audit. Used for audit planning and compliance management.']
  operating_entity_legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the entity that owns and operates this restaurant unit. Used for legal compliance, tax reporting, and franchise agreement documentation.']
  operating_license_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the operating license. Used for renewal tracking and compliance management.']
  operating_license_number varchar [note: 'The business operating license number issued by local authorities for this ownership entity. Used for regulatory compliance and legal documentation.']
  ownership_effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this ownership arrangement ended or will end. Null for current active ownership. Used to close out royalty periods and finalize P&L attribution.']
  ownership_effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this ownership arrangement became effective. Used to determine royalty calculation periods and P&L attribution windows.']
  ownership_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context about this ownership arrangement, including special terms, conditions, or historical information.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of ownership stake held by this entity in joint venture arrangements. 100% for sole ownership. Used for P&L attribution and profit distribution.']
  ownership_record_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for this ownership record, used in franchise agreements and legal documentation.']
  ownership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this ownership record. Active indicates current operational ownership; pending indicates ownership transfer in progress; transferred indicates completed ownership change; terminated indicates closure or reversion; suspended indicates temporary operational hold.']
  ownership_transfer_date date [note: 'The date on which ownership was transferred from a previous owner to the current owner. Used for historical tracking and transfer analysis.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ownership model for this restaurant unit. Determines royalty calculation, P&L attribution, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  previous_owner_name varchar [note: 'The name of the previous owner if this ownership record represents a transfer or resale. Used for historical tracking and franchise relationship management.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of gross sales paid as royalty fees under this ownership arrangement. Null for company-owned units. Used for royalty calculation and financial forecasting.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'The geographic territory code assigned to this franchise ownership. Used for territory management, market planning, and franchise compliance.']
  transfer_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the franchisor approved the ownership transfer. Used for compliance tracking and transfer timeline analysis.']
  transfer_approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the franchisor representative who approved the ownership transfer. Used for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for this ownership record being created or terminated. Used for franchise compliance reporting, trend analysis, and strategic planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_franchise_award|resale|corporate_acquisition|refranchising|closure|default|voluntary_termination|expiration — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Tracks the ownership and operational control history of each restaurant unit including ownership type (company-owned, franchised, licensed, joint-venture), franchise partner reference, ownership effective start date, ownership effective end date, transfer reason (new franchise award, resale, corporate acquisition, closure), and operating entity legal name. Provides the authoritative ownership timeline for royalty calculation, P&L attribution, and franchise compliance reporting. Complements the franchise domain without duplicating franchise partner master data.'
}

  supports_chip boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can read EMV chip cards.']
  supports_contactless boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process contactless (NFC) payments.']
  supports_credit_card boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process credit card payments.']
  supports_debit_card boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process debit card payments.']

  supports_mobile_wallet boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports mobile wallet payments.']
  supports_nfc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports contactless NFC payments (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay).']
  supports_olo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process online orders placed through digital channels.']
  supports_qr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can scan QR codes for payment or ordering.']
  supports_third_party_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process orders from third-party delivery platforms.']
  terminal_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or asset tag used in inventory and reporting.']
  terminal_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for transactions processed by this terminal.']
  terminal_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name assigned to the terminal for operational identification.']
  terminal_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing terminal number or code used for operational identification and reporting.']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the POS terminal by its operational role and form factor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the terminal record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty for the POS terminal hardware expires.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Area of the restaurant where the terminal operates.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pos_terminal. Referenced by: loyalty.offer_redemption.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.payment_method_link.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.redemption.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.visit.pos_terminal_id, order.drive_thru_event.pos_terminal_id'

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each employee.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.department. Business justification: Employees are assigned to restaurant departments (FOH, BOH, management). The existing plain-text \'department\' column on employee is a denormalized representation of restaurant.department. Proper FK en']
  employee_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Primary restaurant location to which the employee is assigned.']
  employee_unit_id varchar [note: 'Primary restaurant location to which the employee is assigned.']
  employee_work_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Specific work site (e.g., kitchen, dining area) within the restaurant.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Specific work site (e.g., kitchen, dining area) within the restaurant.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchise employees are employed by the franchisee entity, not the franchisor. Linking employee to franchisee enables \'franchisee labor compliance reporting\' — compliance audits, FDD labor disclosures']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee\'s direct manager.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the employee\'s residence.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (apartment, suite, etc.).']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number used for direct deposit of payroll.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the employee\'s residence.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the employee\'s residence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the employee, used for age‑based compliance and benefits.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for employee communications.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact in case of emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee within the organization.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship (Full‑Time, Part‑Time, Contractor).']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the employee.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Concatenated first and last name for display purposes.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee started employment with the company.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official title or position held by the employee.']
  labor_percentage_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the employee\'s role.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name of the employee.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for overtime pay.']

  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the employee\'s residence.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record.']
  role_classification varchar [note: 'Primary functional area where the employee works: Back‑of‑House, Front‑of‑House, Administrative, Support.']
  salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual base salary amount before bonuses or overtime.']
  salary_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the salary amount.']
  servsafe_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee holds a valid ServSafe food safety certification.']
  servsafe_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the employee\'s ServSafe certification.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern assigned to the employee.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the employee\'s residence.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identification number (e.g., SSN, SIN).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  union_member boolean [note: 'True if the employee is a member of a labor union.']
  work_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Scheduling model applied to the employee.']

  Note: 'Master record for every restaurant employee across company-owned and franchised locations. Captures full employee lifecycle data including personal details, employment type (FTE/PTE), BOH/FOH role classification, hire date, termination date, employment status, pay grade, home restaurant assignment, Workday HCM employee ID, declared availability windows (preferred dayparts, max weekly hours, blackout dates, cross-location availability), and current benefit enrollment summary. Single source of truth for workforce identity and worker profile across the enterprise.'
}

Table workforce.shift {
  shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shift record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the shift occurs.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.schedule. Business justification: A shift is a specific scheduled work block that belongs to a weekly/period-level schedule. The schedule is the parent container (published labor plan for a restaurant location and period); individual ']
  shift_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee assigned to the shift.']
  shift_scheduled_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who scheduled the shift.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the shift occurs.']
  actual_end timestamp [note: 'Actual clock‑out timestamp recorded for the shift.']
  actual_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual worked hours for the shift (excluding breaks).']
  actual_start timestamp [note: 'Actual clock‑in timestamp recorded for the shift.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total break time in minutes allocated for the shift.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift record was created.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Part of the day the shift is scheduled for.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating soft deletion of the shift record.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost for the shift (rate multiplied by actual hours).']
  labor_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Labor cost expressed as a percentage of projected sales for the shift.']
  labor_rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the labor rate.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Pay rate per hour for the employee on this shift.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the shift.']
  on_call_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shift is an on‑call assignment.']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shift includes overtime hours.']
  scheduled_end timestamp [note: 'Planned end timestamp of the shift.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned shift duration in hours (excluding breaks).']
  scheduled_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start timestamp of the shift.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code for the shift (e.g., SHIFT20230915-001).']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the shift (derived from scheduled_start).']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift (e.g., regular, overtime, on‑call, training).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the shift schedule.']
  station varchar [note: 'Work station or area assigned to the employee (Back‑of‑House, Front‑of‑House, etc.).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shift record.']

  Note: 'Represents a scheduled work shift for an employee at a specific restaurant location, daypart (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night), and station assignment (grill, fry, drive-thru, expo, host, bar, dish). Captures planned start/end times, actual clock-in/clock-out times, assigned BOH/FOH station, shift type (regular, overtime, on-call, training), break duration, scheduling source (Planday), and swap/coverage details (original assignee, covering employee, swap request reason, swap approval status, approval timestamp) when shift reassignment occurs. Core operational record for labor deployment, station coverage planning, Speed of Service (SOS) staffing optimization, and Planday shift coverage workflows.'
}

Table workforce.schedule {
  schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each labor schedule.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pre-period labor cost control by cost center: restaurant managers and finance teams review scheduled labor cost against cost center budgets before the week begins. schedule has no cost_center_id; this']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Schedule-to-period labor cost alignment: schedules span period_start_date/period_end_date and must align to financial periods for labor cost period-close accruals and budget vs. actual reporting. Plai']
  labor_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_budget. Business justification: A published schedule is the operational execution plan for a restaurant location and period, and it must be planned against an approved labor budget. Adding labor_budget_id to schedule links the opera']
  labor_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_forecast. Business justification: Labor schedules in foodservice are built directly from labor forecasts — the projected FTE demand by daypart drives how many employees are scheduled per shift. Adding labor_forecast_id to schedule doc']
  nro_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.nro_pipeline. Business justification: New restaurant openings require pre-opening staffing schedules created before the unit is operational. Linking schedule to nro_pipeline enables \'NRO pre-opening staffing planning\' — franchise developm']
  schedule_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved the schedule.']
  schedule_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved the schedule.']
  schedule_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant unit for which the schedule is created.']
  schedule_unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant unit for which the schedule is created.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the schedule was formally approved by the manager.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Display name of the manager who approved the schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  daypart_schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Optional comments or special instructions for each daypart (e.g., event staffing, overtime alerts).']
  fte_evening decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent staff for the evening shift (e.g., 16:00‑22:00).']
  fte_midday decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent staff for the midday shift (e.g., 12:00‑16:00).']
  fte_morning decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent staff for the morning shift (e.g., 06:00‑12:00).']
  fte_night decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent staff for the night shift (e.g., 22:00‑02:00).']
  fte_total decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sum of planned FTEs across all dayparts for the schedule period.']
  labor_pct_evening decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the evening shift.']
  labor_pct_midday decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the midday shift.']
  labor_pct_morning decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the morning shift.']
  labor_pct_night decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the night shift.']
  labor_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost expressed as a percentage of projected sales for the period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the schedule.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the schedule.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable schedule code assigned by the scheduling system (e.g., SCHED‑2024‑W15).']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule: draft (editable), published (visible to staff), or locked (finalized).']
  total_scheduled_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Aggregate number of labor hours planned across all dayparts and positions for the schedule period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the schedule.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the schedule used for audit and rollback purposes.']

  Note: 'Weekly or period-level labor schedule published for a restaurant location, representing the planned staffing plan across all dayparts. Captures schedule period (start/end dates), restaurant unit, total scheduled hours, scheduled Labor%, FTE count by daypart, publication status (draft/published/locked), and the manager who approved the schedule. Links to individual shifts for granular staffing detail.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the time entry record.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.department. Business justification: Real-time labor cost allocation by department (FOH vs BOH) is a core restaurant finance and operations process. time_entry records actual hours worked; without a department FK, department-level labor ']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the work was performed.']
  payroll_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.payroll_record. Business justification: Time entries (actual clock-in/clock-out events) are the source data aggregated into period-level payroll records. Adding payroll_record_id to time_entry links each time entry to the payroll record it ']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the time entry.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scheduled shift associated with this time entry.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the work was performed.']
  approved_by_manager boolean [note: 'True when a manager has approved the time entry.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the entry was approved by a manager.']
  break_flag boolean [note: 'True if a break was taken during the shift.']
  break_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes taken for breaks during the shift.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the employee clocked in.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the employee clocked out.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the time entry record was first created.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code for the time entry.']
  job_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the employee during the shift.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of the labor for this entry (total_hours * labor_rate).']
  labor_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Hourly wage rate applicable to the employee for this entry (in local currency).']
  missed_punch_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee missed a clock‑in or clock‑out punch.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form comments or remarks about the time entry.']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'True if any overtime hours were recorded.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours worked.']
  pay_code varchar [note: 'Payroll code indicating the type of pay (regular, overtime, sick leave, etc.).']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of non‑overtime hours worked.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date‑time of the shift according to the schedule.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date‑time of the shift according to the schedule.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the time entry record.']
  time_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the time entry.']
  time_entry_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the time entry was recorded.']
  total_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of hours (including overtime and breaks) recorded for the entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the time entry.']
  work_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shift occurred.']

  Note: 'Captures actual clock-in and clock-out events for each employee per shift, sourced from Workday HCM time tracking or POS-integrated time clocks. Records regular hours, overtime hours, break time, missed punch flags, and manager approval status. Foundation for payroll processing, Labor% calculation, and compliance with FLSA/OSHA labor regulations.'
}

  primary_labor_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location for which the labor forecast is generated.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: LTO and promotional events directly drive traffic spikes requiring specific staffing plans. labor_forecast has lto_flag and promotion_flag columns confirming this dependency. Ops teams receive promoti']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level (0‑100) of the forecast output.']

  projected_throughput varchar [note: 'Estimated number of transactions/orders expected during the forecast period.']
  promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a planned promotion or marketing campaign is active during the forecast period.']
  scenario varchar [note: 'Scenario used for the forecast (e.g., baseline, optimistic, pessimistic).']
  week_number varchar [note: 'ISO week number of the forecast date.']
  year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year of the forecast date.']

  Note: 'Projected labor demand by restaurant, daypart, and week based on historical transaction volume (ADT/ATC), seasonal patterns, and planned promotions or LTOs. Captures forecasted cover count, expected throughput, recommended FTE count by BOH/FOH, projected Labor%, and the forecasting model version used. Drives Planday schedule generation and labor budget alignment.'
}

Sourced from Workday HCM payroll module. Serves as the authoritative payroll transaction record for finance integration and Labor% reporting.'
}

  certification_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the certification by the issuing body.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the certification (e.g., ServSafe Food Handler).']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of certification required for foodservice operations.']

Captures credential type, issuing body (NRA ServSafe, local health department, internal L&D), delivery method (in-person, e-learning, OJT), issue/completion date, expiration date, assessment score, trainer/facilitator, and compliance status. Critical for food safety regulatory compliance, health department inspections, scheduling eligibility validation, and employee development tracking.'
}

  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Without this FK, labor bu']

  primary_labor_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant location to which the labor budget applies.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center labor P&L planning: labor budgets feed directly into profit center P&L projections. Restaurant finance teams plan labor cost at the profit center level; labor_budget links to restaurant.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the budget was approved by finance.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget purpose (e.g., operational labor, training, benefits).']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the budget in financial systems.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the labor budget (e.g., "2024 Q1 Labor Budget").']
  budget_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the budget figures (historical data, forecasting model, or manager input).']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget record.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the budget (annual, quarterly, monthly, weekly, or daypart specific).']

  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
