  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Period within the fiscal year (e.g., "Q1", "M03").']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year for which the budget is defined.']
  fte_budget_boh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned BOH full‑time equivalent headcount.']
  fte_budget_foh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned FOH full‑time equivalent headcount.']
  fte_budget_total decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent headcount for the period (combined BOH and FOH).']
  hours_budget_boh decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned BOH labor hours.']
  hours_budget_foh decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned FOH labor hours.']
  hours_budget_total decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned total labor hours for the period.']
  labor_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected labor cost derived from forecasting models, may differ from the budgeted amount.']
  labor_cost_estimate_boh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected BOH labor spend derived from forecasting.']
  labor_cost_estimate_foh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected FOH labor spend derived from forecasting.']
  labor_dollar_budget decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned total labor spend in local currency for the period.']
  labor_percent_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned labor cost as a percentage of total sales for the period.']
  labor_percent_target_boh decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost percentage for BOH operations.']
  labor_percent_target_foh decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost percentage for FOH operations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the budget.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the budgeting period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the budgeting period.']
  scenario varchar [note: 'Forecasting scenario used to generate the budget.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the budget record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the budget record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Period-level labor budget targets by restaurant location, capturing budgeted Labor%, budgeted total labor dollars, budgeted FTE count by BOH/FOH, budgeted hours by daypart, budgeted SPLH (Sales Per Labor Hour), and the fiscal period. Serves as the financial target against which actual labor spend (from payroll_record and time_entry) and forecasted demand (from labor_forecast) are measured. Supports P&L management, AUV optimization, and manager accountability for labor cost control.'
}

Table franchise.franchisee {
  franchisee_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the franchise partner.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Royalty payment processing requires linking each franchisee to its bank account for ACH transfers; experts expect a direct bank_account_id FK.']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_center. Business justification: Assigning each franchisee to a primary distribution center supports logistics planning and delivery scheduling reports.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Franchisees operate as legal entities (LLCs, corporations) registered in the finance system. Tax reporting, FDD compliance, and intercompany reconciliation require linking the franchisee record to its']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. Business justification: Franchisee belongs to a geographic territory; linking franchisee to territory via territory_id enables proper reporting and eliminates reliance on free‑text codes.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the franchisee\'s primary business address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the franchisee\'s primary business address, if applicable.']
  annual_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Reported annual gross revenue of the franchisee.']
  average_unit_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average sales volume per unit for the franchisee.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the franchisee\'s primary business address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status with brand standards and regulatory requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the franchisee\'s primary business address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the franchisee record was first created in the system.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the franchisee by a rating agency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aaa|aa|a|bbb|bb|b|ccc|cc|c|d — promote to reference product]']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name under which the franchisee operates, if different from the legal name.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date the franchisee entity was legally formed.']
  fdd_disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Franchise Disclosure Document compliance for the franchisee.']
  food_safety_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the franchisee holds a current food safety certification (e.g., ServSafe).']
  franchise_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Initial fee paid by the franchisee for the franchise rights.']
  franchisee_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the franchisee for internal tracking.']
  franchisee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the franchisee partnership.']
  franchisee_type varchar [note: 'Legal structure of the franchisee entity.']
  ifa_membership_status varchar [note: 'International Franchise Association membership status of the franchisee.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the restaurant industry the franchisee operates in.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current insurance policy.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number for the franchisee\'s liability and property insurance.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the franchisee entity as registered with government authorities.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next franchise agreement renewal.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Total number of restaurant locations operated by the franchisee.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the franchisee\'s primary business address.']
  royalty_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of royalty fees calculated for a reporting period.']
  royalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross sales payable to the brand as royalty.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the franchisee\'s primary business address.']
  state_tax_number varchar [note: 'State-level tax identification number for the franchisee.']
  tax_id_ein varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number for the franchisee.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the franchise agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the franchisee record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each franchise partner entity — the legal business entity (individual, LLC, corporation) that holds one or more franchise agreements with the brand. Captures franchisee identity, legal structure, ownership details, contact information, financial standing, FDD disclosure status, IFA membership, and FranConnect system ID. SSOT for franchise partner identity across the enterprise.'
}

Table franchise.agreement {
  agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the franchise agreement record.']
  agreement_franchisor_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the franchisor (company) party.']
  agreement_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the franchisor (company) party.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Franchise agreements are brand-specific legal contracts. In multi-brand systems, a franchisee holds separate agreements per brand. Legal, compliance, and royalty reporting all require knowing which br']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Franchise agreements have effective periods that must align to fiscal periods for revenue recognition scheduling under ASC 606/IFRS 15. Finance requires period-tagged agreements for deferred revenue c']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the franchisee party.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Franchise agreements generate royalty revenue streams tracked in profit centers. Finance maps agreement-level royalty streams to profit centers for franchisee P&L reporting and revenue recognition. St']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Franchise agreements contractually specify which loyalty program(s) the franchisee must participate in, including marketing fee obligations and compliance requirements. The \'franchise agreement loyalt']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. Business justification: A franchise agreement is tied to a specific geographic territory; adding territory_id FK normalizes territory data and removes redundant code/description fields.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the agreement (e.g., FA-2023-001).']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the agreement as initial, renewal, transfer, or amendment.']
  amendment_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment becomes effective.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the amendment applied to the agreement.']
  average_unit_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected average sales per unit location for the franchisee.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit of the agreement.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance review.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the agreement (e.g., v1, v2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled expiration date of the agreement (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the agreement becomes legally binding.']
  ftc_compliance_attestation_flag boolean [note: 'Attestation that the agreement complies with FTC Franchise Rule disclosures.']
  initial_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Up‑front fee paid by the franchisee to obtain the franchise rights.']
  marketing_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sales contributed to the brand‑wide marketing fund.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments, special conditions, or observations.']
  renewal_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee payable by the franchisee to renew the agreement for the next term.']
  renewal_term_years varchar [note: 'Number of years for each renewal period after the initial term.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ongoing royalty percentage of gross sales payable to the franchisor.']
  sales_target_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target gross sales amount the franchisee commits to achieve during the term.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the agreement was signed by both parties.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the agreement was terminated prior to its scheduled end.']
  transfer_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the franchisee may transfer the agreement to another party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of each franchise agreement between the franchisor and a franchisee, encompassing the full agreement lifecycle from execution through renewal, transfer, or termination. Captures agreement type (initial, renewal, transfer, successor), effective and expiration dates, territory grant, initial franchise fee, royalty rate and basis, marketing fund contribution rate, renewal terms, and agreement status. Owns all lifecycle event records including: transfers (transferor/transferee, ROFR exercise, approval, transfer conditions, effective date), renewals (updated terms, FDD re-disclosure, renewal fee, execution date), and terminations (type, default notice, cure period, de-identification, post-termination obligations, non-compete). SSOT for all contractual franchise obligations and their complete lifecycle history. Supports FTC Franchise Rule Items 5, 6, 17 disclosure requirements.'
}

  Note: 'Defines the protected or exclusive geographic territory granted to a franchisee under a franchise agreement. Captures territory boundaries (polygon, zip codes, DMA, radius), territory type (exclusive, protected, non-exclusive), population count, trade area classification, territory status, and assignment history. Supports territory conflict resolution and development pipeline planning.'
}

  Note: 'Transactional record of all periodic franchise fees billed to a franchisee for a specific reporting period, including royalties, marketing fund contributions, technology fees, and other recurring charges. Source of truth for franchise revenue recognition, AR aging, and marketing fund governance. Supports FDD Item 6 and Item 11 disclosure compliance.'
}

  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the variance when variance_flag is true.']
  variance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reported figures deviate beyond predefined thresholds.']

  Note: 'Franchisee-submitted periodic gross sales report used as the basis for royalty and marketing fund calculation, SSS (Same-Store Sales) tracking, and operational benchmarking. Captures reporting period (weekly, monthly), reported gross sales by daypart, net sales, transaction count (ATC), average check value (ACV), submission date, submission method (POS auto-pull, manual entry, EDI), validation status, and variance flags. Supports royalty billing, comp sales analysis, franchisee performance benchmarking, and FDD Item 19 financial performance representation validation.'
}

SSOT for the franchise development and facility lifecycle in FranConnect.'

Table franchise.development_schedule {

The schedule_number, total_units_committed, and development phase are all governed by t']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Development schedules commit franchisees to open a specific number of units of a specific brand. Multi-brand franchisees have separate schedules per brand. Franchise development reporting and complian']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Development schedules drive multi-year capex budgets. Finance.budget has nro_flag for this purpose. Linking development schedules to approved budgets enables committed unit tracking against capital al']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Development schedules span fiscal periods; finance tracks committed unit openings by period for revenue forecasting and royalty pipeline projections. Foodservice franchise finance teams use period-tag']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchisee to whom the development schedule is assigned.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Development schedules are contractual commitments executed under a specific legal entity. Multi-entity franchise systems require entity-level development commitment reporting for FDD disclosure and le']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Development schedules commit franchisees to opening specific units; linking to the site being developed tracks which physical locations fulfill contractual unit-opening obligations. Franchise developm']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Geographic territory identifier governing the development schedule.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the franchisee against the development schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the development schedule record was first created in the system.']
  cure_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months granted to the franchisee to cure a schedule breach before penalties apply.']
  development_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the development schedule lifecycle.']
  development_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule (active, inactive, suspended, completed, default).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the development schedule expires if the required units are not opened.']
  last_compliance_check date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance review for the development schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, exceptions, or special conditions related to the schedule.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the development schedule, used in franchise contracts and reporting.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the development agreement (e.g., Multi-Unit Development Agreement, single-unit, renewal).']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the schedule record to track revisions.']

  target_units_year_2 varchar [note: 'Number of units the franchisee must open in the second calendar year of the schedule.']
  target_units_year_3 varchar [note: 'Number of units the franchisee must open in the third calendar year of the schedule.']
  total_units_committed varchar [note: 'Total number of restaurant units the franchisee has agreed to open under the schedule.']
  units_opened_to_date varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of units the franchisee has opened to date under this schedule.']
  units_remaining varchar [note: 'Number of units still required to meet the schedule commitment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the development schedule record.']

  Note: 'Contractual development schedule committing a franchisee to open a specified number of restaurants within defined time windows under a multi-unit development agreement (MUDA). Captures total units committed, units opened to date, units remaining, development period start and end dates, annual opening targets by year, cure period terms, and schedule compliance status. Supports franchise development pipeline forecasting and default tracking.'
}

  primary_compliance_franchise_franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the franchised restaurant unit subject to the audit.']

  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Compliance audits are conducted at a specific restaurant unit. Franchise QA reporting, corrective action tracking, and health inspection scoring all require knowing which unit was audited. A foodservi']

  Note: 'Records the results of brand standards compliance audits conducted at franchised restaurant units. Captures audit date, audit type (scheduled, unannounced, follow-up), auditor identity, overall compliance score, section scores (food safety, cleanliness, service, brand standards, equipment), critical and non-critical violation counts, corrective action required flag, and audit disposition (pass, conditional pass, fail). Supports franchise agreement compliance enforcement and performance scorecard inputs.'
}

  Note: 'Records all one-time and recurring franchise fees charged to franchisees beyond royalties, including initial franchise fees, renewal fees, transfer fees, training fees, technology fees, and marketing fund contributions. Captures fee type, fee amount, billing trigger event, payment status, waiver or discount applied, and associated agreement. Complements royalty_invoice which handles periodic royalty billing specifically.'
}

Table foodsafety.haccp_plan {
  haccp_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the HACCP plan record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the HACCP plan.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the HACCP plan.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: HACCP plans are developed for specific food production facilities and cover physical process flows within that building. Regulatory frameworks (FDA, USDA) require facility-specific HACCP documentation']
  allergen_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allergen control procedures are defined in the plan.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the HACCP plan received formal approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the HACCP plan.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the food safety manager or authority who approved the plan.']
  audit_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent HACCP audit.']
  audit_next_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next HACCP audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent HACCP audit.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current overall compliance standing of the HACCP plan.']
  corrective_action_procedure varchar [note: 'Standard steps to be taken when a CCP deviation occurs.']
  critical_control_points varchar [note: 'List of CCPs defined in the plan, stored as a delimited string.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the HACCP document in the document lifecycle.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic version of the HACCP plan.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the HACCP plan becomes effective and enforceable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the HACCP plan expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  hazard_analysis_summary varchar [note: 'Brief narrative summarizing identified hazards and risk assessments.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the HACCP plan.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the HACCP plan.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often each CCP is monitored.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of the HACCP plan.']
  non_conformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of recorded non‑conformances since the last review.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business code or identifier used to reference the HACCP plan in reports and audits.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the HACCP plan for the restaurant unit.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan based on the entity it governs (e.g., restaurant, franchise, corporate, supplier).']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the HACCP plan, typically following a major.minor scheme.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the HACCP plan record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the HACCP plan record.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory standard(s) the HACCP plan aligns with.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision count for the HACCP plan.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the plan.']
  sanitation_schedule_reference varchar [note: 'Link or code to the sanitation schedule tied to this HACCP plan.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the physical or operational scope covered by the plan.']
  temperature_log_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or path to the temperature log data associated with this plan.']
  temperature_monitoring_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if temperature monitoring is a required control in this plan.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when required training was completed.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff training is required for this plan.']

  Note: 'Master record for each restaurant units Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) plan, including plan version, scope, approval status, regulatory framework alignment (FDA FSMA, ISO 22000, Codex Alimentarius), effective and expiration dates, responsible food safety manager, team members, and prerequisite program references. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for HACCP program governance across all company-owned and franchised units. Each plan undergoes annual review and revalidation.'
}

Table foodsafety.critical_control_point {
  critical_control_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the critical control point.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Critical control points (cooking stations, cold storage, receiving docks) are defined for specific physical process locations within a facility. Facility-specific CCP mapping is required for HACCP pla']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Each Critical Control Point belongs to a single HACCP plan; adding haccp_plan_id creates the required parent link.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required: Each CCP must have a qualified employee monitor it; linking enables CCP compliance reports and corrective‑action tracking.']
  average_deviation_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average magnitude of deviations observed across monitoring events.']
  corrective_action_procedure varchar [note: 'Standardized steps to take when a deviation from the critical limit occurs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CCP record was first created in the system.']
  critical_control_point_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the CCP in SOPs and audits.']
  critical_control_point_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the critical control point.']
  critical_control_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CCP.']
  critical_limit_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the acceptable range for the control parameter (e.g., maximum temperature).']
  critical_limit_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable range for the control parameter (e.g., minimum temperature).']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of recorded deviations from the critical limits.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the CCP is retired or superseded; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the CCP became effective.']
  haccp_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the HACCP plan to which this CCP belongs.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Category of hazard the CCP is designed to control.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CCP is classified as a critical control point (true) or a control point (false).']
  last_monitored_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent monitoring observation.']
  last_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent verification activity.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the CCP is monitored.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Technique or instrument used to monitor the CCP (e.g., calibrated thermometer).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the CCP.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Operational step in the food preparation flow where the CCP applies.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory or certification reference (e.g., FDA FSMA, ISO 22000, ServSafe).']
  responsible_role varchar [note: 'Job role accountable for monitoring and maintaining the CCP.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the critical limit values.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the CCP record.']
  verification_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the CCP is independently verified for compliance.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used during verification (e.g., internal audit, third‑party audit).']

  Note: 'Defines each Critical Control Point (CCP) within a HACCP plan, including the hazard type (biological, chemical, physical), critical limits (min/max temperature, pH, time), monitoring method, corrective action procedure, and verification frequency. Each CCP is tied to a specific process step (e.g., cooking, cooling, receiving) and HACCP plan version.'
}

This is a strong indicator that each food safety audit is conducted against a spe']
  primary_food_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the auditor who performed the audit.']
  primary_food_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the audit was conducted.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Food safety audits are conducted at a specific restaurant location; associating audits with site_id supports site‑wide audit tracking and rent‑related compliance.']
  allergen_control_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with allergen control requirements.']
  attached_documents_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the audit record.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the audit, often used in reports and communications.']
  audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the audit was performed on site.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit source: internal, third‑party, or health department.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the auditor.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated compliance score based on weighted findings.']

  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority overseeing the audit.']
  sanitation_schedule_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with scheduled sanitation procedures.']
  temperature_monitoring_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates if temperature monitoring met required standards during the audit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit record.']

  Note: 'Header-and-line transactional record of each food safety audit conducted at a restaurant unit, including audit metadata (type: internal/third-party/health-department, date, auditor, overall score, pass/fail) and individual findings (category: critical/major/minor, regulatory reference, corrective action required, responsible party, due date, resolution status). Managed via Zenput. Supports QA compliance tracking, trend analysis, and regulatory reporting. Each audit contains zero-to-many findings as line items.'
}

Table foodsafety.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the audit finding record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Remediation cost allocation: corrective actions for findings are budgeted against a cost center.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Audit findings identify deficiencies in specific physical facilities. Facility managers need direct facility-level finding reports to prioritize capital repairs and track remediation. Without this FK,']
  food_safety_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.food_safety_audit. Business justification: Audit findings are recorded under a specific food safety audit; adding food_safety_audit_id links findings to their audit.']
  primary_audit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or contractor accountable for corrective action.']
  primary_audit_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where the audit was performed.']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Audit findings frequently identify specific stock items as non-compliant (expired product, improper labeling, allergen mislabeling). Linking audit_finding to stock_item enables targeted corrective act']
  audit_finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the violation or observation.']
  audit_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the finding.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Prescribed action required to remediate the finding.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finding record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the corrective action must be completed.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'High‑level domain of the finding (e.g., HACCP, sanitation).']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the finding within the audit.']
  finding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the finding was recorded during the audit.']
  has_attachment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documentation (photos, files) is attached.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Code or citation of the applicable regulation (e.g., FDA Food Code section).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was closed or resolved.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Categorical severity classification of the finding.']
  severity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) representing the seriousness of the finding.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that captured the finding (e.g., Zenput).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the finding record.']

  Note: 'Individual finding or violation recorded during a food safety audit, capturing finding category (critical, major, minor), description, regulatory reference (FDA Food Code, local health code), corrective action required, responsible party, due date, and resolution status. Each finding is linked to a specific audit and restaurant unit.'
}

Table foodsafety.health_inspection {
  health_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the health inspection record.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Health inspections generate fee payments (inspection_fee_amount already on health_inspection) processed as AP invoices. Finance must reconcile inspection fee disbursements to specific inspection event']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inspection cost accounting: health inspection fees and related expenses are charged to a cost center.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the inspector.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Health departments inspect specific physical facilities (kitchens, storage areas), not abstract site parcels. A facility_id FK enables facility-level inspection history reporting, compliance scoring p']
  primary_health_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location inspected.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Regulatory health inspection reports are filed per physical site; linking enables site‑level compliance dashboards and audit trails.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency that performed the inspection.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if supporting documents (photos, reports) are attached.']
  closure_order_date date [note: 'Date when the closure order was issued.']
  closure_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the inspection resulted in a closure order.']
  compliance_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or code applicable to the inspection.']
  corrective_action_deadline date [note: 'Date by which corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if corrective actions are required.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required corrective actions.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the inspection fee.']
  follow_up_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the follow-up inspection.']
  follow_up_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if a follow-up inspection is mandated.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the health inspection was conducted.']
  inspection_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the agency for conducting the inspection.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Official inspection number assigned by the regulatory agency.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection record.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the health inspection took place.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of the inspection based on its trigger.']
  inspector_code varchar [note: 'System identifier for the inspector.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Name of the inspector who performed the health inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations recorded by the inspector.']
  overall_grade varchar [note: 'Overall grade assigned by the inspector.']
  pass_fail varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant passed the inspection.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the health permit.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was first entered into the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection record.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification based on inspection findings.']
  score varchar [note: 'Numeric score representing compliance level (higher is better).']
  violation_summary varchar [note: 'Brief description of the violations identified.']
  violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of violations cited in the inspection.']

  Note: 'Header-and-line record of official health inspections conducted by local health departments or regulatory authorities at restaurant units, including inspection header (date, inspector, agency, type: routine/follow-up/complaint-driven, overall grade, permit status, closure orders) and individual violations as line items (violation code, severity: critical/non-critical, FDA Food Code citation, corrective action required, compliance deadline, re-inspection outcome). This is the authoritative regulatory inspection record distinct from internal food safety audits.'
}

Supports regulatory compliance tracking and trend analysis.'
}

This strongly implies a direct relations']

The foodsafety_corrective_action table has no exi']

  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item.   tertiary_corrective_verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who verified the corrective action.']

Linking foodsafety_corrective_action to the training_completion that closes the training requirement enables closu']
  action_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the corrective action for tracking and reporting.']

  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the corrective action was created or initiated.']

  foodsafety_corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corrective action.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  party_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary party (e.g., restaurant unit or franchise) responsible for the corrective action.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the corrective action for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause that triggered the corrective action.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the issue that prompted the corrective action.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective action should be completed.']
  temperature_exceedance boolean [note: 'True if the action addresses a temperature limit breach.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the corrective action record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the verification of the corrective action was performed.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action was effective.']

  Note: 'Tracks corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) initiated in response to food safety audit findings, health inspection violations, CCP deviations, temperature exceedances, pest control findings, or allergen incidents. Captures root cause analysis method (5-Why, fishbone), corrective action description, responsible manager, target and actual completion dates, verification method, effectiveness check outcome, and closure status. Serves as the central CAPA registry managed via Zenput task management, supporting FDA Food Code and ISO 22000 corrective action requirements.'
}

Table foodsafety.temperature_log {
  temperature_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each temperature reading record.']
  critical_control_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.critical_control_point. Business justification: Temperature logs are the operational monitoring records for Critical Control Points under HACCP. The critical_control_point table defines critical_limit_min, critical_limit_max, monitoring_method, and']
  primary_temperature_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who manually entered the reading, if applicable.']
  primary_temperature_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or sensor that generated the temperature reading.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the workforce shift during which the reading was taken.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location or specific area (e.g., kitchen, dock) where the reading occurred.']
  audit_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the reading to a specific food safety audit or inspection.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Optional batch identifier grouping a set of readings for a specific audit period.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the sensor was last calibrated.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next sensor calibration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance check for this reading against HACCP requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the temperature log record was initially created in the system.']
  critical_limit_high decimal(6,2) [note: 'Upper temperature threshold defined by HACCP for the monitoring point.']
  critical_limit_low decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lower temperature threshold defined by HACCP for the monitoring point.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the data quality assessment for the reading.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the temperature reading falls outside the defined critical limits.']
  maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for required maintenance activities.']
  maintenance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment requires maintenance based on the reading or schedule.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the temperature (manual probe or automated sensor).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional observations or comments about the reading.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the temperature measurement was taken at the monitoring point.']
  reading_type varchar [note: 'Category of the monitoring point where the temperature was recorded.']
  sensor_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the temperature sensor device.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the temperature data.']
  temperature_log_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the temperature log record.']
  temperature_trend varchar [note: 'Observed trend of temperature change relative to previous readings.']
  temperature_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured temperature value captured by the sensor or manual probe.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the temperature reading (Fahrenheit or Celsius).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the temperature log record.']

  Note: 'Time-series log of temperature readings captured at critical monitoring points (walk-in coolers, freezers, hot-holding units, cooking equipment, receiving docks), including equipment ID, reading timestamp, measured temperature, unit of measure (°F/°C), critical limit thresholds, deviation flag, and monitoring method (manual probe, automated sensor). Core HACCP monitoring record per Principle 4.'
}

  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius measured in the task area at the time of execution.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Technique employed to confirm task compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each completed or missed sanitation task execution at a restaurant unit, capturing task completion timestamp, employee who performed the task, actual chemical concentration measured, pass/fail status, and any deviation notes. Linked to the master sanitation schedule and managed via Zenput task management.'
}

  Note: 'Transactional record of a reported employee foodborne illness or suspected foodborne illness event at a restaurant unit, capturing report date, employee role, symptoms reported, onset date, suspected pathogen or food item, exclusion-from-work decision, return-to-work clearance date, and health department notification status. }

Table loyalty.member {
  member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program member. Primary key for the member entity.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for assigning a dedicated employee to manage high‑value members, enabling personalized service and performance reporting.']
  acquisition_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Acquisition Campaign Attribution report requires linking each new member to the marketing campaign that drove the sign‑up, enabling ROI calculation of acquisition spend.']
  current_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: member.current_tier is a denormalized STRING storing the tier name/code. Replacing it with a FK to the tier reference catalog (current_tier_id -> loyalty.tier.tier_id) normalizes this relationship and']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Supports franchisee‑level loyalty analytics by linking each member to the owning franchisee, used in franchise performance dashboards.']
  member_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the guest domain master record. Links loyalty membership to the guest profile system of record.']
  member_preferred_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location that the member visits most frequently or has designated as their preferred location. Used for personalized offers and location-based campaigns.']
  member_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the guest domain master record. Links loyalty membership to the guest profile system of record.']
  member_unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the member enrolled, if enrollment occurred at a physical location. Null for digital enrollments.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Every loyalty member is enrolled in a specific loyalty program. The program governs the member\'s points rules, tier structure, earning rates, and redemption thresholds. Adding program_id to member est']
  referred_by_member_id varchar [note: 'Member ID of the existing member who referred this member to the loyalty program. Null if member enrolled without a referral.']
  account_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the loyalty membership was closed or terminated. Null for active memberships. Used for retention analysis and right-to-be-forgotten compliance.']
  account_closure_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for account closure. Examples: member request, fraud, inactivity, duplicate account, policy violation, data privacy request.']
  account_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty member record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  account_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty member record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and change tracking.']
  badges_earned varchar [note: 'Total number of achievement badges earned by the member through gamification activities, challenges, and milestones.']
  birthday_month varchar [note: 'Month of the member\'s birthday (1-12). Stored without year or day for privacy. Used for birthday reward campaigns and personalized offers.']
  current_points_balance varchar [note: 'Current available points balance that the member can redeem. Calculated as lifetime earned minus lifetime redeemed minus expired points.']
  data_privacy_consent_date date [note: 'Date when the member provided explicit consent for data collection, processing, and storage under applicable privacy regulations.']
  direct_mail_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive physical direct mail offers and communications. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional emails. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. OLO (Online Ordering), POS (Point of Sale), mobile app, website, in-restaurant kiosk, or call center.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the start of the member lifecycle.']
  gamification_level varchar [note: 'Current level in the loyalty program gamification system. Members progress through levels by completing challenges, earning badges, and accumulating points.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent loyalty program activity by the member, including points earned, points redeemed, or profile updates. Used for churn prediction and reactivation campaigns.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase transaction where the member earned or redeemed points. Subset of last_activity_date focused on revenue-generating activity.']
  lifetime_points_earned varchar [note: 'Total cumulative loyalty points earned by the member since enrollment. Never decreases; used for tier qualification and lifetime value analysis.']
  lifetime_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total cumulative loyalty points redeemed by the member for rewards, discounts, or offers since enrollment.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Externally-facing unique membership identifier displayed on loyalty cards, mobile app, and customer communications. Human-readable alphanumeric code.']
  next_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the next batch of points will expire. Null if no points are scheduled to expire.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score provided by the member, ranging from 0 (detractor) to 10 (promoter). Measures member satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent NPS survey was completed by the member.']
  points_expiring_soon varchar [note: 'Number of points scheduled to expire within the next 30 days. Used for proactive member engagement and retention campaigns.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred language for communications and app interface. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loyalty membership. Active: member in good standing. Suspended: temporarily blocked due to policy violation. Churned: member opted out or account closed. Inactive: no activity for extended period. Pending: enrollment initiated but not completed.']
  push_notification_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive push notifications through the mobile app. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Unique referral code assigned to this member for member-get-member campaigns. When shared and used by new enrollees, both parties receive bonus points.']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional SMS text messages. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or explanation for the current program status. Examples: fraud detection, member request, inactivity, policy violation, system migration.']
  terms_accepted_date date [note: 'Date when the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions. Required for legal compliance and dispute resolution.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the loyalty program terms and conditions that the member accepted. Format: v1.0, v2.1, etc.']
  third_party_sharing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for marketing purposes. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  tier_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the member achieved or was assigned their current tier level. Used for tier anniversary calculations and renewal cycles.']
  tier_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current tier status expires and member will be re-evaluated for tier qualification. Null for lifetime tiers.']
  total_visits varchar [note: 'Total number of restaurant visits or orders placed by the member since enrollment. Includes dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, and 3PD orders where loyalty was applied.']

  Note: 'Master record for every enrolled loyalty program member. Captures member identity linkage (guest_id FK to guest domain), enrollment channel (OLO, POS, in-app, kiosk, staff-assisted), enrollment date, current tier assignment, lifetime points earned/redeemed, opt-in preferences, program assignment, auto-accrue payment linkage flag, and program status (active, suspended, churned). This is the SSOT for loyalty membership identity — distinct from the guest profile master in the guest domain.'
}

Table loyalty.tier {
  tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty membership tier. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Tier structures (Gold/Silver/Bronze) are brand-specific in multi-brand operators. Brand managers configure and audit tier ladders per brand. Tier eligibility reporting and brand-level loyalty analytic']
  modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tier configuration change management requires tracking which employee modified tier thresholds, multipliers, or qualification criteria — essential for loyalty program governance and compliance audits.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Loyalty tiers (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) are defined within the context of a specific loyalty program. A tier belongs to one program. Adding program_id to tier establishes this parent-child rela']
  annual_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual membership fee charged to maintain this tier status, if applicable. Null or zero indicates no fee. Used for premium tier monetization.']
  benefits_summary varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured text summary of key benefits and perks associated with this tier (e.g., free delivery, birthday rewards, exclusive offers, priority support). Used for member communications and benefit entitlement logic.']
  birthday_reward_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier receive a special birthday reward (e.g., free item, bonus points, discount). True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  color_code varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code used for tier branding in digital channels (e.g., #CD7F32 for bronze, #C0C0C0 for silver, #FFD700 for gold). Used in mobile app, website, and marketing materials.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the loyalty system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  downgrade_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum threshold value required to maintain this tier status during re-evaluation period. Falling below this value triggers downgrade to lower tier. Null indicates no downgrade policy.']
  early_access_lto boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier receive early access to new Limited Time Offers (LTO) before general availability. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this tier configuration was retired or replaced. Null indicates the tier is currently active. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this tier configuration became active and available for member assignment. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  exclusive_offers_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier receive access to exclusive promotional offers and Limited Time Offers (LTO) not available to lower tiers. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  free_delivery_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier are eligible for free delivery on Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD) orders. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after tier expiration during which members retain tier benefits before downgrade. Used to provide buffer period for re-qualification.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the tier badge or icon image asset used in member portal, mobile app, and digital communications. Supports tier visual identity and gamification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tier record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when this tier was officially launched to the market and communicated to guests. May differ from effective_start_date for soft launches or pilot programs.']
  max_redemption_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage discount that can be applied when redeeming points for rewards in this tier (e.g., 20.00 for 20% max discount). Used to control reward economics and prevent abuse.']
  points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base points accrual for members in this tier (e.g., 1.0 for base, 1.5 for 50% bonus, 2.0 for double points). Used to calculate accelerated points earning.']
  priority_support_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier receive priority customer support (e.g., dedicated phone line, faster response times). True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  qualification_metric varchar [note: 'The metric used to determine tier qualification (points accrued, visit count, total spend amount, or transaction count). Defines how qualification_threshold is interpreted.']
  qualification_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days over which the qualification threshold must be met (e.g., 365 for annual qualification, 90 for quarterly). Null indicates lifetime accumulation.']
  qualification_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value required to qualify for this tier (e.g., 1000 points, 25 visits, $500 spend). Interpretation depends on qualification_metric.']
  referral_bonus_points varchar [note: 'Bonus points awarded to members in this tier for each successful referral of a new loyalty program member. Used to incentivize member acquisition through word-of-mouth.']
  rollover_points_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused points can roll over to the next qualification period for members in this tier. True if rollover is allowed, false if points expire at period end.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer defining the hierarchical display order of tiers (e.g., 1 for Bronze, 2 for Silver, 3 for Gold, 4 for Platinum). Lower values represent entry-level tiers; higher values represent premium tiers.']
  target_member_segment varchar [note: 'Description of the target guest segment this tier is designed to attract and retain (e.g., frequent visitors, high spenders, digital-first guests, family diners). Used for marketing strategy and program design.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM). Used as business identifier in operational systems and reporting.']
  tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tier, including value proposition, benefits summary, and marketing messaging. Used in loyalty program communications and member portal.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Bronze Member, Silver Member, Gold Member, Platinum Elite). Used in guest-facing communications and marketing materials.']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for member assignment; inactive tiers are temporarily disabled; retired tiers are no longer offered but may have legacy members; pending tiers are configured but not yet launched.']
  upgrade_notification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members should receive automated notification when they qualify for upgrade to this tier. True if notification is enabled, false otherwise.']
  validity_days varchar [note: 'Number of days a member retains this tier status after qualification before re-evaluation (e.g., 365 for annual renewal). Null indicates permanent tier assignment.']
  welcome_bonus_points varchar [note: 'One-time bonus points awarded to a member upon first achieving this tier. Used to incentivize tier progression and enhance member engagement.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of all loyalty membership tiers (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) defined in the Restaurants loyalty program. Stores tier name, tier code, qualification threshold (points or visit count), tier benefits summary, tier multiplier for points accrual, tier validity period, and sort order. Drives tier assignment logic and benefit entitlement across the program.'
}

Table loyalty.tier_history {
  tier_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each tier change event in the loyalty program. Primary key for the tier history transactional audit log.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order, purchase, or points transaction that triggered this tier change event. Null for manual adjustments or scheduled reviews.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who experienced this tier change event.']
  new_tier_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: tier_history records every tier change event and stores new_tier_code as a STRING. Replacing this with a FK (new_tier_tier_id -> loyalty.tier.tier_id) normalizes the reference to the tier catalog for ']
  override_authorized_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Manual tier override authorization requires tracking which manager authorized the override — critical for fraud prevention and compliance in loyalty operations. override_authorized_by is a denormalize']
  primary_tier_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where the triggering transaction occurred. Null for tier changes not associated with a specific location (e.g., scheduled reviews, manual adjustments).']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that granted this promotional tier upgrade. Null for non-promotional tier changes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tier history record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when the new tier status became active and the member began receiving benefits associated with the new tier level.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date when this tier status is scheduled to expire, requiring re-qualification or resulting in tier review. Null indicates indefinite or lifetime tier status.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier change was manually processed by an administrator or customer service representative rather than automatically triggered by system rules. True for manual adjustments, false for automated changes.']
  is_promotional_tier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier change was the result of a promotional campaign or marketing offer rather than organic qualification. True for promotional upgrades, false for earned tier changes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tier history record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  new_tier_benefits_activated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether benefits associated with the new tier were immediately activated and available for member use. Used for benefit provisioning tracking.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the member of this tier change: email, SMS, push notification, in-app message, physical mail, or none if no notification was sent.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a tier change notification was successfully sent to the member via email, mobile app push, or other communication channel. Used for member engagement tracking.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the tier change notification was sent to the member. Used for communication audit and member experience analytics.']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Business justification and approval notes for manual tier overrides, required for regulatory audit trails and program fairness documentation.']
  previous_tier_benefits_revoked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether benefits associated with the previous tier were immediately revoked upon tier change. Used for benefit lifecycle management and member experience tracking.']
  previous_tier_code varchar [note: 'The loyalty tier code the member held immediately before this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).']
  processing_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which this tier change was processed: POS (Point of Sale), OLO (Online Ordering), mobile app, web portal, call center, or admin portal. Used for channel analytics.']
  qualification_period_end_date date [note: 'The ending date of the measurement period used to evaluate tier qualification criteria.']
  qualification_period_start_date date [note: 'The beginning date of the measurement period used to evaluate tier qualification criteria (e.g., rolling 12 months, calendar year).']
  qualifying_points_balance varchar [note: 'The total loyalty points balance at the time of tier change that contributed to qualification or demotion decision.']
  qualifying_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cumulative spend amount that qualified the member for this tier change, measured in the program\'s base currency. Used for tier progression analytics.']
  qualifying_visit_count varchar [note: 'The number of restaurant visits or transactions during the qualification period that contributed to this tier change.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this tier change event (e.g., Olo Digital Ordering Platform, Oracle MICROS POS, Salesforce CRM). Used for data lineage and system integration tracking.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this tier change record in the source operational system, used for data reconciliation and troubleshooting.']
  tier_change_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about this tier change event, used for customer service context and audit documentation.']
  tier_change_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for this tier change event, used for customer service reference and audit tracking.']
  tier_change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the tier change: qualification (earned through spend/visits), demotion (failed to maintain requirements), manual_override (customer service adjustment), promotional_upgrade (marketing campaign), anniversary_renewal (annual tier review), points_threshold (points-based qualification), spend_threshold (spend-based qualification), visit_threshold (frequency-based qualification).']
  tier_change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed human-readable explanation of why this tier change occurred, providing context for customer service and audit purposes.']
  tier_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the tier change event was processed and recorded in the loyalty system.']
  tier_change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tier movement direction and nature: upgrade (promotion to higher tier), downgrade (demotion to lower tier), lateral (same tier level renewal), initial_enrollment (first tier assignment), reinstatement (reactivation after lapse), manual_adjustment (administrative override).']
  tier_duration_days varchar [note: 'The number of days this tier status is valid, calculated as the difference between effective date and expiry date. Used for tier lifecycle analytics.']
  triggering_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Business-readable reference number of the transaction that triggered this tier change, used for customer service inquiries and audit trails.']

  Note: 'Transactional audit of every tier change event for a loyalty member — captures previous tier, new tier, effective date, expiry date, reason code (qualification, demotion, manual override, promotional upgrade), and the triggering transaction reference. Enables tier progression analytics, member lifecycle tracking, and regulatory audit trails for program fairness.'
}

Table loyalty.points_ledger {
  points_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each points transaction record in the loyalty ledger. Serves as the immutable primary key for this financial-grade system of record.']
  accrual_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.accrual_rule. Business justification: Each points accrual entry in the ledger is generated by a specific accrual rule (e.g., 2x points on weekends, bonus points for specific menu categories). Adding accrual_rule_id to points_ledger links ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign or promotional offer that triggered this points transaction. Populated for bonus awards, multiplier events, or targeted incentives. Null for standard earn/redeem transactions not tied to a specific campaign.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Points ledger entries represent financial transactions (earn/burn) that must be allocated to a cost center for restaurant-level P&L and loyalty program cost reporting. Foodservice finance teams requir']
  enrollment_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.enrollment_event. Business justification: Enrollment events award initial_points_awarded to new members, which generates a points_ledger entry. Adding enrollment_event_id to points_ledger links the welcome/enrollment bonus ledger entry to the']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Points ledger entries drive deferred revenue balances that must be reported by financial period for period-end close. Foodservice finance teams require \'loyalty points deferred revenue period-end clos']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for accounting of each points accrual/redemption to the appropriate GL account per financial reporting standards.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the loyalty program member whose points balance is affected by this transaction. Links to the member master record.']
  offer_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.offer_redemption. Business justification: Offer redemptions can trigger points accrual events (e.g., bonus points for redeeming an offer). Adding offer_redemption_id to points_ledger links the ledger entry to the specific offer redemption tha']
  points_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where this points transaction originated. Populated for POS and kiosk transactions. Null for OLO, mobile app, or admin transactions not tied to a specific physical location.']
  primary_points_adjusted_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or customer service agent who authorized and executed the manual adjustment or reversal. Populated for adjust and reversal transactions. Critical for SOX compliance and fraud prevention.']
  primary_points_franchise_franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchise entity that owns or operates the restaurant where this transaction occurred. Used for royalty calculations and franchise performance analytics. Null for company-owned locations or non-location transactions.']
  primary_points_guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating order transaction that triggered this points movement. Populated for earn and redeem transactions tied to guest purchases. Null for non-order events such as bonus awards, expirations, or manual adjustments.']
  redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: When a member redeems a reward (burn event), a points_ledger entry is created recording the points deduction. Adding redemption_id to points_ledger links the ledger debit entry to the specific redempt']
  reversal_of_transaction_points_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original points_ledger_id that this reversal transaction is canceling. Populated only for reversal transaction types. Creates an audit chain linking reversals to their source transactions.']
  reward_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific reward item redeemed by the member. Populated only for redeem transaction types. Links to the rewards catalog to identify what was claimed (free item, discount, experience).']
  source_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Points accrual channel normalization: points_ledger.source_channel is a plain-text denormalized attribute. FK to order.channel normalizes channel taxonomy for channel-level points accrual reporting an']
  source_transaction_guest_order_id varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier from the originating source system. Enables end-to-end traceability and reconciliation between the loyalty ledger and upstream systems. Format varies by source system.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty membership tier the member held at the time of this transaction. Used to determine earn rates, bonus multipliers, and redemption privileges. Links to tier master data.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why a manual adjustment or reversal was made. Populated only for adjust and reversal transaction types. Supports audit compliance and customer service quality tracking.']
  adjustment_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the customer service representative or system administrator who initiated the adjustment. Populated for adjust and reversal transactions. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch or job that created this ledger entry. Used for bulk operations such as nightly expiration runs, mass bonus awards, or system migrations. Null for real-time individual transactions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month or accounting period number) within the fiscal year when this transaction occurred. Supports monthly financial close, P&L reporting, and loyalty liability accrual tracking.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this points transaction occurred, derived from transaction_timestamp. Supports financial reporting, GAAP revenue recognition for breakage, and year-over-year loyalty program analytics.']
  is_voided boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this transaction has been voided or reversed by a subsequent transaction. True if a reversal exists, false otherwise. Immutable ledger entries are never deleted, only marked as voided.']
  order_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order total amount. Supports multi-currency loyalty programs for international operations. Null for non-order transactions.']
  order_total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the order that triggered this points transaction, in the local currency. Used to calculate points earn rate and for financial reconciliation. Null for non-order transactions.']
  points_balance_after varchar [note: 'The cumulative running balance of points for this member immediately after this transaction was applied. Provides audit trail and reconciliation capability. Must equal prior balance plus points_delta.']
  points_delta varchar [note: 'The signed integer change in points balance for this transaction. Positive values represent accruals (earn, bonus), negative values represent deductions (redeem, expire, adjust downward). Zero-delta entries are not permitted.']
  points_earn_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The rate at which points were earned per currency unit spent. Example: 1.0000 means 1 point per dollar, 2.0000 means 2 points per dollar (double points promotion). Populated for earn transactions, null otherwise.']
  points_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the points earned in this transaction will expire if not redeemed. Populated only for earn and bonus transaction types. Null for redeem, expire, and adjust transactions. Supports FIFO expiration logic and member communication.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger entry was committed to the loyalty system of record. May differ from transaction_timestamp for batch processing or delayed reconciliation. Used for ETL audit and SLA monitoring.']
  restaurant_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable store or unit number for the restaurant location. Denormalized for operational reporting and guest service. Null for non-location-specific transactions.']
  source_order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable order number or receipt identifier from the POS or OLO system. Provides guest-facing reference for customer service inquiries. Null for non-order transactions.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that originated this points transaction. Supports multi-system integration and data lineage tracking. Examples: micros_pos (Oracle MICROS POS), olo_platform (Olo Digital Ordering), salesforce_crm (Salesforce Marketing Cloud), loyalty_engine (core loyalty platform), admin_portal (back-office admin tool).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this points transaction occurred in the source system. Represents the business event time, not the ETL load time. Critical for sequencing and audit.']
